doc.overview: The HTML Filter implements the `IFilter` interface for **HTML and XHTML** documents. It processes character and numeric entities (converting them to Unicode), handles encoding detection from document declarations, and preserves original line-break types. The filter is primarily configured through YAML configuration files that define element rules, translatable attributes, and structural groupings.

cleanupHtml.description: Automatically fix common HTML syntax errors such as unquoted attributes.
cleanupHtml.help: Controls post-processing cleanup of common HTML syntax errors (e.g., unquoted attributes) in the input file. Set to `false` to disable this automatic cleanup.

codeFinderRules.description: Regular expression rules in Okapi #v1 format for identifying inline codes.
codeFinderRules.help:
Defines regular expression rules for identifying inline codes within extracted text. Uses the Okapi `#v1` code finder format.

Backslashes must be **double-escaped** in single-line YAML notation. Use the YAML block scalar (`|-`) syntax to avoid double-escaping.
codeFinderRules.values: Okapi `#v1` code finder format: header line `#v1`, then `count.i=N` (number of rules), then `rule0=...` through `ruleN-1=...` with Java regex patterns. In single-line YAML, use `\\` for regex `\`. In block scalar (`|-`) syntax, use single `\`.
notes: The regular expression `\bVAR\d\b` must be written as `\\bVAR\\d\\b` in single-line YAML string notation.
examples: Single-line: `codeFinderRules: "#v1\ncount.i=1\nrule0=\\bVAR\\d\\b"`

Block scalar:
```yaml
codeFinderRules: |-
    #v1
    count.i=1
    rule0=\bVAR\d\b
```

escapeCharacters.description: Characters to output as HTML named character entity references.
escapeCharacters.help:
A string containing all characters that should be output as **HTML character entity references** (e.g., `©` instead of ©).

By default, extended characters are output as-is (no entity references). Any character in this string that has no defined HTML entity or is not an extended character is output normally.
escapeCharacters.values: A string of Unicode characters. Each character in the string will be converted to its HTML named entity reference in output, if one exists.
examples: `escapeCharacters: "© €µÆ"` — outputs `©`, ` `, `€`, `µ`, `Æ` in place of the literal characters

inlineCdata.description: Treat CDATA sections as inline elements instead of breaking text flow.
inlineCdata.help:
Treats `<![CDATA[...]]>` sections as inline elements rather than breaking the text flow. The CDATA opening marker is treated like an inline opening tag and the closing marker like an inline closing tag.

Useful for formats that embed CDATA in ways that would otherwise split translatable text into separate segments.
examples:
With `inlineCdata: true`, `

Text with CDATA

` is extracted as a single text unit with inline codes.

quoteMode.description: Controls how quote and apostrophe characters are escaped in output.
quoteMode.help:
Controls how quote and apostrophe characters are escaped in the output.

- **0** (`UNESCAPED`) — Do not escape single or double quotes
- **1** (`ALL`) — Escape both single and double quotes to named entities
- **2** (`NUMERIC_SINGLE_QUOTES`) — Escape double quotes to named entity, single quotes to numeric entity
- **3** (`DOUBLE_QUOTES_ONLY`) — Escape double quotes only

dependsOn.condition: must be true for quoteMode to take effect

quoteModeDefined.description: Enable the custom quoteMode setting for quote/apostrophe escaping.
quoteModeDefined.help: Must be set to `true` to activate the custom `quoteMode` setting. Without this flag, the default quoting behavior is used.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when quoteModeDefined is true

useCodeFinder.description: Enable regular expression-based inline code detection in extracted text.
useCodeFinder.help:
Activates the inline code finder, which uses regular expressions defined in `codeFinderRules` to identify spans of extracted text that should be protected as inline codes.

Useful when element content contains variables or placeholders (e.g., `VAR1`) that must not be modified during translation.

limitations: Content of `<style>` and `<script>` elements is not extracted.
Tags from server-side scripts (PHP, ASPX, JSP, etc.) are not formally supported and are treated as non-translatable.
processingNotes: If the document has an encoding declaration, it is used; otherwise the default encoding from filter options is used.
For UTF-8 output: a BOM is included only if the input was also UTF-8 and had a BOM.
If the input file has no declared encoding, the filter tries to add a `<meta>` tag (HTML) or `<meta />` tag (XHTML) in output, but only if a `<head>` element exists.
Output line-breaks match the original input line-break type.
Character and numeric entities are converted to Unicode; DTD/schema-defined entities pass through unchanged.
By default, the filter uses a minimalist configuration with no structural groupings. The `okf_html-wellFormed` configuration enables structural groupings but requires well-formed start/end tags.
All element and attribute names in configuration files must be **lowercase**, regardless of document casing.
Elements or attributes with a prefix (e.g., `xml:lang`) must be declared with the prefix and enclosed in single quotes in the configuration.

examples.title: Inline code finder for variables
examples.description: Protects `VAR1`, `VAR2`, etc. from translation by marking them as inline codes.
examples.input:
```html

Number of files = VAR1

```
examples.output:
```
The text "VAR1" is marked as an inline code in the extracted text unit.
```

examples.title: Character entity references in output
examples.description: Forces specific extended characters to be output as HTML named entity references. Characters without a defined HTML entity (like `Ą`) are output as-is.
examples.input:
```html

© €µÆĄ

```
examples.output:
```html

© €µÆĄ

```

examples.title: Exclude-by-default configuration
examples.description: Inverts the default inclusion behavior so only explicitly configured elements are extracted. Useful for extracting only specific elements like `<title>`.
examples.input:
```html My Page

Not extracted

```
examples.output:
```
Only "My Page" from the <title> element is extracted for translation.
```

examples.title: Conditional translatable attributes
examples.description: Configures `<input>` element attributes to be translatable only when the `type` attribute is not one of `file`, `hidden`, `image`, or `password`.
examples.config:
```yaml
elements:
  input:
    ruleTypes: [INLINE]
    translatableAttributes:
      alt: [type, NOT_EQUALS, [file, hidden, image, password]]
      value: [type, NOT_EQUALS, [file, hidden, image, password]]
      accesskey: [type, NOT_EQUALS, [file, hidden, image, password]]
      title: [type, NOT_EQUALS, [file, hidden, image, password]]
```
examples.input:
```html
<input type="text" value="Submit" alt="Send form">
<input type="hidden" value="secret_token">
```
examples.output:
```
Only the first <input>'s value and alt attributes are extracted; the hidden input is skipped.
```

examples.title: Inline CDATA handling
examples.description: Treats CDATA sections as inline elements so they don't break text flow during extraction.
examples.input:
```html

Text with CDATA

```
examples.output:
```
Extracted as a single text unit: "Text with {inline} CDATA" with CDATA markers as inline codes.
```

simplifierRules.description: Rules for simplifying inline code representation
simplifierRules.title: Simplifier Rules

path.title: ITS Parameters File
path.description: Path to an ITS 2.0 rules document that overrides the default HTML5 processing behavior

doc.overview: Processes **HTML5** documents, including those containing **ITS 2.0** (Internationalization Tag Set) markup. Input documents are expected to be valid HTML5. The filter supports global and local ITS rules and most ITS 2.0 data categories, enabling fine-grained control over translatable content, terminology, language information, and directionality.

mergeAdjacentCodes.help: When multiple inline codes appear next to each other with no text between them (e.g., ``), they are combined into a single placeholder code. This reduces visual clutter in segments presented to translators.

moveLeadingAndTrailingCodesToSkeleton.help: Inline codes (e.g., `<b>`, `<span>`) that appear at the very start or end of a segment are moved out of the translatable content and into the skeleton. This simplifies segments for translators when boundary tags don't affect the translatable text.

path.description: Path to an ITS 2.0 rules document that overrides the default HTML5 processing behavior
path.help:
Path to an **ITS rules file** that overrides the default HTML5 processing behavior.

The default behavior includes:
- `lang` attribute → Language Information
- `id` attribute → Id Value
- Phrasing content elements treated as inline (`withinText="yes"`)
- `translate` attribute → Translate data category (using HTML5-specific behavior, not XML)

Provide a custom ITS document to override any of these defaults. See the [ITS specification](http://www.w3.org/TR/its20/) for the rule format.
path.values: Path to an ITS 2.0 XML rules document (`.its` or `.xml`)

simplifierRules.help: Rules that control how inline codes are simplified for translation. These rules map HTML inline elements to shorter placeholder representations, making segments easier to translate.

limitations: This filter is **BETA**.
processingNotes: **Input encoding** is auto-detected using the HTML5 specification's encoding detection mechanism.
**Output encoding** matches the input encoding unless overridden by the calling tool.
**Line-breaks** in the output match the original input line-break style.

**Quote escaping** can be configured via `quoteModeDefined` and `quoteMode` parameters in the config file. Modes: `0` = unescaped, `1` = all named entities, `2` = named for double / numeric for single, `3` = double quotes only.

The filter uses HTML5-specific `translate` attribute behavior, which differs from the XML ITS behavior. See the [HTML5 spec](http://www.w3.org/TR/2013/CR-html5-20130806/dom.html#the-translate-attribute) for details.
ITS rules in the input document itself will override the default behavior, as will rules from a custom parameters file.

examples.title: Default ITS rules for HTML5
examples.description: The default configuration includes ITS rules that define standard HTML5 translatable attributes, non-translatable elements (`<script>`, `<style>`, `<del>`), bidirectional markup, and preserve-space elements (`<pre>`, `<textarea>`). Custom ITS rules files can override any of these defaults.
examples.config:
```xml
<its:rules xmlns:its="http://www.w3.org/2005/11/its" version="2.0"
 xmlns:h="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">
 <!-- Non-translatable elements -->
 <its:translateRule selector="//h:script|//h:style" translate="no"/>
 <its:translateRule selector="//h:del" translate="no"/>

 <!-- Translatable attributes -->
 <its:translateRule selector="//h:*/@alt|//h:*/@title" translate="yes"/>
 <its:translateRule selector="//h:meta[@name='keywords']/@content" translate="yes"/>
 <its:translateRule selector="//h:meta[@name='description']/@content" translate="yes"/>

 <!-- Preserve space -->
 <its:preserveSpaceRule selector="//h:pre|//h:textarea" space="preserve"/>
</its:rules>
```

examples.title: Quote mode configuration
examples.description: Configure how quotes and apostrophes are escaped in the output by setting `quoteModeDefined` and `quoteMode` in the config file.

entries.description: Extract and update translatable values in i18next / react-i18next JSON resource bundles.

schema.title: i18next JSON
schema.description: Configuration for the i18next / react-i18next JSON localization format

legacyPluralForms.title: Recognise legacy plural forms
legacyPluralForms.description: Recognise the legacy v1–v3 plural sibling keys (key_plural and the numeric key_0 / key_1 / key_2 … forms) in addition to the v4 CLDR suffixes (_zero / _one / _two / _few / _many / _other).

protectInterpolation.title: Protect interpolation and nesting
protectInterpolation.description: Detect i18next interpolation ({{var}}, {{var, format}}) and nesting ($t(key)) and protect them as inline codes so they are never translated.

subfilterHtmlValues.title: Subfilter HTML values (_html keys)
subfilterHtmlValues.description: Hand values whose key ends in "_html" (the i18next convention for markup) to the HTML subfilter so tags are protected and text remains translatable. Off by default: the HTML subfilter is not byte-faithful for bare markup fragments.

doc.overview:
The **i18next JSON** format reads i18next / react-i18next resource bundles —
the conventions of the dominant web i18n library. It is built on the JSON
format and selected explicitly (`-f i18next`), because i18next files use the
generic `.json` extension owned by the JSON format and cannot be reliably
auto-distinguished.

Beyond plain JSON extraction, it protects `{{interpolation}}`,
`{{var, format}}`, and `$t()` nesting as inline codes; annotates v4 CLDR
plural sibling keys (`key_zero`/`key_one`/…/`key_other`, plus legacy
`key_plural` and `key_0`/`key_1`) with their base key and plural category;
annotates context keys (`key_male`/`key_female`); and names blocks by their
full namespace key path. Round-trip is byte-faithful.

subfilterHTMLValues.description: Route values whose key ends in _html through the HTML subfilter.
subfilterHTMLValues.help:
When enabled, values under keys ending in `_html` are parsed by the HTML
subfilter for tag protection. Disabled by default because the HTML
subfilter normalises bare markup fragments, which would break byte-faithful
round-trip; enable it only for tag-protection workflows.

processingNotes: Plural sibling keys stay 1:1 blocks (preserving byte-faithful round-trip) but are annotated with base key + CLDR category.
`{{interpolation}}` and `$t()` nesting are protected as inline codes.
Block names use the full namespace key path for nested objects.

examples.title: Protect HTML in _html values
examples.description: Enable the HTML subfilter for keys ending in _html.

entries.description: Extract translatable text from Adobe InCopy ICML and WCML documents.

doc.overview:
ICML (InCopy Markup Language) is the XML-based format Adobe InDesign and
InCopy use for editorial workflows. Unlike its IDML sibling, an `.icml` (or
`.wcml`) file is a single XML document rather than a ZIP package. The reader
descends Story → ParagraphStyleRange → CharacterStyleRange → Content runs,
emitting one translatable Block per visible text payload.

neokapi reads and writes ICML/WCML, preserving the surrounding style ranges
and document structure as skeleton. By default it does not extract note
content, and it groups paragraph text rather than starting a new translation
unit at each line break — both matching the upstream Okapi ICML filter
defaults. The native reader applies the same defaults but does not expose
them as a configurable JSON schema in this reference.
limitations: Note content is not extracted by default.
Extraction and segmentation toggles match the upstream Okapi ICML filter but are not surfaced as configurable schema properties in this reference.
processingNotes: One translatable Block per visible Content run, walking Story → ParagraphStyleRange → CharacterStyleRange.
Style ranges and the document structure are preserved as skeleton for round-trips.
Handles both `.icml` and `.wcml` extensions.

inlineCodes.description: Pattern-based detection of inline codes (placeholders, variables, tags) within translatable text.

enabled.description: Enable pattern-based detection of inline codes (placeholders, tags, etc.)

rules.description: Regex patterns to identify inline codes in translatable text

rules.description: List of regex patterns to detect inline codes

mergeAdjacent.description: Merge consecutive inline codes into a single placeholder

Create new paragraphs on hard returns (
elements)

skipThreshold.title: Maximum spread size
skipThreshold.description: Skip any spread larger than the given value (in Kbytes)

doc.overview: This filter processes ICML (InCopy Markup Language) and WCML documents, which are XML-based formats used by Adobe InDesign and InCopy. It handles both `.icml` and `.wcml` file extensions. The filter is very similar to the [IDML Filter](IDML_Filter) and supports extraction of notes, master spreads, and configurable inline code simplification.

extraction.extractMasterSpreads.help:
Extracts the content of master spreads if they exist in the document. When disabled, only normal spreads are extracted.

Master spreads contain repeating page elements (headers, footers, page numbers) that appear across multiple pages in the InDesign layout.

extraction.extractNotes.help:
Extracts the content of `<Note>` elements in the ICML/WCML document as translator notes.

Note content is typically editorial annotations added by authors or editors in InCopy/InDesign and may contain context useful for translators.

extraction.newTuOnBr.help:
Controls whether `
` (hard return / break) elements create a new text unit (segment boundary). By default, content separated by `
` tags remains in the same text unit. Enabling this splits the text at each break into separate segments for translation.

extraction.simplifyCodes.help:
Reduces the number of inline codes by re-grouping adjacent codes when possible.

InDesign documents often contain many formatting runs that produce excessive inline codes. Simplifying merges adjacent codes into fewer placeholders, making segments easier for translators to work with.

extraction.skipThreshold.help:
Maximum size for spread files in **KBytes**. Any spread file above this value will either generate an error or be skipped from extraction.

This allows you to skip large spread files that may contain only graphics and require too much memory to open.
extraction.skipThreshold.values: Integer value in KBytes, range 1–32000. Default is 1000 KB.
notes: Skipped files are **not** checked for translatable text — content in oversized spreads will be silently omitted from extraction.

inlineCodes.enabled.help:
Enables pattern-based detection of inline codes (placeholders, tags, etc.) using the regex rules defined below.

When enabled, text matching the defined patterns will be treated as non-translatable inline codes rather than regular translatable text.

inlineCodes.mergeAdjacent.help: Merges consecutive inline codes into a single placeholder. This reduces clutter when multiple adjacent codes appear in the segment, making it easier for translators to work with the text.

inlineCodes.moveBoundaryCodes.help: Moves inline codes that appear at the start or end of a segment into the non-translatable skeleton. This keeps segments cleaner by removing leading/trailing codes that don't need translator attention.

inlineCodes.rules.help:
Defines regex patterns to identify inline codes within translatable text. Each pattern marks matching content as a non-translatable placeholder.

Useful for protecting variables, formatting codes, or placeholder tokens embedded in translatable strings.

inlineCodes.simplifierRules.help: Rules for simplifying inline code representation. Simplifier rules reduce complex nested inline codes into simpler placeholder forms.

limitations: Processing details for WCML/ICML are not fully documented (marked as TODO in the Okapi wiki).
processingNotes: The filter handles both `.icml` and `.wcml` file extensions with the same processing logic.
The filter is very similar to the IDML Filter but operates on single-file ICML/WCML documents rather than IDML packages.
ICML (InCopy Markup Language) is an XML-based format used by Adobe InDesign and InCopy.

examples.title: Skip large graphic-heavy spreads
examples.description: Configure the filter to skip spread files larger than 500 KB, which are likely to contain mostly graphics and little translatable text.

examples.title: Extract notes with simplified codes
examples.description: Enable note extraction for translator context while simplifying inline codes for cleaner segments.
examples.config:
```yaml
extraction:
  extractNotes: true
  simplifyCodes: true
  extractMasterSpreads: true
```

entries.description: Read and write ICU MessageFormat patterns, extracting each branch of plural and select pickers separately.

doc.overview:
ICU MessageFormat is the pattern syntax used by ICU and many i18n
libraries to express messages with placeholders and grammatical
variation. This format treats each input line as one independent
MessageFormat pattern.

A plain-text pattern extracts as a single translatable block. A
pattern with simple placeholders — `{0}`, `{name}`, `{name, type}`,
`{name, type, style}` — also extracts as a single block, with the
placeholders carried as inline runs around the literal text so they
cannot be accidentally translated or reordered. A pattern with a
plural, select, or selectordinal picker splits into one block per
branch body, so each grammatical form can be translated independently;
the picker scaffolding (`{count, plural, …}`, `{gender, select, …}`)
and any text outside the picker is preserved through the skeleton
rather than extracted. ICU quoting is decoded before extraction: `''`
becomes a literal apostrophe and `'{…}'` protects a literal-brace
passage. Java's deprecated `choice` format is rejected with an error.
limitations: Each line is parsed as a self-contained pattern; a single pattern spanning multiple lines is not supported.
The deprecated ICU `choice` format is not supported and is reported as an error.
There are no configurable parameters; upstream output options such as plural-form expansion or pretty-printing are not exposed.
processingNotes: Plain and simple-placeholder patterns extract as one block per line; plural / select / selectordinal patterns extract one block per branch body.
Placeholders are represented as inline runs (`icu:argument`, `icu:number` for `#`) so they survive translation intact.
Blank lines and trailing newlines round-trip as skeleton; with a skeleton store active, output is byte-exact.

examples.title: Translate ICU patterns
examples.description:
No configuration is required. Declare the format to extract each
line as a pattern, splitting plural and select branches into
separate blocks.

ignoreCharacterTracking.description: Prevent tracking differences from creating separate inline codes.

doc.overview: The IDML Filter processes Adobe InDesign IDML documents (InDesign Markup Language), an XML-based format introduced in InDesign CS4. The filter extracts text by spread, gathering stories from `<TextFrame>` and `<TextPath>` elements in spread order. IDML documents are ZIP packages (UCF) containing multiple sub-documents, each treated as a separate sub-document by the filter. The [IDML specification](http://www.adobe.com/content/dam/Adobe/en/devnet/indesign/cs5_docs/idml/idml-specification.pdf) is available on the Adobe Web site.

excludedStyles.description: Styles whose content is excluded from extraction.
excludedStyles.help: Specifies paragraph or character style names whose content should be **excluded** from extraction. Use this to skip non-translatable styled content such as code listings, product IDs, or other fixed-text styles.
excludedStyles.values: List of InDesign style names to exclude from extraction.

extractBreaksInline.description: Treat break elements as inline codes within text units.
extractBreaksInline.help: Treats break elements as inline codes within text units rather than creating separate segments at break boundaries.

extractCustomTextVariables.description: Include custom text variables in the extraction.
extractCustomTextVariables.help: Includes custom text variables defined in the InDesign document in the extraction for translation.

extractExternalHyperlinks.description: Extract external hyperlink URLs for translation.
extractExternalHyperlinks.help: Includes external hyperlink URLs/text in the extraction for translation. Enable this when hyperlink destinations need to be localized (e.g., region-specific URLs).

extractHiddenLayers.description: Include hidden layers in the extraction.
extractHiddenLayers.help: Includes content from hidden layers in the extraction. By default, only visible layers are extracted.

extractHiddenPasteboardItems.description: Include hidden pasteboard items in the extraction.
extractHiddenPasteboardItems.help: Includes content from items on the pasteboard (outside the visible page area) that are hidden. These are typically elements placed outside the page bounds during layout editing.

extractHyperlinkTextSourcesInline.description: Extract hyperlink text sources as inline elements.
extractHyperlinkTextSourcesInline.help:
Controls how hyperlink text sources are represented in the extraction.

- **Enabled**: Hyperlink text sources are extracted as inline elements within the surrounding text.
- **Disabled** (default): Hyperlink text sources are represented as referencing groups of separate textual units.

extractIndexTopics.description: Include index topic entries in the extraction.
extractIndexTopics.help: Includes index topic entries in the extraction for translation.

extractMasterSpreads.description: Extract content from master spreads if they exist.
extractMasterSpreads.help: Includes content from master spreads in the extraction. By default, only normal spreads are processed. Enable this if master spreads contain translatable text (e.g., headers, footers, or recurring labels).

extractMathZones.description: Include math zone content in the extraction.
extractMathZones.help: Includes math zone content in the extraction for translation. Enabled by default. Disable this if math expressions should be left untranslated.

extractNotes.description: Extract the content of Note elements.
extractNotes.help: Includes content from `<Note>` elements in the extraction. By default, notes are skipped during extraction.

formatting.baselineShiftMax.help: Upper bound (inclusive) of the baseline-shift window within which baseline-shift differences are ignored. Only has effect when **Ignore Character Baseline Shift** is enabled. Combined with the min threshold it limits the ignore behaviour to values within the range `[min, max]`; differences outside the range continue to create distinct inline codes.
formatting.baselineShiftMax.values: Decimal number as a string (parsed as a Java `double`). Default is empty (no upper bound).

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this threshold to take effect

formatting.baselineShiftMin.help: Lower bound (inclusive) of the baseline-shift window within which baseline-shift differences are ignored. Only has effect when **Ignore Character Baseline Shift** is enabled. Adjacent runs merge only when both baseline-shift values are at least this threshold; otherwise they stay as separate inline codes.
formatting.baselineShiftMin.values: Decimal number as a string (parsed as a Java `double`). Default is empty (no lower bound).

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this threshold to take effect

formatting.kerningMax.help: Upper bound (inclusive) of the kerning-value window within which kerning differences are ignored. Only has effect when **Ignore Character Kerning** is enabled. When set, adjacent runs are merged only if both their kerning values are at most this threshold. Combined with the min threshold, it defines a closed range `[min, max]`: only differences where both values fall inside the range are folded into the same run.
formatting.kerningMax.values: Decimal number as a string (parsed as a Java `double`). Default is empty (no upper bound).

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this threshold to take effect

formatting.kerningMin.help: Lower bound (inclusive) of the kerning-value window within which kerning differences are ignored. Only has effect when **Ignore Character Kerning** is enabled — otherwise the setter discards the value. When set, adjacent runs are merged only if both their kerning values are at least this threshold; runs whose kerning falls below it still produce separate inline codes. Leaving both min and max empty (the default) ignores every kerning difference unconditionally.
formatting.kerningMin.values: Decimal number as a string (parsed as a Java `double`). Default is empty (no lower bound).

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this threshold to take effect

formatting.leadingMax.help: Upper bound (inclusive) of the leading (line-spacing) window within which leading differences are ignored. Only has effect when **Ignore Character Leading** is enabled. Together with the min threshold it constrains the ignore behaviour to values inside the range `[min, max]`.
formatting.leadingMax.values: Decimal number as a string (parsed as a Java `double`). Default is empty (no upper bound).

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this threshold to take effect

formatting.leadingMin.help: Lower bound (inclusive) of the leading (line-spacing) window within which leading differences are ignored. Only has effect when **Ignore Character Leading** is enabled. Adjacent runs merge only when both leading values are at least this threshold.
formatting.leadingMin.values: Decimal number as a string (parsed as a Java `double`). Default is empty (no lower bound).

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this threshold to take effect

formatting.trackingMax.help: Upper bound (inclusive) of the tracking (letter-spacing) window within which tracking differences are ignored. Only has effect when **Ignore Character Tracking** is enabled. With both min and max set, tracking differences are folded into a single run only when both values lie within `[min, max]`.
formatting.trackingMax.values: Decimal number as a string (parsed as a Java `double`). Default is empty (no upper bound).

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this threshold to take effect

formatting.trackingMin.help: Lower bound (inclusive) of the tracking (letter-spacing) window within which tracking differences are ignored. Only has effect when **Ignore Character Tracking** is enabled. Adjacent runs merge only when both tracking values are at least this threshold; otherwise they remain separate inline codes. Empty min and max (the default) ignore all tracking differences.
formatting.trackingMin.values: Decimal number as a string (parsed as a Java `double`). Default is empty (no lower bound).

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this threshold to take effect

ignoreCharacterBaselineShift.description: Prevent baseline shift differences from creating separate inline codes.
ignoreCharacterBaselineShift.help: Prevents baseline shift differences from creating separate inline codes. Useful when characters have varying baseline offsets (e.g., for visual alignment) that should not affect segmentation.

ignoreCharacterKerning.description: Prevent kerning differences from creating separate inline codes.
ignoreCharacterKerning.help: Prevents character kerning differences from creating separate inline codes. When characters differ only in kerning values, they are treated as the same run of text, reducing unnecessary inline code segmentation.

ignoreCharacterLeading.description: Prevent leading differences from creating separate inline codes.
ignoreCharacterLeading.help: Prevents character leading (line-spacing) differences from creating separate inline codes. Reduces inline code clutter when leading varies within a paragraph.

ignoreCharacterTracking.description: Prevent tracking differences from creating separate inline codes.
ignoreCharacterTracking.help: Prevents character tracking (letter-spacing) differences from creating separate inline codes. Reduces inline code clutter when tracking varies across a text run.

maxAttributeSize.description: Size in MB for the attribute buffer (default 4 MB).
maxAttributeSize.help: Controls the attribute buffer size in MB. Increase this if processing IDML documents with very large XML attributes that exceed the default 4 MB limit.
maxAttributeSize.values: Integer value in MB. Default is `4` (4 × 1024 × 1024 bytes).

mergeAdjacentCodes.description: Merge consecutive inline codes into a single code.
mergeAdjacentCodes.help: Combines consecutive inline codes into a single code. This reduces the number of inline tags translators need to manage, making segments cleaner and easier to translate.

skipDiscretionaryHyphens.description: Omit discretionary (soft) hyphens from extracted text.
skipDiscretionaryHyphens.help: Omits discretionary (soft) hyphens from the extracted text. Discretionary hyphens are hints for line-breaking that may not be appropriate in translated text with different word lengths.

specialCharacterPattern.description: Pattern matching characters treated as inline codes.
specialCharacterPattern.help: Regex pattern matching characters that should be treated as **inline codes** rather than translatable text. The default pattern matches various Unicode special characters including zero-width spaces, zero-width non-joiners, soft hyphens, non-breaking hyphens, and BOM characters.

untagXmlStructures.description: Skip embedded XML structural information when extracting.
untagXmlStructures.help: Strips embedded XML structural information from the extracted content. Use this when the IDML document contains XML tagging that is not relevant for translation and would clutter the translatable segments.

limitations: IDML filter is marked as **ALPHA** in the Okapi help documentation.
processingNotes: The input encoding is **automatically detected**; any user-specified encoding is ignored. The filter always uses UTF-8.
Output line-breaks are always set to simple linefeed (LF).

An IDML document is a ZIP package (UCF — Universal Container Format) containing multiple embedded documents. Each embedded file is treated as a **sub-document** — e.g., in XLIFF output, a single IDML produces multiple `<file>` elements.
Text is extracted **by spread**: for each spread, stories from `<TextFrame>` and `<TextPath>` elements are gathered and extracted in the order they appear.
Stories embedded inside other stories and not declared at a spread level are extracted in a special group.
The filter is actually a wrapper around `IDMLContentFilter` — the inner filter handles raw IDML XML (Story XML) files directly, while the outer `IDMLFilter` handles the full IDML package.

examples.title: Extract with hidden layers and master spreads
examples.description: Configuration to extract all content including hidden layers and master spreads, useful for comprehensive translation of an InDesign document.
examples.config:
```yaml
extractMasterSpreads: true
extractHiddenLayers: true
extractNotes: true
```

examples.title: Clean extraction with reduced inline codes
examples.description: Configuration that minimizes inline code clutter by merging adjacent codes and ignoring formatting-only differences.
examples.config:
```yaml
mergeAdjacentCodes: true
ignoreCharacterKerning: true
ignoreCharacterTracking: true
ignoreCharacterLeading: true
ignoreCharacterBaselineShift: true
```

entries.description: Extract and reinsert translatable values from Java .properties files, with key filtering and escape handling.

schema.title: Java Properties Format
schema.description: Configuration for the Java .properties format reader/writer

codeFinderRules.title: Code Finder Rules
codeFinderRules.description: Regex patterns that match inline codes within translatable text

commentsAreNotes.title: Comments as Notes
commentsAreNotes.description: Treat comments as translator notes attached to the following entry

escapeExtendedChars.title: Escape Extended Characters
escapeExtendedChars.description: Escape extended Unicode characters (non-ASCII) in output using \uXXXX notation

extraComments.title: Additional Comment Markers
extraComments.description: Recognize semicolon and double-slash comment styles in addition to standard # and ! markers

extractOnlyMatchingKey.title: Extract Only Matching Keys
extractOnlyMatchingKey.description: When enabled, extract only entries whose keys match the condition; otherwise exclude matching keys

keyCondition.title: Key Condition Pattern
keyCondition.description: Regular expression pattern to test against property keys for extraction filtering

useCodeFinder.title: Enable Inline Code Detection
useCodeFinder.description: Enable regex-based inline code detection within property values

useJavaEscapes.title: Retain Java Escapes
useJavaEscapes.description: Keep Java property escape sequences (\: \= \# \!) in extracted text instead of resolving them

useKeyCondition.title: Enable Key Condition
useKeyCondition.description: Filter entries for extraction based on a regex condition applied to their keys

doc.overview:
The **Java Properties** format reads `.properties` resource bundles,
extracts the value of each entry as a translatable block, and writes the
translations back while preserving keys, comments, and layout. It is the
standard format for Java application and library localization.

Each line is a `key=value` (or `key:value`) entry; comment lines begin with
`#` or `!`. By default every value is extracted. You can restrict extraction
to entries whose keys match a pattern, treat comments as translator notes,
retain or resolve Java escape sequences, and control non-ASCII escaping in
the output.

codeFinderRules.description: Regex patterns that match inline codes within values.
codeFinderRules.help:
An array of regular expressions; text matching any of them is treated
as an inline code (for example a `{0}`-style message-format argument)
rather than translatable words. Only used when `useCodeFinder` is
enabled.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for these patterns to apply

commentsAreNotes.help:
When enabled (the default), a comment is attached as a translator note
to the entry that follows it. Disable it to keep comments purely as
non-translatable skeleton.

escapeExtendedChars.help:
When enabled (the default), non-ASCII characters are written using
`\uXXXX` notation, matching the historically ASCII-only `.properties`
encoding. Disable it to emit characters literally (for UTF-8 bundles).

extraComments.help:
When enabled, semicolon (`;`) and double-slash (`//`) comment styles are
recognized in addition to the standard `#` and `!` markers.

extractOnlyMatchingKey.help:
When enabled (the default), only entries whose keys match `keyCondition`
are extracted. When disabled, matching keys are excluded and all others
are extracted.

dependsOn.condition: only consulted when key filtering is enabled

keyCondition.help:
A regular expression matched against entry keys. Combined with
`extractOnlyMatchingKey` to decide which entries are extracted. Only
consulted when `useKeyCondition` is enabled.
examples: `.*text.*` — match keys containing the substring `text`

dependsOn.condition: only consulted when key filtering is enabled

separator.help:
The character separating a key from its value, typically `=` or `:`.
Whitespace is permitted around the separator per the properties format.

useCodeFinder.description: Enable regex-based inline-code detection.
useCodeFinder.help:
Enables regex-based detection of inline codes within property values.
The patterns are supplied via `codeFinderRules`.

useJavaEscapes.description: Retain Java escape sequences in extracted text.
useJavaEscapes.help:
When enabled, Java property escape sequences (`\:`, `\=`, `\#`, `\!`)
are kept verbatim in the extracted text instead of being resolved to
their literal characters. By default they are resolved.

useKeyCondition.help:
When enabled, entries are filtered for extraction based on the
`keyCondition` regular expression and the `extractOnlyMatchingKey`
direction. When disabled (the default), every entry is extracted.

limitations: The key filter is a single regex applied to each key; there is no per-pattern routing of values to different handlers.
processingNotes: Each entry's value becomes a translatable block; keys, separators, and comments form the non-translatable skeleton.
Multi-line values continued with a trailing backslash are joined per the properties format.

examples.title: Translate only label keys
examples.description: Extract only entries whose keys contain "label".

examples.title: UTF-8 output
examples.description: Emit characters literally instead of \uXXXX escapes.

exceptions.description: Regex pattern for key names. When extractAllPairs is true, matching keys are excluded. When false, matching keys are included.

extractAllPairs.description: Extract all key-value pairs for translation. When false, use extractionRules to specify which paths to extract.

extractIsolatedStrings.description: Extract string values in arrays (without keys) as translatable blocks.

extractionRules.description: Regex pattern for JSON paths to extract. Overrides extractAllPairs when specified.

genericMetaRules.description: Regex for keys whose values become generic metadata.

subfilter.description: Global subfilter format name (e.g., 'html') applied to all or matching extracted strings.

subfilterRules.title: Sub-filter rules
subfilterRules.description: Regex matching keys for which values should be processed with the sub-filter.

subfilters.title: Subfilter mappings
subfilters.description: Array of {pattern, format} mappings for embedded content in specific keys.

useCodeFinder.title: Enable Inline Code Detection
useCodeFinder.description: Enable pattern-based detection of inline codes (placeholders, tags, etc.).

useFullKeyPath.title: Use the full key path
useFullKeyPath.description: Use full path (/key1/key2) instead of immediate key as name.

useIdStack.title: Use the full path of IDs for resname
useIdStack.description: Build TextUnit IDs from nested key stack.

useKeyAsName.title: Use the key as the resname
useKeyAsName.description: Use JSON key as the Block (TextUnit) name for reference.

useLeadingSlashOnKeyPath.title: Include leading "/" on key path
useLeadingSlashOnKeyPath.description: Include leading slash in key path names.

doc.overview:
The **JSON** format reads JSON documents, extracts string values as
translatable blocks, and writes the translated values back into the same
structure. It is the common format for web and application resource files
(i18next, Chrome extension messages, generic key/value catalogues).

By default every key/value string pair is extracted. From there you can
narrow extraction with a key-exception or extraction pattern, control how
block names are derived from keys, route specific keys to notes, IDs, or
metadata, and hand embedded markup (HTML inside a value) to a subfilter.
Two presets are provided — *Chrome Extension* and *i18next* — as starting
points for those layouts.

codeFinderRules.description: Regex patterns that match inline codes within values.
codeFinderRules.help:
An array of regular expressions; text matching any of them is treated
as an inline code rather than translatable words. Only used when
`useCodeFinder` is enabled.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for these patterns to apply

escapeForwardSlashes.description: Escape forward slashes in JSON output.
escapeForwardSlashes.help:
When enabled (the default), forward slashes are written as `\/` in the
output JSON, matching the conservative escaping some JSON producers use.
Disable it to emit bare `/` characters.

exceptions.description: Regex over key names whose effect depends on `extractAllPairs`.
exceptions.help:
A regular expression matched against key names. When `extractAllPairs`
is `true`, keys matching this pattern are **excluded** from extraction.
When `extractAllPairs` is `false`, only keys matching this pattern are
**included**. The match is applied to the immediate key, or to the full
key path when `useFullKeyPath` is enabled.
examples: `^message$` — with `extractAllPairs: false`, extract only the `message` key

dependsOn.condition: determines whether matches are excluded (true) or included (false)

extractAllPairs.description: Extract all key/value string pairs for translation.
extractAllPairs.help:
When enabled (the default), every string value associated with a key is
extracted. When disabled, nothing is extracted unless a key matches the
`exceptions` pattern (the meaning of `exceptions` inverts — see below) or
an `extractionRules` pattern is set.

extractIsolatedStrings.description: Extract standalone string values inside arrays.
extractIsolatedStrings.help:
String values that appear directly in arrays — without an associated
key — are not extracted by default. Enable this to treat such isolated
strings as translatable blocks.

extractionRules.description: Regex limiting which key paths are extracted.
extractionRules.help:
A regular expression matched against key names (or full key paths when
`useFullKeyPath` is enabled). When set, only matching keys are extracted
and it overrides `extractAllPairs`.
examples: `/messages/.*|/labels/.*` — extract only keys under `messages` or `labels`

genericMetaRules.description: Regex over key names whose values become generic metadata.
genericMetaRules.help:
A regular expression matched against key names. The value of a matching
key is recorded as a generic metadata annotation on the next
translatable block, rather than being extracted as content.

idRules.description: Regex over key names whose values become block IDs.
idRules.help:
A regular expression matched against key names. The value of a matching
key is used as the name/ID of the next translatable block, overriding
`useKeyAsName`.
examples: `/id|/key` — use the value of an `id` or `key` field as the block ID

maxwidthRules.description: Regex over key names whose numeric values set a max-width constraint.
maxwidthRules.help:
A regular expression matched against key names. The numeric value of a
matching key sets a maximum-width property on the next translatable
block, used by length-constrained workflows.

dependsOn.condition: the value is interpreted in this unit

maxwidthSizeUnit.help:
The unit in which `maxwidthRules` values are interpreted: `pixel` or
`char`.

noteRules.description: Regex over key names whose values become translator notes.
noteRules.help:
A regular expression matched against key names. The value of a matching
key is attached as a translator note to the next translatable block.
examples: `/description|/comment` — attach a `description` or `comment` value as a note

subfilter.description: Global subfilter format applied to extracted values.
subfilter.help:
The name of a format used as a subfilter (for example `html`) to parse
embedded markup inside extracted string values. When set, it applies to
all extracted values unless `subfilterRules` restricts it to specific
keys.
examples: `html` — parse HTML inside JSON string values

subfilterRules.description: Regex over key names selecting which values are subfiltered.
examples: `_html$` — subfilter only keys ending in `_html`

dependsOn.condition: only takes effect when a subfilter format is set

subfilters.description: Per-pattern subfilter mappings for embedded content.
subfilters.help:
An array of `{pattern, format}` objects mapping key patterns to format
names, for routing different keys to different subfilters. Each mapping
requires both a non-empty `pattern` and a non-empty `format`.

useCodeFinder.description: Enable pattern-based inline-code detection.
useCodeFinder.help:
Enables regex-based detection of inline codes (placeholders, variables,
tags) inside translatable text. The patterns are supplied via
`codeFinderRules`.

useFullKeyPath.description: Use the full hierarchical key path as the block name.
useFullKeyPath.help:
When enabled, the block name is the full path through nested objects
(for example `parent/child`) rather than just the immediate key. The
same path is then used when matching the rule-based regex patterns.

useIdStack.description: Build block IDs from the nested key stack.
useIdStack.help:
When enabled, the block ID is composed from the stack of enclosing keys,
producing a compound identifier for nested structures.

useKeyAsName.description: Use the JSON key as the block name.
useKeyAsName.help:
When enabled (the default), the JSON key is used as the block's name
(resname) so translators and downstream tools can reference each unit.

useLeadingSlashOnKeyPath.description: Prefix full key paths with a leading slash.
useLeadingSlashOnKeyPath.help:
When `useFullKeyPath` is enabled, this controls whether the path begins
with a leading `/` (for example `/parent/child`). Enabled by default.

dependsOn.condition: only affects names when full key paths are used

limitations: The match target for the rule-based regex patterns is either the immediate key or the full key path, depending on `useFullKeyPath` — not both.
processingNotes: String values become translatable blocks; objects, arrays, and non-string scalars form the surrounding non-translatable structure.
Note, ID, generic-metadata, and max-width rules attach to the next translatable block rather than producing content of their own.

examples.title: Extract a subset of keys
examples.description: Translate only keys under the messages and labels objects.

examples.title: Notes from description keys
examples.description: Attach description values as translator notes (Chrome extension style).

examples.title: HTML inside values
examples.description: Parse HTML markup within values whose key ends in _html (i18next style).

inlineCodes.description: Pattern-based detection of inline codes (placeholders, variables, tags) within translatable text.

enabled.title: Enable Inline Code Detection
enabled.description: Enable pattern-based detection of inline codes (placeholders, tags, etc.)

rules.title: Code Finder Rules
rules.description: Regex patterns for inline code detection

rules.title: Patterns
rules.description: List of regex patterns to detect inline codes

pattern.title: Pattern
pattern.description: Regex pattern for inline code detection

sample.title: Sample Text
sample.description: Sample text to test patterns against

useAllRulesWhenTesting.title: Test All Rules Together
useAllRulesWhenTesting.description: Test all rules together or individually

mergeAdjacent.title: Merge Adjacent Codes
mergeAdjacent.description: Merge consecutive inline codes into a single placeholder

moveBoundaryCodes.title: Move Boundary Codes
moveBoundaryCodes.description: Move inline codes at segment boundaries to the skeleton (non-translatable)

simplifierRules.title: Simplifier Rules
simplifierRules.description: Rules for simplifying inline code representation

excludeKeys.description: Comma-separated list of keys to exclude from extraction.

useFullPath.description: Use full path (/key1/key2) instead of immediate key as name.

idRules.description: Regex for keys whose values become Block IDs (overrides useKeyAsName).

noteRules.title: Note rules
noteRules.description: Regex for keys whose values become translator notes.

metaRules.description: Regex for keys whose values become generic metadata.

ui:groups.label: Control what content is extracted for translation
Configure how JSON keys map to text unit identification

doc.overview: The JSON Filter processes JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) files for translation. It extracts string values — both standalone and key-associated — as translatable content, with flexible control over which paths to extract using regex rules. The filter supports multiple Unicode encodings with BOM detection, inline code patterns for placeholders, sub-filtering for embedded content (e.g., HTML within JSON values), and metadata extraction rules for notes, IDs, and length constraints.

extraction.excludeKeys.help:
Keys whose string values should be excluded from extraction (when `extractAll` is true) or included (when `extractAll` is false — inverted behavior).

When `useFullPath` is enabled in the keys settings, the regex is matched against the full path (e.g. `/root/excludedStructure/key`).
extraction.excludeKeys.values: Comma-separated key names or a regex pattern. With `useFullKeyPath`, use path patterns like `^.*?/excludedStructure/.*`.
examples: `key1,key2,key3` — exclude specific keys by name
`^.*?/excludedStructure/.*` — exclude an entire nested structure (requires full key path)

dependsOn.condition: when enabled, regex patterns apply to full paths instead of key names

extraction.extractAll.help:
Controls the default extraction behavior for key/value pairs.

- **true**: All key/value string pairs are extracted, except those matching `excludeKeys`.
- **false**: No key/value pairs are extracted unless they match `pathRules`.

Use `excludeKeys` or `pathRules` to fine-tune which content is included or excluded.

dependsOn.condition: pathRules overrides this setting when specified

extraction.includeIsolatedStrings.help: Extracts string values that appear in arrays without an associated key. For example, in `["Hello", "World"]`, the strings have no key — they are standalone/isolated. Without this option, only key-value pairs like `{"greeting": "Hello"}` are extracted.

extraction.pathRules.help:
Regex matching full JSON key paths of values to extract. **Overrides `extractAll` when specified.** Paths use `/`-separated key names.

This is an M39+ extraction rule that takes precedence over the older extractAll + excludeKeys approach.
extraction.pathRules.values: Java regex applied against the full key path (e.g. `/menu/popup/menuitem/value`). Use `.*` wildcards for subtrees.
examples: `/widgets/body.*` — extract all keys under `/widgets/body`
`/messages/.*|/labels/.*` — extract from multiple subtrees

extraction.subfilterRules.help:
Specify an Okapi filter ID to process the content of all translatable text values with a sub-filter. For example, use `okf_html` to process HTML markup embedded within JSON string values.

Leave blank for default behavior (no sub-filtering).
notes: **Cannot be used together with inline codes.** If sub-filtering is enabled, the inline code finder is ignored.
examples: `okf_html` — process HTML tags within JSON string values

inlineCodes.enabled.help:
Enables pattern-based detection of inline codes (placeholders, format specifiers, escape sequences, etc.) within extracted text. Matched patterns are converted to inline code placeholders that translators cannot accidentally modify.

The default pattern set detects printf-style format specifiers (`%s`, `%d`), escape sequences (`\n`, `\t`), and ICU-style placeholders (`{0}`).
notes: **Cannot be used together with the sub-filtering option.** If a sub-filter is configured, inline code detection is ignored.

inlineCodes.rules.help:
Regex patterns that identify inline codes within translatable text. Each pattern match is converted to an inline code placeholder.

The default patterns detect:
- Printf-style specifiers: `%s`, `%d`, `%2$s`
- Escape sequences: `\n`, `\r`, `\t`
- ICU/positional placeholders: `{0}`, `{name}`
inlineCodes.rules.values: Each rule contains a `pattern` field with a Java regex. Use the sample field and presets to test patterns.
examples: `<[^>]+>` — detect HTML tags as inline codes
`%[\\d]*[sdioxXucsfeEgGpn]` — detect printf format specifiers
`\\{[^}]+\\}` — detect ICU message format placeholders

keys.idRules.help:
Regex matching keys whose **values** become the `resname` (Block ID) of sibling text units. This overrides `useAsName`.

Useful when the JSON structure stores IDs as values in a sibling key rather than using the key name itself.
keys.idRules.values: Java regex matched against key names or full paths.
examples: Given `{"key": "datePicker_march", "text": "March"}`, setting idRules to `key` produces a text unit with `resname="datePicker_march"` for the "March" value.

keys.leadingSlash.help:
Controls whether the full key path starts with a `/` character. Only applies when `useFullPath` is enabled.

- **true**: `/menu/popup/value`
- **false**: `menu/popup/value`

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when useFullPath is enabled

keys.useAsName.help: Uses the JSON key as the `resname` (resource name / Block name) for the extracted text unit. This makes it easier to identify translations by their original JSON key in downstream tools.

keys.useFullPath.help:
Uses the complete path from root to the key (e.g. `/menu/popup/menuitem/value`) instead of just the immediate key name. `useAsName` must be enabled for this to take effect.

When enabled, exception regex patterns in `excludeKeys` also match against the full path.
examples: Key path: `/menu/value/popup/menuitem/value`

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for useFullPath to take effect

keys.useIdStack.help: Builds text unit IDs by stacking nested JSON keys. Useful for creating deterministic, path-based IDs for deeply nested structures.

maxwidthSizeUnit.description: The size-unit attribute value used for XLIFF length restriction elements.
maxwidthSizeUnit.help:
The string value used as the `size-unit` attribute on XLIFF `<trans-unit>` elements when `maxwidthRules` extracts length restrictions.

Common values include `char`, `byte`, `pixel`, etc.
maxwidthSizeUnit.values: Free-form string used as the XLIFF `size-unit` attribute value (e.g. `char`, `byte`, `pixel`).

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when maxwidthRules is configured

metadata.maxwidthRules.help:
Regex matching keys whose numeric **values** define maximum width constraints for sibling text units. The matched value becomes the `maxwidth` attribute in XLIFF.

- The matched value applies to **all other elements at the same array level**.
- Only **one** matching key is allowed per hierarchy level.
- For nested arrays, each level can have its own maxwidth definition.
- If no key matches at a sublevel but a parent level has a match, the parent's value applies to the deepest level only.
- When both parent and child levels have matches, the parent's matching key must appear **after** the last child element.
metadata.maxwidthRules.values: Java regex matched against key names or full paths.
notes: Only one matching array element per hierarchy level is allowed.
For parent-level keys to apply correctly with child-level keys, the parent matching key must be defined after the last child element.

metadata.metaRules.help:
Regex matching keys whose **values** are added as generic metadata (`<context-group>` in XLIFF) to sibling text units.

This metadata is preserved through the translation pipeline and can be used by downstream tools.
metadata.metaRules.values: Java regex matched against key names or full paths.
examples: `/widgets/image.*` — attach image-related metadata to text units

metadata.noteRules.help:
Regex matching keys whose **values** are attached as translator notes (`<note>` in XLIFF) to sibling text units.

Useful for including descriptions or comments that help translators understand context.
metadata.noteRules.values: Java regex matched against key names or full paths.
examples: `/description|/comment` — keys named "description" or "comment" become notes
`/widgets/name.*` — all name-like keys under widgets become notes

output.escapeSlashes.help:
Controls whether forward slashes (`/`) are escaped as `\/` in the output JSON. JSON allows both forms, but some systems expect escaped slashes.

- **true** (default): forward slashes are escaped (`\/`)
- **false**: forward slashes are written as-is (`/`)

limitations: Comments within a JSON string are parsed as part of the string content, not as comments. A configured sub-filter will then process these as true comments.
Inline codes and sub-filtering cannot be used simultaneously — enabling sub-filtering disables inline code detection.
processingNotes: JSON files are normally in Unicode encoding, but the filter supports any encoding. Encoding is determined by: BOM detection first, then `charset` declaration in the first 1000 characters, then default encoding.
If output encoding is UTF-8 and input was also UTF-8, a BOM is written only if one was detected in the input. If input was not UTF-8, no BOM is written.
Line-break style (CRLF/LF) of the output matches the input document.
Though not technically legal in JSON, the filter supports comments: `// comment`, `# comment`, `/* comment */`, and `<!-- comment -->`.
Comments within a JSON string value are parsed as part of the string content, not as actual comments.

examples.title: Basic JSON file extraction
examples.description: A simple JSON file with key-value pairs. All string values associated with keys are extracted by default.

examples.title: Using extraction rules with ID rules
examples.description: Extract specific paths and use sibling key values as resource names. Given JSON with `key` and `text` pairs, the `idRules` regex `key` causes the value of the `key` field to become the `resname` of the `text` field's text unit.
examples.output:
```xml
<trans-unit id="tu1" resname="datePicker_marchMonth">
  <source>March</source>
</trans-unit>
<trans-unit id="tu2" resname="datePicker_aprilMonth">
  <source>April</source>
</trans-unit>
```

examples.title: FPRM-style extraction rules (M39+)
examples.description: The M39+ extraction rules override the older extractAll + exceptions approach. These regex patterns match against full key paths.
examples.config:
```yaml
extraction:
  pathRules: "/widgets/body.*"
metadata:
  noteRules: "/widgets/name.*"
  metaRules: "/widgets/image.*"
keys:
  idRules: "/widgets/id.*"
```

entries.description: Read and write the Kapi Localization Format (.klf), the JSON envelope for strings extracted from source code.

doc.overview:
This format reads and writes the Kapi Localization Format (`.klf`), a
structured JSON envelope for translatable strings extracted from
source code such as JSX / React components. A KLF document is a
`kind: kapi-localization-format` envelope holding one or more
documents, each carrying blocks. Every block has a structured run
sequence — text plus placeholders, paired inline codes, plurals, and
selects.

On read, each KLF block becomes one translatable block. Its source
text is the flattened run sequence: placeholders contribute their
equivalent (wrapped in braces), paired codes elide their wrappers, and
plural forms collapse to the `other` branch. The block ID is carried
verbatim, and the full structured run graph travels alongside in an
annotation so the writer can reassemble the original document. The
writer reverses the process; a block that arrives without that
annotation (for example one inserted by an intermediate tool) is
written as a minimal text-only run sequence so the file stays
well-formed.
limitations: This is a neokapi-specific exchange format; the okapi-bridge daemon ships no equivalent filter.
Blocks that lose their structured-run annotation in the pipeline are written back as plain text, without the original placeholder or paired-code structure.
processingNotes: One translatable block per KLF block; the block ID is preserved verbatim.
Source text is the flattened run sequence — placeholders become brace-wrapped equivalents, paired codes elide their wrappers, plurals collapse to the `other` branch.
Translator-facing context (file, component, element, JSX path, line, notes) and the structured runs travel in a block annotation for faithful round-tripping.

examples.title: Round-trip a KLF document
examples.description:
No configuration is required. Declare the format to read a `.klf`
envelope and write it back after translation.

useCodeFinder.title: Enable Inline Code Detection

doc.overview:
The **Markdown** format reads Markdown documents, extracts prose as
translatable blocks, and writes the translations back while preserving the
document's structure — headings, lists, emphasis, links, and code. Inline
markup (bold, italic, links, inline code) is exposed as inline codes inside
each block so the surrounding syntax survives translation.

By default paragraphs, headings, list items, blockquotes, and image alt text
are translatable, while code blocks, YAML front matter, raw HTML blocks, and
link/image URLs are emitted as non-translatable structure. Each of those
categories can be toggled, and embedded HTML can be routed to a subfilter.

codeFinderRules.description: Regex patterns that match inline codes within text.
codeFinderRules.help:
An array of regular expressions; text matching any of them is treated
as an inline code rather than translatable words. Only used when
`useCodeFinder` is enabled.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for these patterns to apply

subfilter.description: Subfilter format for HTML content within Markdown.
subfilter.help:
The name of a format used as a subfilter (for example `html`) to parse
HTML content embedded in the Markdown, so HTML markup is handled
structurally instead of as opaque text.
examples: `html` — parse embedded HTML through the HTML format

translateBlockQuotes.help:
When enabled (the default), the prose inside blockquotes is translatable.
Disable it to leave quoted material untranslated.

translateCodeBlocks.description: Make fenced and indented code blocks translatable.
translateCodeBlocks.help:
When enabled, fenced and indented code blocks are extracted as
translatable content. By default they are emitted as non-translatable
data, since code is rarely localized.

translateFrontMatter.description: Make YAML front matter values translatable.
translateFrontMatter.help:
When enabled, values in the document's YAML front matter are extracted
as translatable content. By default front matter is emitted as
non-translatable data.

translateHTMLBlocks.help:
When enabled, raw HTML blocks embedded in the Markdown are extracted as
translatable content. By default they are emitted as non-translatable
data; route them to a subfilter via `subfilter` for structured HTML
handling instead.

translateImageAlt.description: Include image alt text in translatable content.
translateImageAlt.help:
When enabled (the default), the alt text of images is included in the
translatable content of the block that contains the image.

translateURLs.description: Make link and image URLs translatable.
translateURLs.help:
When enabled, the URLs of links and images are extracted as translatable
content (useful when localized assets or pages live at different paths).
By default URLs are left untranslated.

unescapeBackslashCharacters.description: Parse backslash-escaped punctuation in the source.
unescapeBackslashCharacters.help:
When enabled, backslash-escaped punctuation in the source document (for
example `\*`) is parsed as escaped characters rather than literal
backslash sequences.

useCodeFinder.description: Enable regex-based inline-code detection.
useCodeFinder.help:
Enables regex-based detection of additional inline codes within
translatable text, beyond the Markdown inline markup already recognized.
The patterns are supplied via `codeFinderRules`.

limitations: Code blocks, front matter, raw HTML blocks, and URLs are non-translatable by default; enable the corresponding option to extract them.
processingNotes: Inline markup (emphasis, links, inline code) becomes inline codes within blocks, so it survives translation unchanged.
Structural elements (fences, list markers, heading prefixes) form the non-translatable skeleton.

examples.title: Translate front matter too
examples.description: Include YAML front matter values alongside the prose.

examples.title: Handle embedded HTML structurally
examples.description: Route inline and block HTML through the HTML subfilter.

nonTranslateBlocks.description: Comma-separated prefixes identifying block quotes to exclude from translation.

generateHeaderAnchors.title: Generate Header Anchors
