charactersToEscape.title: Characters to Escape
charactersToEscape.description: Punctuation characters to backslash-escape on export.

htmlEntitiesToEscape.title: Characters to escape as HTML entities

unescapeBackslashCharacters.title: Support Backslash Escaping
unescapeBackslashCharacters.description: Parse backslash-escaped punctuation in source documents.

htmlSubfilter.title: HTML Subfilter Configuration
htmlSubfilter.description: Custom HTML filter configuration ID for processing HTML within Markdown.

yamlSubfilter.title: YAML Subfilter Configuration
yamlSubfilter.description: Custom YAML filter configuration ID for processing YAML metadata headers.

doc.overview: The Markdown Filter extracts translatable text from Markdown files based on the [CommonMark](http://commonmark.org) specification, with additional support for [GitHub-flavored Markdown](https://guides.github.com/features/mastering-markdown/). HTML elements within Markdown are processed by the HTML filter (configurable separately). The filter supports inline code finding, subfilter delegation for HTML and YAML content, and various options to control which Markdown constructs are exposed for translation.

charactersToEscape.description: Punctuation characters to backslash-escape on export.
charactersToEscape.help: Lists the punctuation characters that will be backslash-escaped on export. Only applies when `unescapeBackslashCharacters` is enabled.
charactersToEscape.values: A string of punctuation characters. Default: ``*_`{}[]<>()#+\-.!|``

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this setting to take effect

extraction.translateHeaderMetadata.help: Controls how a YAML front-matter block (the content fenced by `---` at the top of the document) is handled. When disabled (the default), the whole front matter is emitted as skeleton and never exposed for translation. When enabled, the YAML between the fences is extracted and parsed through the YAML subfilter, so individual metadata fields (title, description, and so on) become translatable.
notes: Which metadata fields are actually extracted is governed by the YAML subfilter configuration (`yamlSubfilter`).

generateHeaderAnchors.description: Automatically generate named anchors for headings to preserve link targets.
generateHeaderAnchors.help:
Automatically generates explicit named anchors for headings using the `{#my-anchor}` syntax. This provides **stable anchors** for hyperlinks that reference translatable header text — without this, links to headers may break when the header text is translated.

Some Markdown parsers support explicit named anchors in header markup.
examples: `## My Section` → `## My Section {#my-section}` — anchor generated from header text

htmlSubfilter.description: Custom HTML filter configuration ID for processing HTML within Markdown.
htmlSubfilter.help:
Specifies a custom HTML filter configuration to use when processing HTML elements within Markdown. The configuration file must be saved with a `.fprm` suffix in a known location.

Leave empty to use the default HTML filter configuration (which is tailored for Markdown content).

To customize inline code patterns for HTML content within Markdown, configure them in the HTML subfilter configuration, not in the Markdown filter's own inline code settings.
htmlSubfilter.values: Configuration ID in the format `okf_html@custom-name` (without `.fprm` suffix). The corresponding file `okf_html@custom-name.fprm` must exist.
notes: The Markdown filter's own Inline Code Finder does **not** apply to HTML inline tags or HTML blocks. To define inline codes for HTML content, configure them in the HTML subfilter.
examples: `okf_html@markdown-custom` — uses `okf_html@markdown-custom.fprm` configuration

nonTranslateBlocks.description: Comma-separated prefixes identifying block quotes to exclude from translation.
nonTranslateBlocks.help: Prevents specific block quotes from being extracted for translation. Block quotes whose content starts with one of the specified strings will be skipped entirely.
nonTranslateBlocks.values: Comma-separated list of prefix strings. Any block quote starting with one of these strings will not be extracted.
examples: `Note:,Warning:` — skip block quotes that start with 'Note:' or 'Warning:'

output.htmlEntitiesToEscape.help: Writer-side option listing characters that should be encoded as HTML entities when the translated Markdown is written out. Enter the raw characters as a single string — for example `<>&` causes those three to be written as `<`, `>`, and `&`. Leave empty (the default) to disable entity escaping on output.
output.htmlEntitiesToEscape.values: A string of literal characters to escape (default empty — no escaping)
examples: `<>` — escape angle brackets as `<` and `>` on export

parseMdx.description: Parse out multi-line MDX export blocks as non-translatable skeleton.
parseMdx.help: Parses out multi-line `export` blocks as skeleton (non-translatable). This is useful for MDX files that mix Markdown with JSX/JavaScript export expressions.
notes: **Experimental** — this feature uses regex-based parsing of MDX expressions and may not handle all edge cases.

translateCodeBlocks.description: Expose contents of fenced code blocks for translation.
translateCodeBlocks.help: Controls whether the contents of **fenced** code blocks (delimited by ` ``` ` or `~~~`) are exposed for translation. Fenced code blocks often contain code samples that should not be translated, but may contain user-facing text in documentation contexts.

translateImageAltText.description: Extract image alt text from Markdown and HTML img tags for translation.
translateImageAltText.help: Extracts alt text from images for translation. Applies to both Markdown image syntax (`![alt text](url)`) and HTML `` tags with `alt` attributes (``).

translateIndentedCodeBlocks.description: Expose contents of indented code blocks for translation.
translateIndentedCodeBlocks.help: Controls whether the contents of **indented** code blocks (lines indented by 4+ spaces or 1+ tab) are exposed for translation. These are an alternative code block syntax in CommonMark.

translateInlineCodeBlocks.description: Expose contents of backtick-delimited inline code for translation.
translateInlineCodeBlocks.help: Controls whether text delimited by **single backticks** (`` `inline code` ``) is exposed for translation. Inline code typically contains technical terms, commands, or variable names that may not need translation.

translateUrls.description: Extract URLs in link and image statements as translatable subflows.
translateUrls.help:
URLs in link (`[text](url)`) and image (`![alt](url)`) statements are normally treated as non-translatable. Enabling this extracts URLs as **subflows** — they appear as separate translation units *before* the segment that references them.

The extracted subflow is referenced from the main segment using `[#$tuN]` notation in the XLIFF output.
notes: When subflows are extracted, the subflow translation unit appears **before** the segment containing the link in the XLIFF file.

unescapeBackslashCharacters.description: Parse backslash-escaped punctuation in source documents.
unescapeBackslashCharacters.help:
Enables parsing of backslash-escaped punctuation in source documents. When enabled, the filter recognizes constructs like `\*` as escaped punctuation rather than literal backslash + character.

On export, characters listed in `charactersToEscape` will be re-escaped with backslashes.

urlPattern.description: Regex pattern to filter which URLs are extracted when URL translation is enabled.
urlPattern.help: Restricts which URLs are extracted for translation when `translateUrls` is enabled. Only URLs matching this Java regular expression will be extracted as subflows.
urlPattern.values: Java regex pattern. Default `.+` matches all URLs.
examples: `^https://example\.com/.*` — only extract URLs from a specific domain
`.*\.(html|htm)$` — only extract URLs pointing to HTML pages

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this pattern to have any effect

yamlSubfilter.description: Custom YAML filter configuration ID for processing YAML metadata headers.
yamlSubfilter.help:
Specifies a custom YAML filter configuration for processing any YAML metadata header detected in the document. This allows fine-grained control over which metadata fields are extracted for translation.

Leave empty to use the default YAML filter configuration.
yamlSubfilter.values: Configuration ID referencing a YAML filter configuration (`.fprm` file).

limitations: Designed for CommonMark-based Markdown; not all Markdown dialects are fully supported.
MDX parsing (`parseMdx`) is experimental and uses regex-based extraction.

The wiki lists `translateImageAltText` as the parameter name for both 'Translate YAML Metadata Header' and 'Translate Image Alt Text' — this appears to be a documentation error; the YAML header parameter likely has a distinct internal name.
processingNotes: If the file has a Unicode Byte-Order-Mark (BOM), the corresponding encoding (UTF-8, UTF-16, etc.) is used automatically. Otherwise, the default encoding specified in the filter options is used.
HTML inline elements (tags) and HTML blocks (content between block-forming start/end tags) are delegated to the HTML filter for processing.
The Inline Code Finder applies only to translatable text in the Markdown portion of the document — it does **not** apply to HTML inline tags or HTML blocks.
URLs extracted for translation appear as **subflows** — separate translation units that are extracted *before* the segment referencing them.

examples.title: URL translation as subflows
examples.description: When `translateUrls` is enabled, URLs in image/link statements are extracted as separate translation units (subflows) that appear before the referencing segment in the XLIFF output.
examples.input:
```markdown
Please click ![The Information desk logo](images/circled-i.jpg) for help.
```
examples.output:
```xml
<trans-unit id="tu2" restype="x-img-link" xml:space="preserve">
<source xml:lang="en">images/circled-i.jpg</source>
</trans-unit>
<trans-unit id="tu1" xml:space="preserve">
<source xml:lang="en">Please click <bpt id="1">![</bpt>The Information desk logo<ept id="1">]([#$tu2])</ept> for help.</source>
</trans-unit>
```

entries.description: Extract and update translatable prose in MDX (.mdx) files while preserving JSX, ESM, and expressions.

schema.title: MDX Format
schema.description: Configuration for the MDX (.mdx) format reader/writer. MDX is CommonMark Markdown extended with ESM imports/exports, JSX components, and {expression} braces. Markdown prose is extracted using the same toggles as the Markdown format; ESM, JSX, and expressions are preserved verbatim and never translated.

codeFinderRules.title: Code Finder Rules
codeFinderRules.description: Regex patterns that match inline codes within translatable text.

translateBlockQuotes.title: Translate Block Quotes
translateBlockQuotes.description: If true, blockquote content is translatable.

translateCodeBlocks.title: Translate Code Blocks
translateCodeBlocks.description: If true, fenced and indented code blocks are translatable. If false, emitted as non-translatable data.

translateFrontMatter.title: Translate YAML Front Matter
translateFrontMatter.description: If true, YAML front matter values are translatable. If false, emitted as non-translatable data.

translateHTMLBlocks.title: Translate HTML Blocks
translateHTMLBlocks.description: If true, raw HTML blocks are translatable. If false, emitted as non-translatable data.

translateImageAlt.title: Translate Image Alt Text
translateImageAlt.description: If true, image alt text is included in translatable content.

translateURLs.title: Translate Hyperlink URLs
translateURLs.description: If true, link and image URLs are translatable.

useCodeFinder.title: Enable Inline Code Detection
useCodeFinder.description: Enable regex-based inline code detection within translatable text.

doc.overview:
The **MDX** format reads `.mdx` files — CommonMark Markdown extended with
JSX components, ESM `import`/`export` statements, and `{expression}` braces —
the authoring format of modern documentation and content sites (Docusaurus,
Next.js, Astro).

Translatable Markdown prose is extracted using the same machinery and
configuration as the Markdown format (paragraphs, headings, list items,
link/image text, inline emphasis). MDX-specific constructs — ESM statements,
JSX tags with their attributes and children, top-level `{expression}` blocks,
and GFM tables — are preserved byte-for-byte and never translated, with
component names, attribute values, import paths, and expressions left
untouched. Read→write reproduces real-world `.mdx` exactly when nothing is
translated; the reader self-verifies each prose region and falls back to
opaque preservation if a region would not round-trip byte-faithfully.
processingNotes: Markdown prose is translatable; ESM, JSX (including children, in this version), expressions, and GFM tables are opaque and preserved verbatim.
Configuration parameters mirror the Markdown format (e.g. frontmatter and code-block handling).
Detection is by the unique `.mdx` extension.

inlineCodes.description: Pattern-based detection of inline codes (placeholders, variables, tags) within translatable text.

enabled.description: Apply regular expressions to convert matched text patterns into inline codes.

extractBodyPages.title: Body Pages
extractBodyPages.description: Extract translatable text from body pages.

extractMasterPages.title: Master Pages
extractMasterPages.description: Extract translatable text from master pages.

extractReferencePages.title: Reference Pages
extractReferencePages.description: Extract translatable text from reference pages.

extractHiddenPages.title: Hidden Pages
extractHiddenPages.description: Extract translatable text from hidden pages.

extractVariables.title: Extract Variables
extractVariables.description: Extract the definitions of FrameMaker variables for translation.

extractIndexMarkers.title: Extract Index Markers
extractIndexMarkers.description: Extract index marker text from extractable pages as separate text units.

extractLinks.title: Extract Links
extractLinks.description: Extract hypertext link URLs from extractable pages as separate text units.

doc.overview: The MIF Filter processes Adobe FrameMaker Interchange Format (MIF) documents. It supports MIF v8.0 and above, with automatic encoding detection based on file version. The filter extracts translatable text from body, hidden, master, and reference pages, and supports extraction of variables, index markers, and hypertext links. The MIF 9.0 specification is available from Adobe.

extractBodyPages.description: Extract translatable text from body pages.
extractBodyPages.help: Extracts translatable text from the **body pages** — the main content area of the FrameMaker document.

extractHiddenPages.description: Extract translatable text from hidden pages.
extractHiddenPages.help: Extracts translatable text from **hidden pages** in the document.

extractIndexMarkers.description: Extract index marker text from extractable pages as separate text units.
extractIndexMarkers.help:
Extracts index markers from extractable pages. Each index entry's text is extracted as a **separate text unit** placed *before* the text unit containing the index marker.

This only applies to pages that are enabled for extraction (body, hidden, master, or reference pages).

extractLinks.description: Extract hypertext link URLs from extractable pages as separate text units.
extractLinks.help:
Extracts URLs from hypertext links in extractable pages. Each URL is extracted as a **separate text unit** placed *before* the text unit containing the hypertext marker.

This only applies to pages that are enabled for extraction.

extractMasterPages.description: Extract translatable text from master pages.
extractMasterPages.help: Extracts translatable text from **master pages**, which define page layouts, headers, and footers.

extractReferencePages.description: Extract translatable text from reference pages.
extractReferencePages.help: Extracts translatable text from **reference pages**. Note that FrameMaker creates new documents with several reference pages that already contain text, so enabling this may produce unexpected text units from default template content.
notes: By default, FrameMaker creates new documents with several reference pages that contain text — be aware of extra content when enabling this option.

extractVariables.description: Extract the definitions of FrameMaker variables for translation.
extractVariables.help: Extracts the **definitions** of FrameMaker variables so they can be translated. This covers all variable definitions in the document's variable catalog.

extraction.extractHardReturnsAsText.help: Determines how hard returns (forced line breaks, encoded as `HardReturn` characters) inside a paragraph's text are treated. When enabled (the default), the line feed is kept as text and the whole paragraph stays in one text unit. When disabled, a hard return is moved to the skeleton and closes the current string, so a paragraph containing hard returns is split into separate text units at each break. Disable it only when the document uses hard returns as genuine segment boundaries rather than as soft formatting within a sentence.

extraction.extractPgfNumFormatsInline.help: Controls how the autonumber / paragraph-number format text (`<PgfNumFormat>`, e.g. the literal label and counter pattern of a numbered list or heading) is extracted. When disabled (the default), the format text is extracted as a **separate referent text unit** placed before the paragraph and referenced by a placeholder code, keeping the numbering pattern apart from the body text. When enabled, the format text is folded **inline** into the paragraph's own text unit. Formats found inside the paragraph catalog (`PgfCatalog`) are always treated as inline regardless of this setting.

extraction.extractReferenceFormats.help: Includes the cross-reference format definitions block (`<XRefFormats>`) in the extraction. When enabled, the filter processes the cross-reference format building blocks so their textual parts can be translated; when disabled (the default), the whole block is skipped and emitted as non-translatable skeleton. Enable this when localized documents need cross-reference wording (e.g. "see page", "on page") adapted for the target language.

useCodeFinder.description: Apply regular expressions to convert matched text patterns into inline codes.
useCodeFinder.help:
Enables inline code detection using regular expressions. Any match in extracted text is converted to an inline code.

The default expression matches FrameMaker building-block tags:
```
<\$.*?>
```
This matches patterns like `<$daynum>`, `<$monthname>`, `<$year>` commonly found in FrameMaker variable definitions.

limitations: MIF versions below 8.0 are **not supported**.
Very large embedded insets (e.g. images) may cause **Java heap memory issues**. The workaround is to link to external objects rather than embed them.

The filter does not perform **font mapping** — if the translated file uses a language not supported by the source fonts, you must manually update the paragraph and character catalogs to use appropriate fonts.
processingNotes: Input encoding is **automatically detected** based on the MIF file version and document metadata.
MIF v8 and above use **UTF-8** for both input and output.
Index entries and link URLs are extracted as separate text units placed **before** the text unit containing their respective markers.

examples.title: Default inline code pattern
examples.description: The default code finder expression matches FrameMaker building-block variable tags like `<$daynum>`, `<$monthname>`, `<$year>`. These are converted to inline codes so translators see them as placeholders rather than translatable text.

entries.description: Write compiled GNU gettext MO catalogs — the binary runtime form of a PO file.

schema.title: MO Format (GNU Gettext, binary)
schema.description: Writer for compiled gettext MO catalogs. Consumes Blocks and emits a binary catalog keyed by (msgctxt, msgid). msgctxt is taken from Block.Properties["context"] when present, else Block.Name, else Block.ID.

doc.overview:
MO (Machine Object) is the compiled binary catalog that GNU gettext loads
at runtime. It stores one entry per `(msgctxt, msgid)` pair together with
its translation, indexed for lookup by gettext libraries. Where a PO file
is the editable source catalog, the MO file is the artifact an application
ships and reads.

neokapi provides MO as a **writer only**. The pipeline consumes translatable
Blocks and emits a binary catalog for a single target locale. `msgctxt` is
resolved from each Block in order: `Properties["context"]`, then `Name`, then
`ID` — so Blocks produced by the PO reader (which set the context property)
and Blocks produced by the JSON reader with full key paths (which set the
Block name) both compile to MO without extra configuration. Untranslated
entries are dropped, since a runtime catalog is expected to carry only
translations, and the required empty-`msgid` metadata header entry is always
written.

MO cannot be read back through the pipeline. Although the format registers a
reader so that `.mo` is recognised for output routing and extension
detection, opening an MO file as a source returns an error directing you to
load compiled catalogs with a gettext runtime library instead.
limitations: Write-only in the pipeline; MO files cannot be parsed as a translation source.
Emits a catalog for a single target locale; untranslated entries are omitted.
The output is a binary catalog, not the editable PO source — keep the PO file as the editable form.
processingNotes: One MO entry is emitted per translated Block, keyed by `(msgctxt, msgid)`.
`msgctxt` is taken from `Block.Properties["context"]`, falling back to `Block.Name`, then `Block.ID`.
The empty-`msgid` metadata header entry is always written.

entries.description: Read and write Moses InlineText files — line-aligned corpora for statistical MT.

doc.overview:
Moses InlineText is the line-oriented format used by the Moses statistical
machine-translation system. Each line in the file is one text unit, which
makes the format a natural fit for line-aligned source/target corpora.
Inline codes are represented with XLIFF-flavoured markup: `<g>` paired tags,
`<x/>` placeholders, `<bx/>`/`<ex/>` paired open/close codes (all carrying
numeric ids), and `<lb/>` for line breaks within an entry.

neokapi reads and writes Moses InlineText. The reader walks the input
line by line — carriage return, line feed, and CRLF all act as line
separators — and emits each non-empty line as one translatable Block with
whitespace preserved; empty lines flow through as non-translatable data. It
also decodes the Moses inline markup, parsing `<g>`/`<x/>`/`<bx/>`/`<ex/>`
into inline codes and `<lb/>` into line breaks so the surrounding text
reaches the Block as translatable content while the markup survives as
opaque codes. This format has no configurable JSON schema in this reference.
limitations: Each line is treated as an independent text unit; there is no document-level structure beyond line ordering.
Empty lines carry no translatable content and pass through as data.
processingNotes: One translatable Block per non-empty line; CR, LF, and CRLF all act as line separators and whitespace is preserved.
Decodes Moses inline markup — `<g>`, `<x/>`, `<bx/>`, `<ex/>` into inline codes and `<lb/>` into line breaks.
Empty lines flow through as non-translatable data.

doc.overview: The Moses Text Filter processes InlineText files used by the [Moses MT system](http://www.statmt.org/moses/). Each line in the file is read as a separate text unit. Inline codes are represented using `<g>`, `<x>`, `<bx>`, and `<ex>` elements with numeric identifiers, and line-breaks within entries are represented by `<lb/>`.
processingNotes: If the document has a BOM, it is used to determine the input encoding; otherwise **UTF-8** is used regardless of any specified default encoding.
Output encoding is always forced to **UTF-8**.
Output line-break style matches the original input.

Reading a Moses InlineText file may produce more text units than the original document because each segment is extracted as a single entry, and the Moses format has no way to mark that multiple entries belong to the same text unit.
Use the **Moses InlineText Extraction Step** to create Moses files from other formats, and the **Moses InlineText Leveraging Step** to re-group entries back into their original text units.

examples.title: Moses InlineText format
examples.description: Example of a Moses InlineText file with four text units. Inline codes use `<g>`, `<x>`, `<bx>`, `<ex>` elements; line-breaks within entries use `<lb/>`.
examples.input:
```
Text in the first entry.
Text of the second entry<lb/>which spans<lb/>several lines
Third entry.
Fourth entry with <g id="1">bold words</g> and some code:<x id="2"/>
```

csvAutoDetectColumnTypes.title: Use auto-detection (a special row in each input file)

csvAutoDetectColumnTypesRow.title: Row at which the special configuration information is (first=1)

csvFormatCols.title: Comma-separated list of the colums with non-plain-text content (e.g. '3:okf_markdown', first=0)

csvNoExtractCols.title: Comma-separated list of the colums not to extract (first=0)

csvStartingRow.title: Row at which the extraction starts (first=1)

doc.overview: The Multi-Parsers Filter extracts translatable text from two-level complex formats, where an outer format contains inner formats. A typical use case is a CSV file where some columns contain Markdown, some HTML, and some plain text. By default, the filter is configured to process a CSV file where all columns are translatable and treated as plain text.

extraction.csvAutoDetectColumnTypes.help: Selects where per-column extraction types come from. When disabled (the default), the types are taken from the static configuration (`csvNoExtractCols` and `csvFormatCols`). When enabled, they are read at runtime from a designated row of each input file (`csvAutoDetectColumnTypesRow`), whose cells hold tokens such as `notrans`, `text`, or a filter id like `okf_html`. Enabling this disables `csvNoExtractCols` and `csvFormatCols`.

extraction.csvAutoDetectColumnTypesRow.help: The 1-based row number that holds the per-column type tokens when `csvAutoDetectColumnTypes` is enabled. Each cell names the handling for its column — for example `notrans,text,okf_html,okf_markdown,text`. Has no effect unless auto-detection is on.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this row to be consulted

extraction.csvFormatCols.help:
Comma-separated list mapping 0-based column indexes to the sub-filter that should parse that column's content, using the form `index:filterId`. Columns not listed are treated as plain text. Whitespace between entries is allowed. Leave empty (the default) to treat every column as plain text.

Ignored when `csvAutoDetectColumnTypes` is enabled.
extraction.csvFormatCols.values: Comma-separated `index:filterId` pairs (default empty — all columns plain text)
examples: `1:okf_markdown, 5:okf_html` — parse column 1 as Markdown and column 5 as HTML

dependsOn.condition: ignored when auto-detection is enabled

extraction.csvNoExtractCols.help:
Comma-separated list of 0-based column indexes that should not be extracted; their content is sent to the skeleton instead of becoming translatable text. Whitespace between values is allowed. Leave empty (the default) to extract every column.

Ignored when `csvAutoDetectColumnTypes` is enabled, since the per-column types then come from the detection row instead.
extraction.csvNoExtractCols.values: Comma-separated 0-based column indexes (default empty — all columns extracted)
examples: `2,4` — leave columns 2 and 4 out of extraction

dependsOn.condition: ignored when auto-detection is enabled

extraction.csvStartingRow.help: The 1-based row number at which extraction begins. Rows before it are written to the skeleton and not extracted — set it to `2` to skip a single header row. The default of `1` extracts from the first row.

limitations: This filter is **BETA** — behavior and parameters may change in future releases.
processingNotes: If the input file has a Unicode Byte-Order-Mark (BOM), the corresponding encoding (UTF-8, UTF-16, etc.) is used automatically.
If no BOM is present, the default encoding specified in the filter options is used.

examples.title: Per-column sub-filters by index
examples.description: Treat a CSV where column 1 holds Markdown and column 5 holds HTML; the remaining columns are extracted as plain text. Column indexes are 0-based.

examples.title: Skip non-translatable columns and start below the header
examples.description: Exclude an internal ID column (index 0) from extraction and begin extracting at row 2, leaving the header row in the skeleton.

examples.title: Auto-detect column types from a configuration row

doc.overview: The OpenOffice Filter processes OpenOffice.org documents in OpenDocument format (ODF): ODT (text), ODS (spreadsheet), ODP (slides), ODG (graphics), and their corresponding template formats. The filter internally wraps an ODF Filter for raw OpenDocument XML files. Documents are ZIP archives containing multiple sub-documents (`content.xml`, `meta.xml`, `style.xml`), each treated independently during extraction.

extractMetadata.help: Extracts translatable metadata from `meta.xml` within the ODF package (e.g., document title, subject, keywords).

extractNotes.description: Extract the content of annotation elements as translatable text
extractNotes.help:
Extracts the content of `<office:annotation>` elements (notes/comments) as translatable text.

When disabled, annotations are skipped entirely during extraction.
notes: **Known issue:** This option may not take effect — notes may be extracted regardless of the setting.

extractReferences.description: Extract the content of bookmark reference elements as translatable text
extractReferences.help:
Extracts the content of `<text:bookmark-ref>` elements. These elements contain a **copy** of the referenced text that is automatically updated by OpenOffice when the document is refreshed.

Any translation of referenced text will be **overwritten** the next time the document updates its references. However, enabling this can be useful to provide context — the referenced text appears inline within the segment where it is inserted.
notes: **Known issue:** This option may not take effect — references may be extracted regardless of the setting.
Translations of bookmark references will be lost when OpenOffice updates the document, since the reference content is regenerated from the referent.

extraction.encodeCharacterEntityReferenc.help: Writer-side option affecting only the read-only inline elements that carry encodable glyphs — `text:page-number` and `text:bookmark-ref`. When enabled (the default), the character data inside those elements is run through the XML encoder, so reserved characters such as `&`, `<`, and `>` are written as character entity references. When disabled, that text is passed through literally. Other elements are unaffected by this option.

limitations: Some deleted text may get extracted. Accept or reject all revision changes before processing the input document.
The `extractNotes` and `extractReferences` options may not work — content may be extracted regardless of settings.
Sequential tabs may be reduced to a single tab during extraction and merge round-trip due to incorrect handling of space/tab elements on input.
The target (output) encoding **must** be set to UTF-8 when extracting documents intended to be merged back.
processingNotes: Input encoding is automatically detected; any user-specified encoding is ignored. Output is always UTF-8.
Line-breaks in output are always simple linefeed (LF).
ODF documents are ZIP archives. The filter processes `content.xml`, `meta.xml`, and `style.xml` as separate sub-documents. In XLIFF output, each becomes a separate `<file>` element.
Typically only `content.xml` contains extracted text; `meta.xml` and `style.xml` are often empty.

examples.title: Keep special glyphs literal in page-number and reference text
examples.description: Disable entity encoding so character data inside read-only inline elements (page numbers, bookmark references) is passed through verbatim instead of being escaped to character entity references on output.

examples.title: Also extract annotations
examples.description: Notes and bookmark references are not extracted by default; enable them to surface annotation content as translatable text.

okf_plaintext_regex_lines.sample: $0d$$0a$This is the first sentence. And this is the second one.$0d$$0a$Second paragraph. Each one ends at the line-break.$0d$$0a$$0d$$0a$Third paragraph.$0d$$0a$And the last paragraph may have no line-break.

okf_plaintext_regex_paragraphs.sample: $0d$$0a$This is the first sentence. And this is the second one.$0d$$0a$Second paragraph. Each one ends at the line-break.$0d$$0a$$0d$$0a$Third paragraph.$0d$$0a$And the last paragraph may have no line-break.

doc.overview: The Plain Text filter processes text files encoded in ANSI, Unicode, UTF-8, and UTF-16 with automatic Byte-Order-Mark detection. It provides three extraction modes: **line-based** (`okf_baseplaintext`), **paragraph-based** (`okf_paraplaintext`), and **regex-based** (`okf_regexplaintext`), plus a **spliced lines** variant (`okf_splicedlines`) for source code with line-continuation characters. Each mode shares common parser and inline code settings but exposes different extraction parameters.

extraction.createPlaceholders.help: Creates an inline code placeholder at each position where a splicer character was removed during line merging. This preserves the original line break positions in the translatable content, allowing translators to see where breaks occurred.
notes: Only active in the spliced lines sub-filter (`okf_splicedlines`).

extraction.extractParagraphs.help:
Groups consecutive non-empty lines into paragraph text units, separated by one or more blank lines. Each paragraph becomes a single translatable unit with internal line breaks handled according to `wrapMode`.

When disabled, each line becomes its own text unit.
notes: Only available in the paragraph-based sub-filter (`okf_paraplaintext`).

extraction.regexOptions.help:
Bitmask of `java.util.regex.Pattern` flags to apply to the extraction regex. Common flags:

- **8** = `MULTILINE` — `^` and `$` match line boundaries (not just start/end of input)
- **32** = `DOTALL` — `.` matches any character including newline
- **40** = `MULTILINE | DOTALL` (8 + 32)
extraction.regexOptions.values: Integer bitmask combining `java.util.regex.Pattern` flag constants: `CASE_INSENSITIVE` (2), `MULTILINE` (8), `DOTALL` (32), etc.
examples: `8` — MULTILINE: for line-by-line extraction
`40` — MULTILINE + DOTALL: for paragraph extraction across line boundaries

extraction.rule.help:
Java regular expression used to extract translatable text. The filter applies this regex to the input and creates text units from the matches. Use capturing groups to isolate the translatable portion, then set `sourceGroup` to the group index.

The regex is applied with the flags specified in `regexOptions`.
extraction.rule.values: Java `java.util.regex.Pattern` syntax. Use capturing groups `()` to isolate translatable text.
examples: `(^(?=.+))(.*?)$` — extract each non-empty line (group 2 = content, with MULTILINE flag)

`(
*)(.*?)(

|\Z)` — extract paragraphs separated by blank lines (group 2 = paragraph text, with MULTILINE + DOTALL flags)

extraction.sample.help: Sample text used to test the extraction regex interactively. Extracted matches are shown enclosed in square brackets below the sample. This is for preview/testing only and does not affect processing.

extraction.sourceGroup.help:
The 1-based index of the capturing group in the extraction regex whose match contains the translatable text. Group 0 is the entire match.

For example, with regex `(prefix)(content)(suffix)`, set to `2` to extract only the `content` group.
extraction.sourceGroup.values: Integer index of a capturing group defined in the extraction `rule`. Must be ≥ 0.

extraction.splicer.help:
The character that indicates a line continues on the next line (line-continuation character). When found at the end of a line, the filter merges that line with the following one into a single text unit.

Common splicers: `\` (backslash, used in C/C++/shell), `_` (underscore, used in VB).
extraction.splicer.values: A single character. Common values: `\` (backslash), `_` (underscore), or any custom character.
notes: Only active in the spliced lines sub-filter (`okf_splicedlines`).
examples: `\` — backslash splicer for C/shell continuation lines
`_` — underscore splicer for Visual Basic continuation lines

inlineCodes.enabled.help: Activates regex-based detection of inline codes within extracted text. Any text matching the defined patterns will be converted to inline code placeholders, protecting them from translation.

inlineCodes.rules.help:
Define regex patterns to identify inline codes (placeholders, format specifiers, escape sequences, etc.) within translatable text. Each pattern match is converted to an inline code placeholder.

Default rules typically match C-style format specifiers (`%d`, `%s`) and escape sequences (`\n`, `\t`).
examples: `(\\r\\n)|\\a|\\b|\\f|\\n|\\r|\\t|\\v` — C escape sequences

parser.preserveWS.help: Preserves original whitespace in extracted text units. Typically enabled for paragraph and regex modes where whitespace structure matters.

parser.trimLeading.help: Strips leading spaces and tabs from each extracted text unit. Useful for indented source files where leading whitespace is not part of the translatable content.
notes: For CSV-type usage, this works together with the CSV "Exclude leading/trailing white spaces" option.

parser.trimTrailing.help: Strips trailing spaces and tabs from each extracted text unit. Commonly used to clean up lines with trailing whitespace before translation.
notes: For CSV-type usage, this works together with the CSV "Exclude leading/trailing white spaces" option.

parser.unescapeSource.help: Converts escape sequences (`\t`, `\n`, `\\`, `\uXXXX`) in the source text into their corresponding characters before extraction. Disable if the file contains literal backslash sequences that should be preserved as-is.

parser.wrapMode.help:
Controls how multiple lines are combined into a single text unit when extracting multi-line content:

- **NONE** — Lines are separated by `\n` line feeds (preserves original line structure)
- **SPACES** — Lines are unwrapped and merged with a space between them (useful for reflowable text)
- **PLACEHOLDERS** — Lines are joined with an inline code placeholder representing the original line break (allows translators to reposition breaks)
parser.wrapMode.values: `NONE` = separate with line feeds, `SPACES` = unwrap/merge with spaces, `PLACEHOLDERS` = inline code placeholders for line breaks

processingNotes: The filter auto-detects Unicode Byte-Order-Mark (BOM) to determine encoding. If no BOM is found, the configured default encoding is used.
For UTF-8 output: a BOM is written only if the input was UTF-8 *and* had a BOM. Converting from non-UTF-8 to UTF-8 produces no BOM.
Output line-break type preserves the original input's line-break convention (CR, LF, or CRLF).
The regex-based sub-filter (`okf_regexplaintext`) detects a wider range of linebreaks at the cost of lower speed and higher memory usage compared to the line-based sub-filter.

examples.title: Line-by-line extraction with trailing trim
examples.description: Uses the base plain text filter to extract each line as a separate text unit, trimming trailing whitespace.

examples.title: Paragraph extraction
examples.description: Groups consecutive non-empty lines into paragraph text units. Paragraphs are separated by one or more blank lines.
examples.input:
```text
This is the first sentence.
And this continues the paragraph.

This is a new paragraph.
```
examples.output:
```text
Text Unit 1: "This is the first sentence.\nAnd this continues the paragraph."
Text Unit 2: "This is a new paragraph."
```

examples.title: Regex extraction by line
examples.description: Uses a regex rule with MULTILINE flag to extract each non-empty line. Group 2 captures the line content.

examples.title: Spliced lines with backslash continuation
examples.description: Merges lines ending with backslash into single text units, inserting inline code placeholders where splicers were removed.
examples.input:
```text
This line continues \
on the next line.
This is separate.
```
examples.output:
```text
Text Unit 1: "This line continues <x/>on the next line."
Text Unit 2: "This is separate."
```

okf_plaintext_regex_lines.sample: $0d$$0a$This is the first sentence. And this is the second one.$0d$$0a$Second paragraph. Each one ends at the line-break.$0d$$0a$$0d$$0a$Third paragraph.$0d$$0a$And the last paragraph may have no line-break.

okf_plaintext_regex_paragraphs.sample: $0d$$0a$This is the first sentence. And this is the second one.$0d$$0a$Second paragraph. Each one ends at the line-break.$0d$$0a$$0d$$0a$Third paragraph.$0d$$0a$And the last paragraph may have no line-break.

entries.description: Extract translatable text from OpenDocument Format files (ODT, ODS, ODP, ODG).

doc.overview:
ODF (OpenDocument Format) is the OASIS standard used by LibreOffice,
OpenOffice, and compatible suites. neokapi handles the text (`.odt`),
spreadsheet (`.ods`), presentation (`.odp`), and graphics (`.odg`) variants,
auto-detecting the variant from the package's `mimetype` entry. Each file is
a ZIP archive containing a `content.xml` payload plus styles and metadata
sub-documents; the reader extracts translatable text from the content while
preserving the surrounding XML and the package structure as skeleton.

neokapi reads and writes ODF, round-tripping the archive so the translated
output remains a valid OpenDocument file. By default it extracts
presentation notes but skips hidden content — matching the upstream Okapi
OpenOffice filter behaviour. The native reader applies these defaults but
does not expose them as a configurable JSON schema in this reference.
limitations: Hidden content is not extracted by default.
Extraction toggles match the upstream Okapi OpenOffice filter but are not surfaced as configurable schema properties in this reference.
processingNotes: The ODF variant (text, spreadsheet, presentation, graphics) is auto-detected from the package `mimetype` entry.
Presentation notes are extracted by default; the surrounding XML and package structure are preserved as skeleton.
Each sub-document inside the ZIP archive is processed independently.

doc.overview:
The OpenOffice filter processes OpenDocument packages — the ZIP archives produced by OpenOffice.org and LibreOffice: text (`.odt`), spreadsheet (`.ods`), presentation (`.odp`), graphics (`.odg`), and their template variants. It is the package-level companion to the ODF filter: it opens the ZIP, walks each embedded sub-document (`content.xml`, `meta.xml`, `styles.xml`), and delegates the inner OpenDocument XML to the ODF filter for extraction. The translated sub-documents are re-zipped so the output is a valid OpenDocument package.

Use this configuration when the input is a complete `.odt`/`.ods`/`.odp`/`.odg` file. To process a raw, unpackaged OpenDocument XML stream directly, use the ODF filter instead. Both share the same parameters, so the extraction and output options behave identically.

extractMetadata.help: Extracts translatable metadata from `meta.xml` within the ODF package (e.g., document title, subject, keywords).

extractNotes.description: Extract the content of annotation elements as translatable text
extractNotes.help:
Extracts the content of `<office:annotation>` elements (notes/comments) as translatable text.

When disabled, annotations are skipped entirely during extraction.
notes: **Known issue:** This option may not take effect — notes may be extracted regardless of the setting.

extractReferences.description: Extract the content of bookmark reference elements as translatable text
extractReferences.help:
Extracts the content of `<text:bookmark-ref>` elements. These elements contain a **copy** of the referenced text that is automatically updated by OpenOffice when the document is refreshed.

Any translation of referenced text will be **overwritten** the next time the document updates its references. However, enabling this can be useful to provide context — the referenced text appears inline within the segment where it is inserted.
notes: **Known issue:** This option may not take effect — references may be extracted regardless of the setting.
Translations of bookmark references will be lost when OpenOffice updates the document, since the reference content is regenerated from the referent.

extraction.encodeCharacterEntityReferenc.help: Writer-side option affecting only the read-only inline elements that carry encodable glyphs — `text:page-number` and `text:bookmark-ref`. When enabled (the default), the character data inside those elements is run through the XML encoder, so reserved characters such as `&`, `<`, and `>` are written as character entity references. When disabled, that text is passed through literally. Other elements are unaffected. Behaviour is identical to the ODF filter, since the package filter delegates inner-XML extraction to it.

limitations: Input must be a ZIP-packaged OpenDocument file; raw XML streams should be processed with the ODF filter.
processingNotes: Each entry in the ZIP package is processed as a separate sub-document; in XLIFF output each becomes its own `<file>`.
Typically only `content.xml` yields extracted text; the other sub-documents are often empty.
The translated sub-documents are repackaged into a ZIP archive on output.

examples.title: Keep special glyphs literal in page-number and reference text
examples.description: Disable entity encoding so character data inside the read-only inline elements that carry glyphs (page numbers, bookmark references) is passed through verbatim rather than escaped to character entity references on output.

ignoreSoftHyphenTag.title: Ignore soft hyphen tags
ignoreSoftHyphenTag.description: Ignore soft hyphen tags in the document.

includeHighlightColors.title: Include highlight colors
includeHighlightColors.description: If non-empty, only extract text with these highlight colors.

includeStyles.title: Include styles
includeStyles.description: If non-empty, only extract text with these styles.

tabAsCharacter.title: Add tab as character
tabAsCharacter.description: Treat tab elements as tab characters instead of placeholder spans.

translateComments.title: Translate comments
translateComments.description: Extract comment text from Word documents.

translateDocProperties.title: Translate document properties
translateDocProperties.description: Extract title, subject, keywords from document properties.

translateFootnotes.title: Translate footnotes and endnotes
translateFootnotes.description: Extract footnotes and endnotes from Word documents.

translateHeadersFooters.title: Translate headers and footers
translateHeadersFooters.description: Extract text from headers and footers in Word documents.

translateHiddenSlides.title: Translate hidden slides
translateHiddenSlides.description: Extract content from hidden slides in PowerPoint.

translateHiddenText.title: Translate hidden text
translateHiddenText.description: Extract text with the vanish (hidden) property in Word documents.

translateSharedStrings.title: Translate shared strings
translateSharedStrings.description: Extract shared strings from Excel workbooks.

translateSheetNames.title: Translate sheet names
translateSheetNames.description: Extract sheet names in Excel workbooks.

translateSlideMasters.title: Translate master slides

useCodeFinder.title: Enable Inline Code Detection
useCodeFinder.description: Enable pattern-based detection of inline codes (placeholders, tags, etc.).

doc.overview:
The OpenXML format handles Microsoft Office Open XML documents — Word
(`.docx`), Excel (`.xlsx`), and PowerPoint (`.pptx`). All three share a
ZIP-and-XML package, so one filter serves them and auto-detects the
variant from the package contents, per ECMA-376 / ISO/IEC 29500.

Extraction is selective: by default the reader pulls the main body text
plus the parts that are normally user-visible — document properties,
headers and footers, footnotes, comments, and hyperlink text in Word;
shared strings in Excel; and speaker notes in PowerPoint — while leaving
hidden, structural, or rarely translated content out unless you opt in.
A set of style and colour filters lets you include or exclude runs by
paragraph/character style or highlight colour. This mirrors Okapi's
OpenXML filter, including its default of accepting tracked changes
before extraction.

aggressiveCleanup.description: Strip revision ids and proofing noise before merging runs.
aggressiveCleanup.help:
When enabled (the default), noise such as revision ids (`w:rsid*`),
proofing-error markers, and last-rendered page breaks is stripped
before adjacent runs are merged, which yields cleaner, fewer inline
codes. Disable it to keep that markup.

automaticallyAcceptRevisions.description: Accept tracked changes before extracting text.
automaticallyAcceptRevisions.help:
When enabled (the default, matching Okapi), tracked changes are
accepted before extraction: inserted runs are kept and deleted runs
are dropped; table rows marked `<w:trPr><w:del/>` (ECMA-376
§17.13.5.13) are removed entirely and rows marked
`<w:trPr><w:ins/>` (§17.13.5.16) are kept. Disable it to extract the
document as authored, with revision markup intact.

codeFinderRules.description: Regular-expression patterns that match inline codes in text.
codeFinderRules.help:
A list of regular expressions; each match within translatable text is
protected as an inline code rather than translated. Only takes effect
when `useCodeFinder` is enabled.
examples: `\{[0-9]+\}` — protect numbered placeholders like `{0}`

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for the rules to take effect

complexFieldDefinitionsToExtract.description: Field-instruction prefixes whose cached display text is extracted.
complexFieldDefinitionsToExtract.help:
A list of complex-field instruction prefixes whose cached display
text is extracted for translation. The default is `["HYPERLINK"]`,
matching Okapi — without it the user-visible anchor text of HYPERLINK
fields would round-trip in the source language. Add prefixes such as
`REF` to translate the display text of other field types.
examples: `["HYPERLINK", "REF"]`

excludeColors.description: Font colours to exclude from extraction.
excludeColors.help:
A list of font colours (hex RGB) whose runs are skipped. Use it to
leave text in a particular colour (for example, internal annotations)
untranslated.
excludeColors.values: Array of hex-RGB strings (e.g. `FF0000` for red)

excludeHighlightColors.description: Highlight colours to exclude from extraction.
excludeHighlightColors.help:
A list of highlight colour names whose runs are skipped. Use it to
leave highlighted text untranslated.
examples: `["yellow"]`

excludeStyles.description: Paragraph or character styles to exclude from extraction.
excludeStyles.help:
A list of paragraph or character style names whose text is skipped.
Use it to leave code, caption, or reference styles untranslated.
examples: `["Code", "Caption"]`

excludedColumns.description: Column letters to exclude from extraction.
excludedColumns.help:
A list of column letters whose cells are skipped. Use it to leave
identifier or numeric columns untranslated.

excludedSheets.description: Worksheet names to exclude from extraction.
excludedSheets.help:
A list of worksheet names whose content is skipped entirely. Use it
to leave reference or data sheets untranslated.
examples: `["Data", "Lookups"]`

extractRunFontsInfo.description: Emit font metadata as annotations on blocks.
extractRunFontsInfo.help:
When enabled, font information for each run is attached to the
extracted block as annotations. When disabled (the default), no font
metadata is emitted.

fontMappings.description: Map font names to script groups.
fontMappings.help:
A map from font name to script group, used to associate runs with a
script for downstream handling. Empty by default.
fontMappings.values: Object mapping font name to script-group code
examples: `{"MS Gothic": "ja"}`

ignoreSoftHyphenTag.description: Ignore soft-hyphen tags in the document.
ignoreSoftHyphenTag.help:
When enabled, soft-hyphen tags are ignored during extraction. When
disabled (the default), they are retained.

includeHighlightColors.description: Restrict extraction to text with these highlight colours.
includeHighlightColors.help:
When non-empty, only runs with one of the listed highlight colours
are extracted; all other text is skipped. Leave it empty (the
default) to apply no highlight-based restriction.
includeHighlightColors.values: Array of highlight-colour names (default: no restriction)
examples: `["green"]`

includeStyles.description: Restrict extraction to text in these styles.
includeStyles.values: Array of style-name strings (default: no restriction)
examples: `["Heading 1", "Normal"]`

includedSlides.description: Restrict extraction to specific slide numbers.
includedSlides.help:
When non-empty, only the listed slide numbers (1-based) are
extracted; all other slides are skipped. Leave it empty (the default)
to extract every slide.
includedSlides.values: Array of 1-based slide numbers (default: all slides)

lineSeparatorReplacement.description: The replacement string for line-separator characters.
lineSeparatorReplacement.help:
The string substituted for the Unicode line separator when
`replaceLineSeparator` is enabled. The default is a newline (`\n`).

dependsOn.condition: applied only when line-separator replacement is enabled

replaceLineSeparator.description: Replace the Unicode line separator (U+2028) in output.
replaceLineSeparator.help:
When enabled, the Unicode line-separator character (U+2028) is
replaced in the output by `lineSeparatorReplacement`. When disabled
(the default), it is left as-is.

replaceNoBreakHyphenTag.description: Replace no-break hyphen tags with the non-breaking hyphen character.
replaceNoBreakHyphenTag.help:
When enabled, no-break hyphen tags are replaced with the actual
non-breaking hyphen character in the text. When disabled (the
default), the tag representation is kept.

tabAsCharacter.description: Treat tab elements as tab characters instead of placeholder spans.
tabAsCharacter.help:
When enabled, `<w:tab/>` elements are extracted as literal tab
characters in the text. When disabled (the default), they are
represented as inline placeholder spans.

translateCharts.help:
When enabled, strings inside embedded charts (titles, axis labels,
data labels) are extracted. When disabled (the default), chart text
is skipped.

translateComments.description: Extract comment text from Word documents.
translateComments.help:
When enabled (the default), the text of Word review comments is
extracted. This matches Okapi, which treats `word/comments.xml` as a
styled-text part alongside the main document. Disable it to leave
comments untranslated.

translateDiagrams.help:
When enabled, the text held in SmartArt diagram data is extracted.
When disabled (the default), diagram text is skipped.

translateDocProperties.help:
When enabled (the default), the metadata in `docProps/core.xml` —
title, subject, keywords — is extracted for translation. Disable it
to leave document properties untouched.

translateFootnotes.help:
When enabled (the default), footnote and endnote text in Word
documents is extracted. Disable it to skip them.

translateHeadersFooters.description: Extract text from headers and footers.
translateHeadersFooters.help:
When enabled (the default), header and footer text in Word documents
is extracted. Disable it to leave headers and footers untranslated.

translateHiddenSlides.help:
When enabled, the content of slides marked hidden is extracted. When
disabled (the default), hidden slides are skipped.

translateHiddenText.description: Extract text marked with the hidden (vanish) property.
translateHiddenText.help:
When enabled, runs carrying the `w:vanish` (hidden) property are
extracted. When disabled (the default), hidden text is skipped.

translateHyperlinks.description: Extract the display text of hyperlinks.
translateHyperlinks.help:
When enabled (the default), the visible anchor text of hyperlinks is
extracted for translation. Disable it to leave hyperlink text
untouched. (The HYPERLINK field's cached display text is governed
separately by `complexFieldDefinitionsToExtract`.)

translateSharedStrings.description: Extract shared strings from Excel workbooks.
translateSharedStrings.help:
When enabled (the default), the cell text held in the workbook's
shared-strings table is extracted — this is where most Excel text
lives. Disable it to skip cell content.

translateSheetNames.help:
When enabled, worksheet names are extracted for translation. When
disabled (the default), sheet names are left as-is.

translateSlideMasters.help:
When enabled, text held on slide masters and layouts is extracted.
When disabled (the default), master-slide text is skipped.

translateSlideNotes.description: Extract speaker notes from PowerPoint presentations.
translateSlideNotes.help:
When enabled (the default), the speaker-notes text of slides is
extracted. Disable it to leave notes untranslated.

useCodeFinder.description: Detect inline codes inside text using regular expressions.
useCodeFinder.help:
Enables pattern-based detection of inline codes (placeholders,
variables, tags) within extracted text, using the patterns in
`codeFinderRules`. When disabled (the default), no pattern-based
inline codes are detected.

limitations: One filter covers Word, Excel, and PowerPoint; some options apply only to the relevant variant (for example, the slide options affect PowerPoint only).
processingNotes: The document variant (Word / Excel / PowerPoint) is auto-detected from the package contents.
The writer is faithful by default — source run properties are preserved inline rather than rewritten into synthesised styles.

examples.description: Include comments, hidden text, and slide masters that are off by default.

examples.title: Limit an Excel workbook to selected sheets
examples.description: Skip data and lookup sheets during extraction.

translateHeadersFooters.description: Extract text from headers and footers in Word documents.

translateHidden.title: Translate hidden cells
translateHidden.description: Extract text from hidden cells in Excel.

translateSheetNames.title: Translate sheet names
translateSheetNames.description: Extract sheet names in Excel workbooks.

translateDrawings.title: Translate drawings
translateDrawings.description: Extract text from shapes and drawings in Excel.

translateMasters.title: Translate master slides
translateMasters.description: Extract text from slide masters in PowerPoint.

translateNotes.title: Translate slide notes
translateNotes.description: Extract speaker notes in PowerPoint presentations.

doc.overview: Processes Microsoft Office documents from Office 2007 and later, including DOCX (Word), XLSX (Excel), and PPTX (PowerPoint). These documents use the OpenXML (ZIP-based) format, as opposed to the binary formats used by pre-2007 versions of Office. The filter provides format-specific extraction options for each document type, with support for comments, hidden content, document properties, charts, diagrams, and color-based exclusion.

excel.excludeColors.help: Excludes cells with a foreground or background color matching any selected colors from translation. The named colors available in the UI correspond to the **standard Excel 2010 color palette**.
notes: Colors not listed in the UI can be excluded by modifying the `tsExcelExcludedColors` section of the configuration file with RGB hex values in `RRGGBB` format.
examples:
To exclude Pantone 292 (#69b3e7), add to config:
```
tsExcelExcludedColors.i=1
ccc0=69b3e7
```

excel.preserveStylesInTargetColumns.help: Preserves the existing cell styles in target columns rather than overwriting them with source column styles.

excel.translateCellsCopied.help: Cell data are copied on extraction to allow **contextualised and independent translations** of each cell.

excel.translateDiagramData.help: Exposes SmartArt and other diagram data in Excel for translation.

excel.translateDrawings.help: Exposes text content from shapes and drawings in Excel for translation.

excel.translateHidden.help: Exposes hidden rows and columns in Excel spreadsheets for translation.

excel.translateSheetNames.help: Exposes worksheet tab names in Excel workbooks for translation.

extractExcelCellsExplicitlySpecified.description: Only extract cells explicitly specified in the Worksheet Configurations.
extractExcelCellsExplicitlySpecified.help: Only cells specified in the **Worksheet Configurations** are extracted. The explicitly mentioned source and target columns are eligible for this handling.

extractExcelSourceAndTargetColumnsJoined.description: Join source and target columns in a row during extraction rather than extracting them separately.
extractExcelSourceAndTargetColumnsJoined.help: Joins source and target columns (cells in a row) during extraction rather than extracting them separately.

extractExcelWorksheetsExplicitlySpecifie.description: Only extract worksheets whose names match patterns defined in the Worksheet Configurations.
extractExcelWorksheetsExplicitlySpecifie.help: Only worksheets whose names match a pattern in the **Worksheet Configurations** are extracted. All other worksheets are ignored.

general.addLineSeparator.help: Adds line separator characters (instead of inline codes) for line breaks within paragraphs.

general.addTabAsCharacter.help: Adds tab characters (instead of inline codes) for tabs within text runs.

general.aggressiveCleanup.help: Strips additional formatting tags related to text spacing. Improves filtering quality when Office documents were **converted from other formats** (particularly PDF), where imperfect conversion adds extra formatting noise.

general.allowEmptyTargets.help: Allows empty target segments during merge. Without this, empty targets may be skipped or filled with source text.

general.extractExternalHyperlinks.help: Exposes external hyperlink URLs for translation.

general.fileType.help: Identifies the type of Office document being processed. Normally set automatically based on file extension. Only needs manual configuration in special cases.
general.fileType.values: `MSWORD` — Word documents, `MSEXCEL` — Excel spreadsheets, `MSWORDDOCPROPERTIES` — Word doc properties only, `MSPOWERPOINTCOMMENTS` — PowerPoint comments only.

general.ignoreSoftHyphenTag.help: Ignores soft hyphen tags in the document, removing them from extracted content.

general.inExcludeHighlightMode.help:
Controls the highlight-color-based include/exclude mechanism.
- **Include**: only text with specified highlight colors is extracted
- **Exclude**: all content except text with specified highlight colors is extracted

Default: Exclude with no colors selected (all visible content extracted).
notes: Text excluded via highlight colors is treated as **hidden** — enabling "Translate Hidden" options will still extract it.
Starting in 1.48.0, this option also applies to PowerPoint content in addition to Word.

general.inExcludeMode.help:
Controls the style-based include/exclude mechanism.
- **Exclude** mode: text with specified styles is skipped
- **Include** mode: only text with specified styles is extracted

general.lineSeparatorReplacement.help: The string used to replace line separator elements in the output.
general.lineSeparatorReplacement.values: A replacement string. Default is `\n` (newline character).

general.maxAttributeSize.help: Maximum size in bytes for XML attributes in the document. Increase if processing documents with very large embedded data in attributes.
general.maxAttributeSize.values: Integer value in bytes. Default: `4194304` (4 MB).

general.replaceNoBreakHyphenTag.help: Replaces no-break hyphen tags with actual no-break hyphen characters.

ignoreWhitespaceStyles.description: Ignore whitespace character formatting and treat it as equivalent to consequential styles during aggressive tag cleanup.
ignoreWhitespaceStyles.help: Whitespace character styles (formatting) are ignored and considered equal to the consequential ones during tag cleanup.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this option to take effect

powerpoint.ignorePlaceholdersInMasters.help: Ignores placeholder elements in slide masters, preventing their content from being extracted for translation.

powerpoint.includedSlideNumbersOnly.help: Restricts extraction to only specific slide numbers rather than all slides in the presentation.

powerpoint.reorderCharts.help: Reorders chart parts to be placed **after the related slide/layout/master part** and after their diagram data parts.

powerpoint.reorderComments.help: Reorders comment parts to be placed **after the related slide part** and after its note parts.

powerpoint.reorderDiagramData.help: Reorders diagram data parts to be placed **after the related slide/layout/master part** and after their relationship parts.

powerpoint.reorderDocProperties.help: Reorders document properties to be placed **after the root relationship part** (`_rels/.rels`).

powerpoint.reorderNotes.help: Reorders note parts to be placed **after the related slide part** and after its chart parts. Useful for controlling the order of parts in the output ZIP archive.

powerpoint.reorderNotesAndComments.help: Reorders both notes and comments together, placing them after their related slide parts.

powerpoint.reorderRelationships.help: Reorders relationship parts to be placed **after the related slide/layout/master part**.

powerpoint.translateCharts.help: Exposes chart text content in PowerPoint for translation.

powerpoint.translateComments.help: Exposes slide comments for translation. Both this option **and** the General Options **Translate Comments** must be enabled.
notes: Currently coupled with the General Options comment setting — both must be checked. These will be separated in a future release.

dependsOn.condition: currently coupled — both must be enabled for PowerPoint comments to be extracted

powerpoint.translateDiagramData.help: Exposes SmartArt and other diagram data in PowerPoint for translation.

powerpoint.translateDocProperties.help: Exposes PowerPoint document properties (title, subject, creator, description, category, keywords, content status) for translation. Both this option **and** the General Options **Translate Document Properties** must be enabled.
notes: Currently coupled with the General Options doc properties setting — both must be checked. These will be separated in a future release.

dependsOn.condition: currently coupled — both must be enabled for PowerPoint doc properties to be extracted

powerpoint.translateHidden.help: Exposes hidden slides in PowerPoint presentations for translation.

powerpoint.translateMasters.help: Exposes slide masters and notes masters for translation. Also exposes content from **layouts currently in use** by at least one slide.

powerpoint.translateNotes.help: Exposes speaker notes attached to slides for translation.

preserveAsciiAndHighAnsiFontCategoriesOn.description: Preserve ASCII and HighAnsi run font categories when merging consequential runs.
preserveAsciiAndHighAnsiFontCategoriesOn.help: Preserves the ASCII and HighAnsi run font categories when merging consequential runs. Relevant when aggressive cleanup is used and font category preservation matters for output fidelity.

removeEmbeddedExcel.description: Remove the embedded Excel data package and its references from chart parts when extracting cached chart values.
removeEmbeddedExcel.help: Removes the embedded Excel package from the document, along with any references to it in chart parts and related relationships.

dependsOn.condition: at least one of cachedChartStrings or cachedChartNumbers must be enabled
at least one of cachedChartStrings or cachedChartNumbers must be enabled

translatePowerpointCachedChartNumbers.description: Expose cached chart numbers and format codes in PowerPoint for translation.
translatePowerpointCachedChartNumbers.help: Exposes cached chart numbers and format codes in PowerPoint for translation.

translatePowerpointCachedChartStrings.description: Expose pre-rendered chart string values stored in PowerPoint chart XML for translation.
translatePowerpointCachedChartStrings.help: Exposes cached chart string values in PowerPoint for translation. These are the pre-rendered text values stored in chart XML.

translatePowerpointGraphicDescription.description: Expose @descr attributes of drawings and word art in PowerPoint presentations for translation.
translatePowerpointGraphicDescription.help: Exposes `@descr` attribute values associated with drawings and word art in PowerPoint for translation.

translatePowerpointGraphicName.description: Expose @name attributes of drawings and word art in PowerPoint presentations for translation.
translatePowerpointGraphicName.help: Exposes `@name` attribute values associated with drawings and word art in PowerPoint for translation.

translateWordGraphicDescription.description: Expose @descr attributes of drawings and word art in Word documents for translation.
translateWordGraphicDescription.help: Exposes `@descr` attribute values associated with drawings and word art in Word documents for translation.

translateWordGraphicName.description: Expose @name attributes of drawings and word art in Word documents for translation.
translateWordGraphicName.help: Exposes `@name` attribute values associated with drawings and word art in Word documents for translation.

treatExcelSourceColumnStylesForExclusion.description: Use source column cell styles when evaluating color-based exclusion rules in Excel.
treatExcelSourceColumnStylesForExclusion.help: Applies source column cell styles when evaluating color-based exclusion rules.

word.allowStyleOptimisation.help: Allows optimization of styles — common formatting of all runs in a paragraph is moved to the styles part of the document. This can simplify the tag structure of extracted segments.

word.automaticallyAcceptRevisions.help: Automatically accepts tracked changes (revisions) in Word documents before processing.

word.excludeColors.help: Enables font colour-based exclusion for Word content. Text using any selected colours will not be exposed for translation.

word.excludeGraphicMetaData.help: When enabled, `@name` and `@descr` attribute values associated with drawings and word art are **not** exposed for translation. The individual `translateWordGraphicName` and `translateWordGraphicDescription` options provide finer-grained control.

word.fontColorsMax.help: Font colours are ignored up to this threshold value. When empty, defaults to white.
word.fontColorsMax.values: Accepts **preset colour names** (`white`, `White`) or **RGB hex strings** (`FFFFFF`). Example: `FFFFFF` means threshold ends at white.
examples: `white` or `FFFFFF` — threshold at white
`Black` or `000000` — threshold at black

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for thresholds to apply

word.fontColorsMin.help: Font colours are ignored starting from this threshold value. When empty, defaults to white.
word.fontColorsMin.values: Accepts **preset colour names** (`black`, `Black`) or **RGB hex strings** (`000000`). Example: `000000` means threshold starts at black.
examples: `black` or `000000` — threshold at black
`White` or `FFFFFF` — threshold at white

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for thresholds to apply

word.ignoreFontColors.help: Ignores font colours during processing. When combined with **Clean Tags Aggressively** and empty ignorance thresholds, font colour run properties are **permanently removed from the document structure** on filtering — meaning font colour information will also be absent on merge.
notes: If both `aggressiveCleanup` and this option are enabled with empty thresholds, font colour properties are removed from the document. This removal is irreversible on merge.

dependsOn.condition: when both are enabled with empty thresholds, font colour properties are permanently removed

word.translateComments.help: Exposes document comments in Word for translation.

word.translateDocProperties.help: Exposes the following document properties for translation: **title**, **subject**, **creator**, **description**, **category**, **keywords**, **content status**.

word.translateHeadersFooters.help: Exposes header and footer content from Word documents for translation.

word.translateHidden.help: Exposes hidden text in Word documents for translation. Also extracts text that was excluded via highlight color settings when this is enabled.
notes: Text excluded using the highlight color mechanism is treated as hidden — this option will extract it.

word.translateNumberingLevelText.help: Exposes numbering-level text (e.g., list bullet/number format strings) for translation.

limitations: Various known issues tracked in the [Okapi Bitbucket issue tracker](https://bitbucket.org/okapiframework/okapi/issues?status=new&title=~OpenXML).
Pre-2007 binary Office formats (`.doc`, `.xls`, `.ppt`) are **not** supported — only OpenXML (`.docx`, `.xlsx`, `.pptx`, etc.).
PowerPoint `translateComments` and `translateDocProperties` are currently coupled with the General Options equivalents — both must be enabled to extract these elements.
processingNotes: The filter processes OpenXML (ZIP-based) Office documents by extracting and processing individual XML parts within the archive.
The highlight-color include/exclude mechanism treats excluded text as hidden, so "Translate Hidden" options can override highlight-based exclusions.
Starting in 1.48.0, Word highlight color exclusion/inclusion also applies to PowerPoint content.
Excel worksheet configurations use `java.util.regex.Pattern` syntax for name patterns, and ALPHA-26 notation (A, B, C…) for column references.
Colors in Excel exclusion support both named UI colors (standard Excel 2010 palette) and custom RGB hex values (`RRGGBB` format) via manual config file editing.

examples.title: Excel worksheet configuration with source/target columns
examples.description: Configure extraction to translate column A into column B, excluding header rows 1-2 and columns C-D from a specific worksheet named `Sheet1`.

examples.title: Excel worksheet configuration with metadata
examples.description: Configure extraction to translate columns A and B, treat column D as row-level metadata, rows 1-2 as column metadata headers, exclude row 5, and skip column C entirely.

examples.title: Excluding custom Excel colors by RGB
examples.description: Exclude a specific color (Pantone 292 / #69b3e7) not available in the standard UI palette by editing the configuration file directly.

entries.description: Read and write plain text split into paragraphs at blank lines.

doc.overview:
Paragraph plain text treats a `.txt` document as a sequence of
paragraphs separated by blank lines (a double newline). Each
paragraph becomes one translatable block; the blank-line separators
between paragraphs are preserved as non-translatable structure.

This is the right reader when the natural translation unit is a whole
paragraph rather than a single line — prose, descriptions, or release
notes where a sentence may wrap across several physical lines. Line
breaks inside a paragraph are kept in the block text, so internal
wrapping survives the round-trip.
limitations: No sub-paragraph segmentation; the entire paragraph is a single block. Apply a segmentation tool downstream if sentence-level units are needed.
There are no configurable parameters; the paragraph boundary is always the blank line.
processingNotes: Paragraphs are split on a blank line (double newline); CRLF and LF endings are both recognised.
With a skeleton store active, original line endings and blank-line separators are preserved for byte-exact round-trips; leading and trailing blank lines round-trip as skeleton.

examples.title: Translate prose paragraphs
examples.description:
No configuration is required. Declare the format to extract each
blank-line-separated paragraph as one block.
