doc.overview: The Base Table Filter is the generic, delimiter-agnostic foundation of the table filter family. It treats input as line-oriented records and column-based fields, but does not itself split lines into cells — each concrete table format (CSV, TSV, fixed-width columns) supplies that splitting. Used directly, it reads the whole line as a single field, so it is normally configured through one of its specialisations rather than on its own. The column-role settings (`sourceColumns`, `targetColumns`, `commentColumns`, ID columns), header handling, and whitespace controls documented here are shared by every table configuration.

codeFinderRules.help: The inline-code detection rules applied to extracted cell text, in Okapi `#v1` rule format. The defaults match printf-style format specifiers and common backslash escape sequences. Only applied when `useCodeFinder` is enabled.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for these rules to apply

columnNamesLineNum.help:
The 1-based line number that holds the column captions (field names). When set to a line within the header area (before `valuesStartLineNum`), the filter reads that line to learn the column names. Those names are used to label comment metadata and, when a source or target column has not been assigned a locale, the column name is parsed as a locale code and used to set the pipeline's source/target locale.

Leave at `0` when the file has no column-name line.
notes: Lines are numbered from 1. For a CSV with a header on line 1 and data from line 2, set `columnNamesLineNum=1` and `valuesStartLineNum=2`.

columns.columnNamesLineNum.help: The 1-based line number that holds the column captions. The filter reads that line to learn column names, which label comment metadata and can be parsed as locale codes to set the pipeline source/target locale. Leave at `0` when the file has no column-name line.

columns.commentColumns.help: Comma-separated 1-based indices of columns whose values attach as notes (or metadata, see `references.exposeCommentsAsMetadata`) to the source column they annotate via `references.commentSourceRefs`, rather than being extracted as translatable text.

columns.detectColumnsMode.help: How the column count per row is decided: per-row (no detection), from the count of names on the column-names line, or a fixed `columns.numColumns`. A fixed count pads short rows with empty cells and truncates long ones.

columns.numColumns.help: The fixed number of columns expected per row. Consulted only when `columns.detectColumnsMode` is set to fixed-number.

dependsOn.condition: only active when set to 2 (fixed number)

columns.recordIdColumn.help: The 1-based index of the column whose value identifies the row. Combined with `references.sourceIdSuffixes` to synthesise an ID for source columns lacking an explicit ID cell.

columns.sendColumnsMode.help: Which columns of each data row are extracted: none, only the columns listed in the column-role settings, or all columns as source. `listed` mode is what enables bilingual extraction, IDs, and notes.

columns.sendHeaderMode.help: Which header-area lines are extracted as translatable text: none, the column-names line only, or all header lines.

columns.sourceColumns.help: Comma-separated 1-based indices of the columns extracted as source (translatable) text. Consulted in `listed` mode.

columns.sourceIdColumns.help: Comma-separated 1-based indices of columns whose values become the resource names (IDs) of source text units. Each is linked to its source column via `references.sourceIdSourceRefs`; an empty ID cell falls back to `columns.recordIdColumn` + `references.sourceIdSuffixes`.

columns.targetColumns.help: Comma-separated 1-based indices of columns holding existing target text. Each is tied to a source column via `references.targetSourceRefs` and a locale via `columns.targetLanguages`; supplying them makes the configuration bilingual.

columns.targetLanguages.help: Locale of each target column, aligned position-by-position with `columns.targetColumns`. A single value applies to all target columns; otherwise the pipeline target locale is used.

columns.valuesStartLineNum.help: The 1-based line number where data rows begin. Lines before it form the header area, handled per `columns.sendHeaderMode`.

commentColumns.help: The columns whose values are attached as notes (or, with `exposeCommentsAsMetadata`, as metadata) to a source text unit rather than extracted as translatable text. Each comment column is linked to the source column it annotates via `commentSourceRefs`. To have a comment translated instead, list its column under `sourceColumns`.

dependsOn.condition: maps each comment column to the source column it annotates

commentSourceRefs.help: Maps the columns in `commentColumns` to the source columns they annotate. Given as a comma-separated list of 1-based source-column indices, aligned position-by-position with `commentColumns`. The comment becomes a read-only note on the referenced source text unit.

detectColumnsMode.help: Controls how the filter decides how many columns each row has. With no detection, rows with differing field counts simply yield differing numbers of text units. With column-name detection, the column count is fixed to the number of names found on the column-names line. With a fixed number, every row is padded with empty cells or truncated to exactly `numColumns` cells.
detectColumnsMode.values: `0` = none (per-row), `1` = from the column-names line, `2` = fixed `numColumns` (default `0`)

dependsOn.condition: used only when set to 2 (fixed number)
used only when set to 1 (from column names)

exposeCommentsAsMetadata.help: When enabled, values drawn from `commentColumns` are attached to the source text unit as metadata (keyed by the column name) instead of as a translator note. Use it when downstream tooling consumes structured metadata rather than free-form notes.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when comment columns are defined

mergeAdjacentCodes.help: When multiple inline codes appear consecutively with no text between them, they are combined into a single placeholder code. This simplifies the translatable content by reducing the number of inline codes translators must handle. For example, `` would become a single placeholder rather than two separate codes.

moveLeadingAndTrailingCodesToSkeleton.help: Inline codes appearing at the very beginning or end of a segment are moved into the skeleton (non-translatable portion). This reduces clutter in the translatable text when boundary codes carry no translational significance. For example, if a segment starts with `` and ends with ``, those codes are moved to skeleton so translators see only the text content.

numColumns.help: The fixed number of columns expected per row. Only consulted when `detectColumnsMode` is set to fixed-number. Rows with fewer cells are padded with empty cells; rows with more cells have the extras dropped.

dependsOn.condition: only active when detectColumnsMode = 2 (fixed number)

parser.preserveWS.help: Inherited from the plain-text base. When enabled, whitespace inside extracted cell values is preserved; cell-edge trimming is governed separately by `parser.trimMode`, `parser.trimLeading`, and `parser.trimTrailing`.

parser.trimLeading.help: Removes leading spaces and tabs from each extracted cell value. Forced on whenever `parser.trimMode` is not none.

parser.trimMode.help: How leading/trailing whitespace is removed from cells: none, non-qualified fields only (matters only for qualifier-aware formats), or all fields. Any value other than none forces `parser.trimLeading` and `parser.trimTrailing` on.

parser.trimTrailing.help: Removes trailing spaces and tabs from each extracted cell value. Forced on whenever `parser.trimMode` is not none.

parser.unescapeSource.help: Inherited from the plain-text base. When enabled, `\t`, `\n`, `\\`, and `\uXXXX` escape sequences in extracted cell values are converted to the characters they represent.

parser.wrapMode.help: How a cell value spanning multiple physical lines is represented: `NONE` joins wrapped lines with a line feed, `SPACES` joins with a single space, `PLACEHOLDERS` inserts an inline placeholder at each internal break so wrapping can be reproduced on output.

preserveWS.help: Inherited from the plain-text base filter. When enabled, whitespace within extracted cell values is preserved as-is. When disabled, the filter is permitted to normalise whitespace. Cell-edge trimming is controlled separately by `trimMode`, `trimLeading`, and `trimTrailing`.

recordIdColumn.help: The 1-based index of the column whose value identifies the record (the row key). When set, that column's value is combined with the entries in `sourceIdSuffixes` to build the resource name (ID) for source columns that do not carry their own ID from `sourceIdColumns`.

references.commentSourceRefs.help: Maps each column in `columns.commentColumns` to the source column it annotates, as a comma-separated list of 1-based source-column indices aligned position-by-position. The comment becomes a read-only note on that source text unit.

references.exposeCommentsAsMetadata.help: When enabled, comment-column values attach to the source text unit as metadata keyed by column name instead of as translator notes. Use it when downstream tooling consumes structured metadata.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when comment columns are defined

references.sourceIdSourceRefs.help: Maps each column in `columns.sourceIdColumns` to the source column it names, as a comma-separated list of 1-based source-column indices aligned position-by-position.

references.sourceIdSuffixes.help: Suffixes appended to the `columns.recordIdColumn` value to build an ID for a source column that has no explicit ID cell. Aligned position-by-position with `columns.sourceColumns`.

references.targetSourceRefs.help: Maps each column in `columns.targetColumns` to the source column it translates, as a comma-separated list of 1-based source-column indices aligned position-by-position. A target whose reference cannot be resolved is sent to the skeleton.

sendColumnsMode.help: Controls which columns of each data row are extracted. With `none`, no cells are extracted. With `listed`, only the columns named in `sourceColumns`, `targetColumns`, `sourceIdColumns`, and `commentColumns` are processed — this is what enables bilingual extraction, IDs, and notes. With `all`, every cell in the row is extracted as source text and the column-role lists are ignored.
sendColumnsMode.values: `0` = none, `1` = listed columns only, `2` = all columns (default `1`)
notes: Bilingual extraction (source paired with target) is only available in `listed` mode with a non-empty `targetColumns`.

sendHeaderMode.help: Controls which header-area lines (those before `valuesStartLineNum`) are extracted as translatable text. With `0`, no header lines are extracted. With `1`, only the column-names line is extracted. With `2`, every header line is extracted; non-column-name header lines are sent whole rather than split into cells.
sendHeaderMode.values: `0` = none, `1` = column names only, `2` = all header lines (default `0`)

simplifierRules.description: Rules for simplifying inline code representation in extracted segments
simplifierRules.help:
Define rules for simplifying inline code representation in extracted text. Uses the Okapi simplifier rules syntax to merge or reduce inline codes into simpler placeholders.

This is edited via a dedicated rules editor widget.

sourceColumns.help: The columns whose cells are extracted as source (translatable) text, given as a comma-separated list of 1-based column indices. Only consulted when `sendColumnsMode` is `listed`. When empty in `listed` mode, no columns are treated as source.
examples: `1,3` — extract columns 1 and 3 as source text

sourceIdColumns.help: The columns whose values become the resource names (IDs) of the corresponding source text units, as a comma-separated list of 1-based indices. Each ID column is linked to the source column it names via `sourceIdSourceRefs`. When an ID cell is empty, the filter falls back to `recordIdColumn` + `sourceIdSuffixes`.

dependsOn.condition: maps each ID column to the source column it names

sourceIdSourceRefs.help: Maps the columns in `sourceIdColumns` to the source columns they identify. Given as a comma-separated list of 1-based source-column indices, aligned position-by-position with `sourceIdColumns`.

sourceIdSuffixes.help: Suffixes appended to the `recordIdColumn` value to synthesise an ID for a source column when that column has no explicit ID cell. The list is aligned position-by-position with `sourceColumns`, so each source column can receive a distinct suffix.

targetColumns.help: The columns that hold existing target (translated) text, as a comma-separated list of 1-based indices. Each target column is tied to a source column through `targetSourceRefs` and given a locale through `targetLanguages`. Supplying target columns makes the configuration bilingual.

dependsOn.condition: maps each target column to its source column
assigns the locale of each target column

targetLanguages.help: The locale of each target column, as a comma-separated list aligned position-by-position with `targetColumns`. A single value applies that locale to all target columns. When a target column has no locale here, the pipeline's target locale is used.
targetLanguages.values: Comma-separated BCP-47/locale codes, e.g. `fr` or `fr,it,de` (default empty)
examples: `fr,de` — first target column is French, second is German

targetSourceRefs.help: Maps the columns in `targetColumns` to the source columns they translate. Given as a comma-separated list of 1-based source-column indices, aligned position-by-position with `targetColumns`. A target whose reference cannot be resolved is sent to the skeleton rather than paired.

trimLeading.help: Removes leading spaces and tabs from each extracted cell value. Setting `trimMode` to anything other than `none` forces this on regardless of its stored value.

trimMode.help: Controls how leading and trailing whitespace is removed from extracted cell values. `none` leaves cells untouched. `non-qualified only` trims cells that are not wrapped in a text qualifier, leaving the inside of qualified fields intact. `all` trims every cell, qualified or not. Setting any value other than `none` also forces `trimLeading` and `trimTrailing` on.
trimMode.values: `0` = none, `1` = non-qualified fields only, `2` = all fields (default `1`)
notes: `non-qualified only` matters only for formats that have a text qualifier (CSV); for TSV and fixed-width it behaves like trimming all cells.

trimTrailing.help: Removes trailing spaces and tabs from each extracted cell value. Setting `trimMode` to anything other than `none` forces this on regardless of its stored value.

unescapeSource.help: Inherited from the plain-text base filter. When enabled, escape sequences in extracted cell values — `\t`, `\n`, `\\`, and `\uXXXX` — are converted to the characters they represent before extraction.

useCodeFinder.help: Inherited from the plain-text base filter. When enabled, the regular expressions in `codeFinderRules` are applied to extracted cell text and matching runs are turned into inline codes rather than translatable words. Used to protect printf-style variables, escape sequences, and similar markup embedded in cell values.

dependsOn.condition: supplies the patterns applied when enabled

valuesStartLineNum.help: The 1-based line number where actual data rows begin. Every line before it is treated as the header area. Header lines are emitted (or not) according to `sendHeaderMode`; data lines are split into cells and processed according to the column settings.
valuesStartLineNum.values: 1-based line number (default `1`, i.e. data starts on the first line, no header)

wrapMode.help: Determines how a cell value that spans multiple physical lines is represented in the extracted text unit. `NONE` joins the wrapped lines with a line feed. `SPACES` joins them with a single space (unwrapping). `PLACEHOLDERS` inserts an inline placeholder code at each internal line break so the wrapping can be reproduced on output.

limitations: None known according to the wiki documentation.
processingNotes: Records are lines; fields are columns. The base filter emits the entire line as one cell — a derived format (CSV/TSV/fixed-width) overrides cell extraction.
Each extracted cell carries `line_number`, `column_number`, and `row_number` properties; header rows report `row_number` 0.
In `listed` column mode, source cells are emitted as referents and a document part carries the row skeleton, so delimiters and non-extracted cells round-trip on output.
When a source or target column has no assigned locale and the column-names line holds a valid locale code, the filter parses it and updates the pipeline source/target locale.

examples.title: Generic two-column source/target table
examples.description: The default base-table layout extracts column 1 as source and pairs column 2 as its target (a bilingual configuration). Because the base filter does not split lines, this is most useful as a starting point that a delimiter-aware sub-config refines.
examples.config:
```yaml
configId: okf_basetable
columns:
  sourceColumns: "1"
  targetColumns: "2"
  sendColumnsMode: 1   # listed columns only
references:
  targetSourceRefs: "1"  # target column 2 translates source column 1
```

examples.title: Header with a column-names line
examples.description: Treat line 1 as column names and start data on line 2, extracting only the column names from the header.
examples.config:
```yaml
configId: okf_basetable
columns:
  columnNamesLineNum: 1
  valuesStartLineNum: 2
  sendHeaderMode: 1    # column names only
```

simplifierRules.title: Simplifier Rules

doc.overview: The Table Filter handles plain-text tabular documents including CSV, TSV, and fixed-width column formats. It supports multiple table types with configurable column delimiters, text qualifiers, escaping modes, and column-based extraction rules. The filter ships with six built-in configurations: generic table, CSV with header, Haiku CatKeys, source-target tab-separated, fixed-width columns, and TSV. Each configuration embeds its own column definitions, delimiter settings, and extraction behavior as serialized string parameters.

simplifierRules.description: Rules for simplifying inline code representation in extracted segments
simplifierRules.help:
Define rules for simplifying inline code representation in extracted text. Uses the Okapi simplifier rules syntax to merge or reduce inline codes into simpler placeholders.

This is edited via a dedicated rules editor widget.

sourceColumns.help: The columns whose cells are extracted as source (translatable) text, given as a comma-separated list of 1-based column indices. Only consulted when `sendColumnsMode` is `listed`. When empty in `listed` mode, no columns are treated as source.
examples: `1,3` — extract columns 1 and 3 as source text

sourceIdColumns.help: The columns whose values become the resource names (IDs) of the corresponding source text units, as a comma-separated list of 1-based indices. Each ID column is linked to the source column it names via `sourceIdSourceRefs`. When an ID cell is empty, the filter falls back to `recordIdColumn` + `sourceIdSuffixes`.

dependsOn.condition: maps each ID column to the source column it names

sourceIdSourceRefs.help: Maps the columns in `sourceIdColumns` to the source columns they identify. Given as a comma-separated list of 1-based source-column indices, aligned position-by-position with `sourceIdColumns`.

sourceIdSuffixes.help: Suffixes appended to the `recordIdColumn` value to synthesise an ID for a source column when that column has no explicit ID cell. The list is aligned position-by-position with `sourceColumns`, so each source column can receive a distinct suffix.

Each target column is tied to a source column through `targetSourceRefs` and given a locale through `targetLanguages`. Supplying target columns makes the configuration bilingual.

dependsOn.condition: maps each target column to its source column
assigns the locale of each target column

targetLanguages.help: The locale of each target column, as a comma-separated list aligned position-by-position with `targetColumns`. A single value applies that locale to all target columns. When a target column has no locale here, the pipeline's target locale is used.
targetLanguages.values: Comma-separated BCP-47/locale codes, e.g. `fr` or `fr,it,de` (default empty)
examples: `fr,de` — first target column is French, second is German

targetSourceRefs.help: Maps the columns in `targetColumns` to the source columns they translate. Given as a comma-separated list of 1-based source-column indices, aligned position-by-position with `targetColumns`. A target whose reference cannot be resolved is sent to the skeleton rather than paired.

trimLeading.help: Removes leading spaces and tabs from each extracted cell value. Setting `trimMode` to anything other than `none` forces this on regardless of its stored value.

trimMode.help: Controls how leading and trailing whitespace is removed from extracted cell values. `none` leaves cells untouched. `non-qualified only` trims cells that are not wrapped in a text qualifier, leaving the inside of qualified fields intact. `all` trims every cell, qualified or not. Setting any value other than `none` also forces `trimLeading` and `trimTrailing` on.
trimMode.values: `0` = none, `1` = non-qualified fields only, `2` = all fields (default `1`)
notes: `non-qualified only` matters only for formats that have a text qualifier (CSV); for TSV and fixed-width it behaves like trimming all cells.

trimTrailing.help: Removes trailing spaces and tabs from each extracted cell value. Setting `trimMode` to anything other than `none` forces this on regardless of its stored value.

unescapeSource.help: Inherited from the plain-text base filter. When enabled, escape sequences in extracted cell values — `\t`, `\n`, `\\`, and `\uXXXX` — are converted to the characters they represent before extraction.

useCodeFinder.help: Inherited from the plain-text base filter. When enabled, the regular expressions in `codeFinderRules` are applied to extracted cell text and matching runs are turned into inline codes rather than translatable words. Used to protect printf-style variables, escape sequences, and similar markup embedded in cell values.

dependsOn.condition: supplies the patterns applied when enabled

valuesStartLineNum.help: The 1-based line number where actual data rows begin. Every line before it is treated as the header area. Header lines are emitted (or not) according to `sendHeaderMode`; data lines are split into cells and processed according to the column settings.
valuesStartLineNum.values: 1-based line number (default `1`, i.e. data starts on the first line, no header)

wrapMode.help: Determines how a cell value that spans multiple physical lines is represented in the extracted text unit. `NONE` joins the wrapped lines with a line feed. `SPACES` joins them with a single space (unwrapping). `PLACEHOLDERS` inserts an inline placeholder code at each internal line break so the wrapping can be reproduced on output.

limitations: None known according to the wiki documentation.
processingNotes: **Input encoding**: If the file has a Unicode BOM, the corresponding encoding (UTF-8, UTF-16, etc.) is used. Otherwise the default encoding from filter options is used.
**Output encoding**: When output is UTF-8, a BOM is included only if the input was also UTF-8 and had a BOM. Converting from non-UTF-8 to UTF-8 produces no BOM.
**Line breaks**: Output preserves the same line-break style as the original input.
The filter uses `serializationFormat: stringParameters` — most configuration is embedded within each built-in configuration rather than as top-level schema properties.

Six built-in configurations are available: `okf_table` (generic), `okf_table_csv` (CSV with header), `okf_table_catkeys` (Haiku CatKeys), `okf_table_src-tab-trg` (2-column source+target), `okf_table_fwc` (fixed-width columns), `okf_table_tsv` (tab-separated values).

examples.title: CSV with header row
examples.description: Use the `okf_table_csv` configuration for standard CSV files where the first line contains column names and data starts on line 2. The default CSV configuration uses comma as field delimiter and double-quote as text qualifier.
examples.config:
```yaml
configId: okf_table_csv
# Built-in settings:
#   fieldDelimiter: ","
#   textQualifier: '"'
#   columnNamesLineNum: 1
#   valuesStartLineNum: 2
#   sendColumnsMode: 2 (extract by column definitions)
```

examples.title: Two-column source/target tab-separated
examples.description: Use `okf_table_src-tab-trg` for simple bilingual files where column 1 is source text and column 2 is target text, separated by tabs. No header line is expected.
examples.input:
```
Hello world	Bonjour le monde
Goodbye	Au revoir
```

examples.title: Handling qualifiers with embedded delimiters
examples.description: In CSV mode, text qualifiers allow field delimiters inside field values. If the qualifier itself appears in the text, it must be escaped — either by duplication (`""`) or backslash (`\"`) depending on the escaping mode setting.
examples.input:
```csv
Name,Description
"Widget","A small, useful device"
"Quote Test","She said ""hello"""
```

doc.overview: The TEX Filter extracts translatable prose from TeX and LaTeX source documents while leaving the command structure intact for round-trip writing. It tokenises the document into text, commands, comments, and newlines, then classifies each command to decide whether the text it introduces is translatable. Body paragraphs become translatable text units, separated by blank lines; the surrounding markup is preserved as skeleton.
limitations: Comments (lines beginning with `%`) are treated as non-translatable document parts.

Structural commands and their braced arguments are not extracted — for example `\begin`, `\end`, `\documentclass`, `\usepackage`, `\label`, `\ref`, `\cite`/`\citealt`/`\newcite`, `\pageref`, `\includegraphics`, `\hspace`/`\vspace`/`\hskip`/`\vskip`, `\put`, `\pagestyle`, and `\thispagestyle`.
Command recognition is based on a fixed built-in list; commands outside that list are treated as unknown markup. The filter has no configurable parameters to extend or override this classification.
processingNotes: Paragraph-level commands whose braced argument is translatable as its own unit include `\title`, `\author`, `\chapter`/`\Chapter`, `\section`, `\subsection`, `\caption`, `\titlerunning`, `\index`, and `\typeout`.

Inline formatting commands fold their text into the surrounding paragraph rather than starting a new unit — for example `\textbf`, `\textit`, `\texttt`, `\textsf`, `\emph`/`\em`, `\bf`, `\tt`, `\footnote`, `\hbox`/`\mbox`/`\vbox`.

Accent and national-symbol commands (`\oe`, `\AE`, `\ss`, `\o`, `\l`, the text-mode accents `\``, `\'`, `\^`, `\"`, `\~`, `\=`, `\.`, and similar) are recognised as character markup, not as new units.
Two or more consecutive newlines end the current paragraph; a single newline is treated as part of the running paragraph.
Encoding is auto-detected via a BOM; a UTF-8 BOM and UTF-8 encoding are honoured on output.
The filter has no configurable parameters.

examples.title: Sectioning command and body paragraph
examples.description: A sectioning command argument and the following paragraph are each extracted as translatable units, while structural commands stay in the skeleton.
examples.output:
```
Two text units are extracted: "Introduction" (from \section) and "This is the first paragraph.". The \label command and its argument are preserved as non-translatable skeleton.
```

entries.description: Extract translatable text from TeX and LaTeX documents.

doc.overview:
TeX/LaTeX is a markup-based typesetting language. neokapi reads the LaTeX2e
document syntax — preamble, body, command sequences, environments, math
mode, and comments — and extracts the prose that should be translated while
leaving the command structure intact.

neokapi reads and writes TeX/LaTeX. Body paragraphs inside
`\begin{document}` … `\end{document}` become translatable Blocks, one per
paragraph (paragraphs are separated by blank lines; a soft line break inside
a paragraph collapses to a space). Visible-text header commands such as
`\title`, `\author`, and `\date`, and paragraph-level commands such as
`\section`, `\chapter`, and `\caption`, are translatable. Inline formatting
commands such as `\textbf`, `\emph`, and `\footnote` fold into the
surrounding paragraph. Structural commands (`\label`, `\ref`, `\cite`,
`\usepackage`, `\documentclass`), comments, math (inline and environment),
and verbatim/listing environments are non-translatable and preserved as
skeleton. This format has no configurable JSON schema in this reference.
limitations: Math (inline and environment) and verbatim/listing environments are not extracted.
Comments and structural commands are non-translatable.
processingNotes: Body paragraphs inside the document environment become one translatable Block each; blank lines separate paragraphs.
Header and sectioning commands (`\title`, `\section`, `\caption`, …) are translatable; inline commands fold into the surrounding paragraph.
Structural commands, comments, math, and verbatim environments are preserved as skeleton for round-trips.

entries.description: Read and write TMX translation-memory exchange files, one block per translation unit.

schema.title: TMX (Translation Memory eXchange)
schema.description: Configuration for the native TMX translation memory format reader/writer

codeFinderRules.title: Code finder rules
codeFinderRules.description: Regex patterns that match inline codes within translatable text

exitOnInvalid.title: Exit on invalid TUs
exitOnInvalid.description: Stop processing when encountering invalid TUs. When false, invalid TUs are skipped.

processAllTargets.title: Read all target entries
processAllTargets.description: Read all target language TUVs from each TU. When false, only the first target is read.

useCodeFinder.title: Enable inline code detection
useCodeFinder.description: Enable regex-based inline code detection in translatable text

doc.overview:
TMX (Translation Memory eXchange) is the XML interchange format for
translation memories. A TMX document holds a header declaring the
source language (`srclang`) and a body of `<tu>` translation units;
each unit carries one `<tuv>` translation-unit variant per language,
with optional unit- and header-level `<note>` and `<prop>` metadata.

The reader emits one translatable block per `<tu>`: the source segment
matches the header `srclang` and a target segment is produced for each
other-language `<tuv>`. The header attributes and any header-level note
or property are surfaced as a single non-translatable data part ahead
of the blocks, while unit-level notes and properties attach to the
block. This makes TMX usable both as an input for review or alignment
and as an output for exporting a memory. It mirrors Okapi's TMX filter.

codeFinderRules.description: Regular-expression patterns that match inline codes in segment text.
codeFinderRules.help:
A list of regular expressions; each match within a translatable
block is protected as an inline code rather than translated. Used to
shield placeholders or markup carried inside TM segments. Only takes
effect when `useCodeFinder` is enabled; invalid patterns are skipped.
examples: `\{[0-9]+\}` — protect numbered placeholders like `{0}`

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for the rules to take effect

escapeGT.description: Escape the greater-than character as `>` in output XML.
escapeGT.help:
A writer-side option. When enabled, the `>` character is written as
the `>` entity. When disabled (the default), `>` is written
literally, which is valid XML outside of a CDATA close sequence.
Enable it for stricter downstream tools that expect `>` escaped.

exitOnInvalid.description: Stop processing when an invalid translation unit is encountered.
exitOnInvalid.help:
Governs error handling for malformed `<tu>` elements. When disabled
(the default), invalid units are skipped silently and parsing
continues. When enabled, the first invalid unit aborts processing —
use it to fail fast on corrupt or non-conformant input.

processAllTargets.description: Read every target-language variant in each translation unit.
processAllTargets.help:
When enabled (the default), all non-source `<tuv>` variants of a
`<tu>` are read, producing a target segment for each language. When
disabled, only the first non-source variant is read and the rest are
skipped — useful when a multilingual memory should be reduced to a
single target language.

useCodeFinder.description: Detect inline codes inside segment text using regular expressions.
useCodeFinder.help:
Enables regex-based detection of inline codes (placeholders,
variables, tags) within translatable text, using the patterns in
`codeFinderRules`. When disabled (the default), no pattern-based
inline codes are extracted.

processingNotes: The source segment matches the header `srclang`; one target segment is produced per other-language `<tuv>`.
Header attributes and header-level notes and properties are emitted as a single non-translatable data part ahead of the blocks; unit-level notes and properties attach to the block.

examples.title: Read only the first target
examples.description: Reduce a multilingual memory to a single target language.

examples.title: Fail fast on invalid units
examples.description: Abort processing when a malformed translation unit is found.

inlineCodes.title: Inline Codes
inlineCodes.description: Pattern-based detection of inline codes (placeholders, variables, tags) within translatable text.

enabled.title: Enable Inline Code Detection
enabled.description: Enable pattern-based detection of inline codes (placeholders, tags, etc.)

rules.title: Code Finder Rules
rules.description: Regex patterns to identify inline codes in translatable text

rules.title: Patterns
rules.description: List of regex patterns to detect inline codes

pattern.title: Pattern
pattern.description: Regex pattern for inline code detection

sample.title: Sample Text
sample.description: Sample text to test patterns against

useAllRulesWhenTesting.title: Test All Rules Together
useAllRulesWhenTesting.description: Test all rules together or individually

mergeAdjacent.title: Merge Adjacent Codes
mergeAdjacent.description: Merge consecutive inline codes into a single placeholder

moveBoundaryCodes.title: Move Boundary Codes
moveBoundaryCodes.description: Move inline codes at segment boundaries to the skeleton (non-translatable)

simplifierRules.title: Simplifier Rules
simplifierRules.description: Rules for simplifying inline code representation

segType.title: Creates or not a segment for the extracted <Tu>

exitOnInvalid.title: Exit when encountering invalid <tu>s (default is to skip invalid <tu>s).

consolidateDpSkeleton.title: Consolidate Document Part Skeleton
consolidateDpSkeleton.description: Group all document part skeleton into a single resource to reduce pipeline events.

propValueSep.title: String used to delimit property values when there are duplicate properties

doc.overview: The TMX Filter implements the `IFilter` interface for **TMX (Translation Memory eXchange)** documents. TMX is a LISA standard for transporting translation memory data between translation tools. The filter reads `<tu>` entries with configurable target and segmentation behavior, and supports the TMX 1.4b specification.

consolidateDpSkeleton.description: Group all document part skeleton into a single resource to reduce pipeline events.
consolidateDpSkeleton.help:
Consolidates skeleton parts from document-part resources and sends fewer events through the pipeline.

This is sufficient for most use cases. Disable only if you need fine-grained access to individual document-part resources in the pipeline (e.g., for custom pipeline step development).

extraction.exitOnInvalid.help: Determines how the reader reacts to a malformed `<tu>` element. By default the invalid unit is skipped, a warning naming its `tuid` is logged, and parsing continues — which risks producing output whose structure no longer matches the input. Enable this to abort on the first invalid `<tu>` instead, so corrupt or non-conformant input fails fast and can be corrected before re-running.
notes: With the default (skip), an invalid `<tu>` is dropped silently apart from a log warning, so the merged output may not round-trip the input.

extraction.processAllTargets.help:
Controls how many target-language variants are read from each `<tu>`. When enabled (the default), every non-source `<tuv>` is read and contributes a target segment, so a multilingual memory yields one target per language. When disabled, only the source `<tuv>` and the `<tuv>` matching the requested target locale are read; all other target variants are written to the skeleton untranslated.

Whether the extra targets are usable depends on the downstream pipeline — only steps that handle multiple targets benefit from reading them all.
notes: Any effect this setting has depends on the following pipeline steps and their ability to process multiple targets.

extraction.propValueSep.help: The delimiter inserted between values when the same property `type` appears more than once on a single `<tu>` or `<tuv>`. Duplicate `<prop>` elements are collapsed into one property whose value is the original values joined by this string.
extraction.propValueSep.values: Any string (default `", "` — comma followed by a space)

extraction.segType.help: Sets the default segmentation behaviour for an extracted `<tu>` and is consulted only when the unit's own `segtype` attribute (and, failing that, the header `segtype`) does not resolve the type. The value is an integer: `0` always segments as sentence, `1` always as paragraph, `2` segments as sentence when `segtype` is `sentence` or absent (the default), and `3` segments as paragraph when `segtype` is absent. An explicit `segtype="sentence"` on the unit or header always wins over this default.

output.escapeGT.help: Writer-side option. When enabled, every `>` in output is written as `>`. When disabled (the default), `>` is emitted literally, which is valid XML outside a `]]>` CDATA close sequence. Enable it for stricter downstream tools that require `>` escaped.

limitations: The `<sub>` element is not supported. When encountered, a warning is issued and the element content is merged into its parent element's content.
The filter cannot reconstruct any DTD declaration from the original document.
processingNotes: **Input encoding:** Uses the document's encoding declaration if present; otherwise defaults to UTF-8 regardless of any specified default encoding.
**Output BOM handling:** When output encoding is UTF-8 and input was also UTF-8, a BOM is written only if one was detected in the input. If input was not UTF-8, no BOM is written.
**Line breaks:** The output preserves the same line-break style as the original input document.

examples.title: Default configuration for TM exchange
examples.description: The default settings read all target entries and consolidate skeleton, which is suitable for most TM exchange workflows.

entries.description: Read and write Trados TagEditor TTX bilingual documents.

doc.overview:
TTX is the XML-based bilingual format produced by Trados TagEditor and read
by several other translation tools. There is no official public TTX
specification. The format stores source and target text as translation
units, carries existing segmentation, and records match percentages and
translation annotations.

neokapi reads and writes TTX. It walks the XML once, collects segment text
inside the `<Tu>`/`<Tuv>` translation-unit elements, and emits one Block per
translation unit — the first translation-unit variant as the source and the
second as the target. The surrounding XML is preserved as skeleton. By
default the reader auto-detects segmentation (extracting existing segments
when present, otherwise all text), and the writer does not escape
greater-than characters. These choices track the upstream Okapi TTX filter;
the native reader does not expose them as a configurable JSON schema in this
reference.
limitations: There is no official public TTX specification; behaviour follows the conventions Trados tools write.
Segmentation mode and output-escaping toggles match the upstream Okapi TTX filter but are not surfaced as configurable schema properties in this reference.
processingNotes: One Block per `<Tu>`; the first `<Tuv>` becomes the source and the second the target.
Segmentation is auto-detected by default — existing segments are extracted when present, otherwise all text.
The surrounding XML is preserved as skeleton for round-trips.

entries.description: Read and write TXML bilingual documents (the Wordfast Pro XML format).

doc.overview:
TXML is the XML-based bilingual format used by Wordfast Pro and supported by
several other tools. There is no official public TXML specification. A TXML
document has a top-level `<txml>` root carrying `locale` and `targetlocale`
attributes, optional `<skeleton>` wrappers holding original markup, and
`<translatable>` blocks whose `<segment>` children each hold a
`<source>`/`<target>` pair.

neokapi reads and writes TXML. Each `<translatable>` element becomes one
Block, with one segment per `<segment>` child, preserving existing
translations on read. The surrounding markup is kept as skeleton. By default
the writer permits empty target segments — emitting an empty target element
for an untranslated segment rather than omitting it — matching the upstream
Okapi TXML filter. The native reader applies this default but does not
expose it as a configurable JSON schema in this reference.
limitations: There is no official public TXML specification; behaviour follows the schema the upstream Okapi filter implements.
The empty-target output toggle matches the upstream Okapi TXML filter but is not surfaced as a configurable schema property in this reference.
processingNotes: One Block per `<translatable>` element, with one segment per `<segment>` child.
Existing `<source>`/`<target>` translations are preserved on read; surrounding markup is kept as skeleton.
By default an untranslated segment writes an empty target element rather than omitting it.

doc.overview: The RTF Filter reads **Trados-tagged bilingual RTF** documents — the segmented RTF exchange format produced by legacy Trados Workbench / TagEditor workflows, in which each translation segment is wrapped in `{0>source<}n*{>target<0}` markup. The filter parses the RTF token stream (control words and groups), recognises the Trados bilingual markers, and extracts the source text from each segment as a translatable text unit. It is a reading-only filter: it is intended for harvesting source content from existing tagged RTF, not for producing tagged RTF output.
limitations: This is a **read-only** filter (its display name is "Trados-Tagged RTF Filter - READING ONLY"). It is meant for extracting source segments from Trados-tagged bilingual RTF, not for writing translated RTF.

The input is expected to follow the Trados bilingual segment markup (`{0>...<}n*{>...<0}`). Plain RTF without this tagging will not yield bilingual segments; if the stream does not look like RTF at all, the filter logs a warning.
Hidden text appearing in target content is detected and reported; it is not treated as translatable source.
processingNotes: The parser walks RTF control words and groups, extracting text from segment runs while skipping non-translatable destinations such as the font table, colour table, stylesheet, document info, pictures, and field instructions.

Common RTF escapes are decoded on read: `\uN` Unicode escapes, `\'HH` hex escapes, and special-character control words including `\par`, `\tab`, `\line`, `\endash`/`\emdash`, the smart-quote words (`\lquote`/`\rquote`/`\ldblquote`/`\rdblquote`), and `\bullet`.
The `tw4winInternal` character style and field destinations are used to distinguish Trados tag markup from translatable text.
The filter has no configurable parameters; behaviour is fixed.

examples.title: Extracting a Trados bilingual segment
examples.description: A Trados-tagged RTF segment wraps source and target text in `{0>...<}n*{>...<0}` markup. The filter recognises the markers and extracts the source text as a translatable unit.
examples.output:
```
The source text "Hello, world." is extracted as a translatable text unit; the Trados markup and the existing target are treated as non-translatable structure.
```

openProject.title: Open the project file before processing

doc.overview: The Transifex Filter processes online [Transifex](http://www.transifex.org) translation projects by reading a local `.txp` project file. It connects to a Transifex host, downloads PO-based translation resources, and processes them as standard PO filter events. When used with a filter-writer, modified PO files are pushed back to the Transifex project.

general.openProject.help: An interactive option that, when enabled (the default), opens the `.txp` project file in an editor dialog before processing so a user can review the host and credentials and choose which resources to fetch. It drives a Swing/SWT dialog and has no effect when the filter runs without a UI; in a headless or automated context leave it at the default and edit the `.txp` file beforehand to control resource selection.
notes: Requires a graphical environment; the dialog is skipped where no UI is available.
The project file must be a UTF-8 text file with the `.txp` extension.

limitations: This filter is **BETA**.

Transifex loses fuzzy flags and their corresponding translations when downloading a PO file uploaded with fuzzy entries. Round-trips without losing target text require not labeling entries as fuzzy, which may incorrectly show resources as 100% translated.
Only PO-based files in Transifex are handled — TS files are not supported.

Due to pipeline mechanism limitations, this filter cannot be used with steps that re-write different output corresponding to the resource, other than the **Filter Events to Raw Document Step**. For example, it will not work properly with the **Rainbow Translation Kit Creation Step**.
processingNotes: Input is a local `.txp` project file (UTF-8 text) that describes the Transifex project connection and resource list.
If the `.txp` file contains no resources, the filter connects to Transifex and fetches the full resource list. If resources are already listed, the filter uses those without refreshing.
Only PO resources whose source language matches the specified source locale are processed.
For each matching resource, the filter downloads the translation file for the target language. If no translation file exists, one with empty entries is created.
When a filter-writer is used, modified PO files are pushed back to the Transifex project using the same credentials.

examples.title: Transifex project file (.txp)
examples.description: A `.txp` file defines the connection settings, locale fall-backs, and which resources to process. Lines starting with `#` are comments. Each resource can be selected (`yes`) or deselected (`no`) with a tab-separated flag; resources without a flag default to selected.
examples.config:
```properties
host=http://www.transifex.net
user=johndoe
password=xrt34@asf
projectId=myproject
protectApproved=yes
# Fall-back values for the source and target locales:
sourceLocale=en
targetLocale=fr
# Resources
myfile1xmlpo	yes
myfile2odtpo	no
```

entries.description: Read and write Okapi's TransTable v1 tab-separated bilingual exchange table.

doc.overview:
TransTable v1 is the tab-separated bilingual exchange table that
Okapi's generic writer produces for its "Translation Table" output.
The first line is a header declaring the signature (`TransTableV1`)
and the source and target locales as three tab-separated cells. Each
following line is a context crumb (`okpCtx:tu=<id>`) followed by a
source cell and an optional target cell.

The reader extracts one translatable block per text unit, pairing the
source and target cells. Cells may be wrapped in double quotes, which
are stripped on read; the literal sequences `\t` and `\n` inside a
cell are unescaped to tab and newline. Whitespace-only lines are
skipped. A crumb may carry a segment suffix (`:s=<seg-id>`); rows that
share the same `tu=<id>` and carry segment suffixes are merged into a
single segmented text unit. A missing or malformed header aborts the
read.

Configuration is supplied on the collection's `format:` block in a
`.kapi` project. The one key is `allowSegments` (default true): when
enabled, `:s=<seg-id>` suffixes are honoured and rows sharing a
`tu=<id>` merge into one segmented unit; when disabled, every row is
its own text unit regardless of any segment suffix. It mirrors
Okapi's `allowSegments` parameter.
limitations: The header must be a valid `TransTableV1` signature line; otherwise the read fails.
Only the v1 table shape is supported.
processingNotes: One block per text unit; source and target cells pair into source and target segments.
Surrounding double quotes are stripped and `\t` / `\n` escapes are decoded on read.
With `allowSegments` enabled, rows sharing a `tu=<id>` and carrying `:s=<seg-id>` suffixes merge into one segmented block.

examples.title: Read a bilingual table without segment grouping
examples.description:
Disable segment grouping so each row becomes its own text unit,
regardless of any `:s=<seg-id>` suffix on the crumb.
examples.config:
format:
  name: transtable
  config:
    allowSegments: false

doc.overview: The Table Filter handles plain-text tabular documents including CSV, TSV, and fixed-width column formats. It supports multiple table types with configurable column delimiters, text qualifiers, escaping modes, and column-based extraction rules. The filter ships with six built-in configurations: generic table, CSV with header, Haiku CatKeys, source-target tab-separated, fixed-width columns, and TSV. Each configuration embeds its own column definitions, delimiter settings, and extraction behavior as serialized string parameters.

codeFinderRules.help: The inline-code detection rules applied to extracted cell text, in Okapi `#v1` rule format. The defaults match printf-style format specifiers and common backslash escape sequences. Only applied when `useCodeFinder` is enabled.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for these rules to apply

columnNamesLineNum.help:
The 1-based line number that holds the column captions (field names). When set to a line within the header area (before `valuesStartLineNum`), the filter reads that line to learn the column names. Those names are used to label comment metadata and, when a source or target column has not been assigned a locale, the column name is parsed as a locale code and used to set the pipeline's source/target locale.

Leave at `0` when the file has no column-name line.
notes: Lines are numbered from 1. For a CSV with a header on line 1 and data from line 2, set `columnNamesLineNum=1` and `valuesStartLineNum=2`.

commentColumns.help: The columns whose values are attached as notes (or, with `exposeCommentsAsMetadata`, as metadata) to a source text unit rather than extracted as translatable text. Each comment column is linked to the source column it annotates via `commentSourceRefs`. To have a comment translated instead, list its column under `sourceColumns`.

dependsOn.condition: maps each comment column to the source column it annotates

commentSourceRefs.help: Maps the columns in `commentColumns` to the source columns they annotate. Given as a comma-separated list of 1-based source-column indices, aligned position-by-position with `commentColumns`. The comment becomes a read-only note on the referenced source text unit.

detectColumnsMode.help: Controls how the filter decides how many columns each row has. With no detection, rows with differing field counts simply yield differing numbers of text units. With column-name detection, the column count is fixed to the number of names found on the column-names line. With a fixed number, every row is padded with empty cells or truncated to exactly `numColumns` cells.
detectColumnsMode.values: `0` = none (per-row), `1` = from the column-names line, `2` = fixed `numColumns` (default `0`)

dependsOn.condition: used only when set to 2 (fixed number)
used only when set to 1 (from column names)

exposeCommentsAsMetadata.help: When enabled, values drawn from `commentColumns` are attached to the source text unit as metadata (keyed by the column name) instead of as a translator note. Use it when downstream tooling consumes structured metadata rather than free-form notes.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when comment columns are defined

mergeAdjacentCodes.help: When multiple inline codes appear consecutively with no text between them, they are combined into a single placeholder code. This simplifies the translatable content by reducing the number of inline codes translators must handle. For example, `` would become a single placeholder rather than two separate codes.

moveLeadingAndTrailingCodesToSkeleton.help: Inline codes appearing at the very beginning or end of a segment are moved into the skeleton (non-translatable portion). This reduces clutter in the translatable text when boundary codes carry no translational significance. For example, if a segment starts with `` and ends with ``, those codes are moved to skeleton so translators see only the text content.

numColumns.help: The fixed number of columns expected per row. Only consulted when `detectColumnsMode` is set to fixed-number. Rows with fewer cells are padded with empty cells; rows with more cells have the extras dropped.

dependsOn.condition: only active when detectColumnsMode = 2 (fixed number)

preserveWS.help: Inherited from the plain-text base filter. When enabled, whitespace within extracted cell values is preserved as-is. When disabled, the filter is permitted to normalise whitespace. Cell-edge trimming is controlled separately by `trimMode`, `trimLeading`, and `trimTrailing`.

recordIdColumn.help: The 1-based index of the column whose value identifies the record (the row key). When set, that column's value is combined with the entries in `sourceIdSuffixes` to build the resource name (ID) for source columns that do not carry their own ID from `sourceIdColumns`.

sendColumnsMode.help: Controls which columns of each data row are extracted. With `none`, no cells are extracted. With `listed`, only the columns named in `sourceColumns`, `targetColumns`, `sourceIdColumns`, and `commentColumns` are processed — this is what enables bilingual extraction, IDs, and notes. With `all`, every cell in the row is extracted as source text and the column-role lists are ignored.
sendColumnsMode.values: `0` = none, `1` = listed columns only, `2` = all columns (default `1`)
notes: Bilingual extraction (source paired with target) is only available in `listed` mode with a non-empty `targetColumns`.

sendHeaderMode.help: Controls which header-area lines (those before `valuesStartLineNum`) are extracted as translatable text. With `0`, no header lines are extracted. With `1`, only the column-names line is extracted. With `2`, every header line is extracted; non-column-name header lines are sent whole rather than split into cells.
sendHeaderMode.values: `0` = none, `1` = column names only, `2` = all header lines (default `0`)

simplifierRules.description: Rules for simplifying inline code representation in extracted segments
simplifierRules.help:
Define rules for simplifying inline code representation in extracted text. Uses the Okapi simplifier rules syntax to merge or reduce inline codes into simpler placeholders.

This is edited via a dedicated rules editor widget.

sourceColumns.help: The columns whose cells are extracted as source (translatable) text, given as a comma-separated list of 1-based column indices. Only consulted when `sendColumnsMode` is `listed`. When empty in `listed` mode, no columns are treated as source.
examples: `1,3` — extract columns 1 and 3 as source text

sourceIdColumns.help: The columns whose values become the resource names (IDs) of the corresponding source text units, as a comma-separated list of 1-based indices. Each ID column is linked to the source column it names via `sourceIdSourceRefs`. When an ID cell is empty, the filter falls back to `recordIdColumn` + `sourceIdSuffixes`.

dependsOn.condition: maps each ID column to the source column it names

sourceIdSourceRefs.help: Maps the columns in `sourceIdColumns` to the source columns they identify. Given as a comma-separated list of 1-based source-column indices, aligned position-by-position with `sourceIdColumns`.

sourceIdSuffixes.help: Suffixes appended to the `recordIdColumn` value to synthesise an ID for a source column when that column has no explicit ID cell. The list is aligned position-by-position with `sourceColumns`, so each source column can receive a distinct suffix.

targetColumns.help: The columns that hold existing target (translated) text, as a comma-separated list of 1-based indices. Each target column is tied to a source column through `targetSourceRefs` and given a locale through `targetLanguages`. Supplying target columns makes the configuration bilingual.

dependsOn.condition: maps each target column to its source column
assigns the locale of each target column

targetLanguages.help: The locale of each target column, as a comma-separated list aligned position-by-position with `targetColumns`. A single value applies that locale to all target columns. When a target column has no locale here, the pipeline's target locale is used.
targetLanguages.values: Comma-separated BCP-47/locale codes, e.g. `fr` or `fr,it,de` (default empty)
examples: `fr,de` — first target column is French, second is German

targetSourceRefs.help: Maps the columns in `targetColumns` to the source columns they translate. Given as a comma-separated list of 1-based source-column indices, aligned position-by-position with `targetColumns`. A target whose reference cannot be resolved is sent to the skeleton rather than paired.

trimLeading.help: Removes leading spaces and tabs from each extracted cell value. Setting `trimMode` to anything other than `none` forces this on regardless of its stored value.

trimMode.help: Controls how leading and trailing whitespace is removed from extracted cell values. `none` leaves cells untouched. `non-qualified only` trims cells that are not wrapped in a text qualifier, leaving the inside of qualified fields intact. `all` trims every cell, qualified or not. Setting any value other than `none` also forces `trimLeading` and `trimTrailing` on.
trimMode.values: `0` = none, `1` = non-qualified fields only, `2` = all fields (default `1`)
notes: `non-qualified only` matters only for formats that have a text qualifier (CSV); for TSV and fixed-width it behaves like trimming all cells.

trimTrailing.help: Removes trailing spaces and tabs from each extracted cell value. Setting `trimMode` to anything other than `none` forces this on regardless of its stored value.

unescapeSource.help: Inherited from the plain-text base filter. When enabled, escape sequences in extracted cell values — `\t`, `\n`, `\\`, and `\uXXXX` — are converted to the characters they represent before extraction.

useCodeFinder.help: Inherited from the plain-text base filter. When enabled, the regular expressions in `codeFinderRules` are applied to extracted cell text and matching runs are turned into inline codes rather than translatable words. Used to protect printf-style variables, escape sequences, and similar markup embedded in cell values.

dependsOn.condition: supplies the patterns applied when enabled

valuesStartLineNum.help: The 1-based line number where actual data rows begin. Every line before it is treated as the header area. Header lines are emitted (or not) according to `sendHeaderMode`; data lines are split into cells and processed according to the column settings.
valuesStartLineNum.values: 1-based line number (default `1`, i.e. data starts on the first line, no header)

wrapMode.help: Determines how a cell value that spans multiple physical lines is represented in the extracted text unit. `NONE` joins the wrapped lines with a line feed. `SPACES` joins them with a single space (unwrapping). `PLACEHOLDERS` inserts an inline placeholder code at each internal line break so the wrapping can be reproduced on output.

limitations: None known according to the wiki documentation.
processingNotes: **Input encoding**: If the file has a Unicode BOM, the corresponding encoding (UTF-8, UTF-16, etc.) is used. Otherwise the default encoding from filter options is used.
**Output encoding**: When output is UTF-8, a BOM is included only if the input was also UTF-8 and had a BOM. Converting from non-UTF-8 to UTF-8 produces no BOM.
**Line breaks**: Output preserves the same line-break style as the original input.
The filter uses `serializationFormat: stringParameters` — most configuration is embedded within each built-in configuration rather than as top-level schema properties.

Six built-in configurations are available: `okf_table` (generic), `okf_table_csv` (CSV with header), `okf_table_catkeys` (Haiku CatKeys), `okf_table_src-tab-trg` (2-column source+target), `okf_table_fwc` (fixed-width columns), `okf_table_tsv` (tab-separated values).

examples.title: CSV with header row
examples.description: Use the `okf_table_csv` configuration for standard CSV files where the first line contains column names and data starts on line 2. The default CSV configuration uses comma as field delimiter and double-quote as text qualifier.
examples.config:
```yaml
configId: okf_table_csv
# Built-in settings:
#   fieldDelimiter: ","
#   textQualifier: '"'
#   columnNamesLineNum: 1
#   valuesStartLineNum: 2
#   sendColumnsMode: 2 (extract by column definitions)
```

examples.title: Two-column source/target tab-separated
examples.description: Use `okf_table_src-tab-trg` for simple bilingual files where column 1 is source text and column 2 is target text, separated by tabs. No header line is expected.
examples.input:
```
Hello world	Bonjour le monde
Goodbye	Au revoir
```

examples.title: Handling qualifiers with embedded delimiters
examples.description: In CSV mode, text qualifiers allow field delimiters inside field values. If the qualifier itself appears in the text, it must be escaped — either by duplication (`""`) or backslash (`\"`) depending on the escaping mode setting.
examples.input:
```csv
Name,Description
"Widget","A small, useful device"
"Quote Test","She said ""hello"""
```

inlineCodes.title: Inline Codes
inlineCodes.description: Pattern-based detection of inline codes (placeholders, variables, tags) within translatable text.

enabled.title: Has Inline Codes
enabled.description: Enable pattern-based inline code detection using the defined regular expressions.

rules.title: Code Finder Rules
rules.description: Regex patterns to identify inline codes in translatable text

rules.title: Patterns
rules.description: List of regex patterns to detect inline codes

pattern.title: Pattern
pattern.description: Regex pattern for inline code detection

sample.title: Sample Text
sample.description: Sample text to test patterns against

useAllRulesWhenTesting.title: Test All Rules Together
useAllRulesWhenTesting.description: Test all rules together or individually

mergeAdjacent.title: Merge Adjacent Codes
mergeAdjacent.description: Merge consecutive inline codes into a single placeholder

moveBoundaryCodes.title: Move Boundary Codes
moveBoundaryCodes.description: Move inline codes at segment boundaries to the skeleton (non-translatable)

simplifierRules.title: Simplifier Rules
simplifierRules.description: Rules for simplifying inline code representation

decodeByteValues.title: Convert byte values to raw characters

doc.overview: The TS Filter processes **Qt TS (Translation Source)** resource files used in Qt Linguist localization workflows. It implements the `IFilter` interface in the class `net.sf.okapi.filters.ts.TsFilter`. The implementation follows the [TS File Format specification](http://doc.trolltech.com/4.3/linguist-ts-file-format.html). The filter handles translation state mapping from TS `type` attributes and supports `<byte/>` element conversion.

output.decodeByteValues.help:
Controls how `<byte value="..."/>` elements are read from source text. These elements carry characters that are invalid in XML, encoded as a number (hexadecimal when prefixed with `x`, otherwise decimal). When disabled (the default), each `<byte/>` is preserved as an inline placeholder code, keeping the value intact through exchange. When enabled, the byte is decoded to its raw character and merged into the segment text.

Keep it disabled when the downstream exchange format is XML-based (XLIFF, TMX): the decoded characters are invalid XML and would break those documents.
notes: Characters stored as `<byte/>` are invalid XML; decoding them to raw characters can corrupt XML-based exchange formats such as XLIFF or TMX.
A `<byte/>` whose `value` is not a valid number aborts processing with an error.

output.escapeGT.help: Writer-side option. When enabled, the `>` character is written as `>` in output. When disabled (the default), `>` is emitted literally, which is valid XML outside a `]]>` CDATA close sequence. Enable it for stricter downstream tools that require `>` escaped.

limitations: There are sometimes issues with extracting and merging back `<numerusform>` elements. Perform a round-trip test before sending for translation.
processingNotes: Line-breaks in the output match the original input file's line-break style.
Entries with `<translation type="obsolete">` are **not extracted** — Qt treats these as unused entries, not just obsolete translations.
Entries with `<translation type="unfinished">` are extracted with the target `approved` property set to `no`.
Entries with no `type` attribute on `<translation>` are extracted with the target `approved` property set to `yes`.
The `<translatorcomment>` element is stored as text unit `Property.TRANSNOTE` and can be used by subsequent pipeline steps or editors.

examples.title: TS translation state mapping
examples.description:
Shows how the TS `type` attribute on `<translation>` maps to Okapi's `approved` property:

| TS `type` attribute | Okapi behavior |
|---|---|
| *(none)* | Extracted, approved = `yes` |
| `unfinished` | Extracted, approved = `no` |
| `obsolete` | **Not extracted** |

examples.title: Default inline code pattern
examples.description: The default regular expression detects printf placeholders, escape sequences, and numbered placeholders in extracted text.
examples.input:
```
File %s has %d errors\nSee {0} for details
```
examples.output:
```
File <1> has <2> errors<3>See <4> for details
```

columnNamesLineNum.title: Line with column names (0 if none)

numColumns.title: Number of Columns
numColumns.description: Fixed number of columns when column detection mode is set to fixed.

trimMode.title: Exclude leading/trailing white spaces from extracted text

useCodeFinder.title: Has inline codes as defined below

codeFinderRules.title: Code Finder Rules
codeFinderRules.description: Regex patterns to identify inline codes in translatable text

simplifierRules.description: Rules for simplifying inline code representation
simplifierRules.title: Simplifier Rules

doc.overview: The Tab-Separated Values Filter extracts translatable text from tab-delimited tables, where each record is a line and columns are separated by one or more tab characters. Unlike a single-character CSV delimiter, runs of consecutive tabs are treated as one separator, so empty columns are not produced by extra tabs and the layout tolerates aligned, multi-tab spacing. It has no text qualifier or escaping machinery of its own; it reuses the table family's column-role model, header handling, and whitespace controls. It is well suited to simple bilingual files (source tab target) and to tab-aligned exports.

codeFinderRules.help: The inline-code detection rules applied to extracted cell text, in Okapi `#v1` rule format. The defaults match printf-style format specifiers and common backslash escape sequences. Only applied when `useCodeFinder` is enabled.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for these rules to apply

columnNamesLineNum.help: The 1-based line number holding the column captions. The line is split into cells to read the names, which label comment metadata and may set the pipeline locale when a column name is a locale code. Use `0` when there is no column-name line.
notes: Lines are numbered from 1. For a CSV with a header on line 1 and data from line 2, set `columnNamesLineNum=1` and `valuesStartLineNum=2`.

commentColumns.help: Comma-separated 1-based indices of columns whose values attach as notes (or metadata, see `exposeCommentsAsMetadata`) to the source column they annotate via `commentSourceRefs`, rather than being extracted as translatable text.

dependsOn.condition: maps each comment column to the source column it annotates

commentSourceRefs.help: Maps each column in `commentColumns` to the source column it annotates, as a comma-separated list of 1-based source-column indices aligned position-by-position. The comment becomes a read-only note on that source text unit.

detectColumnsMode.help: How the column count per row is decided: per-row, from the number of names on the column-names line, or a fixed count.

dependsOn.condition: used only when set to 2 (fixed number)
used only when set to 1 (from column names)

exposeCommentsAsMetadata.help: When enabled, comment-column values attach to the source text unit as metadata keyed by column name instead of as translator notes.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when comment columns are defined

mergeAdjacentCodes.help: When multiple inline codes appear consecutively with no text between them, they are combined into a single placeholder code. This simplifies the translatable content by reducing the number of inline codes translators must handle. For example, `` would become a single placeholder rather than two separate codes.

moveLeadingAndTrailingCodesToSkeleton.help: Inline codes appearing at the very beginning or end of a segment are moved into the skeleton (non-translatable portion). This reduces clutter in the translatable text when boundary codes carry no translational significance. For example, if a segment starts with `` and ends with ``, those codes are moved to skeleton so translators see only the text content.

numColumns.help: The fixed number of columns expected per row. Only consulted when `detectColumnsMode` is set to fixed-number. Rows with fewer cells are padded with empty cells; rows with more cells have the extras dropped.

dependsOn.condition: only active when detectColumnsMode = 2 (fixed number)

preserveWS.help: Inherited from the plain-text base. When enabled, whitespace inside extracted values is preserved; cell-edge trimming is governed by `trimMode`, `trimLeading`, and `trimTrailing`.

recordIdColumn.help: The 1-based index of the column whose value identifies the row. Combined with `sourceIdSuffixes` to synthesise an ID for source columns lacking an explicit ID cell.

sendColumnsMode.help: Which columns of each data row are extracted: none, only the columns named in the column-role settings, or all columns as source. `listed` mode enables bilingual extraction, IDs, and notes.
notes: Bilingual extraction (source paired with target) is only available in `listed` mode with a non-empty `targetColumns`.

sendHeaderMode.help: Which header-area lines are extracted as translatable text: none, the column-names line only, or all header lines.

simplifierRules.description: Rules for simplifying inline code representation in extracted segments
simplifierRules.help:
Define rules for simplifying inline code representation in extracted text. Uses the Okapi simplifier rules syntax to merge or reduce inline codes into simpler placeholders.

This is edited via a dedicated rules editor widget.

sourceColumns.help: Comma-separated 1-based indices of the columns extracted as source (translatable) text. Consulted when `sendColumnsMode` is `listed`.
examples: `1,3` — extract columns 1 and 3 as source text

sourceIdColumns.help: Comma-separated 1-based indices of columns whose values become the resource names (IDs) of source text units, linked to their source column via `sourceIdSourceRefs`. An empty ID cell falls back to `recordIdColumn` + `sourceIdSuffixes`.

dependsOn.condition: maps each ID column to the source column it names

sourceIdSourceRefs.help: Maps each column in `sourceIdColumns` to the source column it names, as a comma-separated list of 1-based source-column indices aligned position-by-position.

sourceIdSuffixes.help: Suffixes appended to the `recordIdColumn` value to build an ID for a source column that has no explicit ID cell. Aligned position-by-position with `sourceColumns`.

targetColumns.help: Comma-separated 1-based indices of columns holding existing target text. Each is tied to a source column via `targetSourceRefs` and a locale via `targetLanguages`; supplying them makes the configuration bilingual.

dependsOn.condition: maps each target column to its source column
assigns the locale of each target column

targetLanguages.help: Locale of each target column, aligned position-by-position with `targetColumns`. A single value applies to all target columns; otherwise the pipeline target locale is used.
examples: `fr,de` — first target column is French, second is German

targetSourceRefs.help: Maps each column in `targetColumns` to the source column it translates, as a comma-separated list of 1-based source-column indices aligned position-by-position. A target whose reference cannot be resolved is sent to the skeleton.

trimLeading.help: Removes leading spaces and tabs from each extracted cell value. Forced on whenever `trimMode` is not none.

trimMode.help: How leading/trailing whitespace is removed from cells: none, non-qualified only, or all. TSV has no text qualifier, so the non-qualified option behaves like trimming all cells. Any non-none value forces `trimLeading` and `trimTrailing` on.
notes: `non-qualified only` matters only for formats that have a text qualifier (CSV); for TSV and fixed-width it behaves like trimming all cells.

trimTrailing.help: Removes trailing spaces and tabs from each extracted cell value. Forced on whenever `trimMode` is not none.

unescapeSource.help: Inherited from the plain-text base. When enabled, `\t`, `\n`, `\\`, and `\uXXXX` escape sequences in extracted values are converted to the characters they represent.

useCodeFinder.help: Inherited from the plain-text base. When enabled, the configured code-finder rules are applied to extracted cell text and matching runs become inline codes rather than translatable words. The default rules cover printf-style format specifiers and common backslash escape sequences — useful for tab-separated resource exports that embed variables.

dependsOn.condition: supplies the patterns applied when enabled

valuesStartLineNum.help: The 1-based line where data rows begin. Lines before it form the header area, handled per `sendHeaderMode`.

wrapMode.help: How a value spanning multiple physical lines is represented: `NONE` joins wrapped lines with a line feed, `SPACES` joins with a single space, `PLACEHOLDERS` inserts an inline placeholder at each internal break so the wrapping can be reproduced on output.

limitations: Consecutive tabs collapse into a single separator, so a run of tabs cannot represent an empty intermediate column.
Column roles are addressed by 1-based index, not by header name.
There is no text qualifier; a tab cannot be embedded within a field value.
processingNotes: Lines are split into cells on runs of one or more tab characters; leading whitespace before the first printable character is skipped when locating a cell.
Empty lines are rejected (produce no record).
Cells carry `line_number`, `column_number`, and `row_number` properties; header rows report `row_number` 0.

examples.description: A simple bilingual file: column 1 is source, column 2 is target, separated by a tab. No header line.
examples.config:
```yaml
configId: okf_table_tsv
sourceColumns: "1"
targetColumns: "2"
targetSourceRefs: "1"
valuesStartLineNum: 1
```
examples.input:
```
Hello world	Bonjour le monde
Goodbye	Au revoir
```

examples.title: Protect printf variables as inline codes
examples.description: Enable the code finder so printf-style placeholders and escape sequences in cell values are treated as inline codes rather than translatable words.
