iwsBlockLockStatus.title: Set locked Finished Segments as translate="no"

iwsBlockMultipleExact.title: Set Finished multiple_exact segments as translate="no"

iwsBlockTmScore.title: Set segments with matching tm_score as translate="no"

iwsIncludeMultipleExact.title: Include segments with multiple_exact regardless of tm_score

doc.overview: The XLIFF Filter processes **XLIFF 1.2** (XML Localisation Interchange File Format) documents, an OASIS standard for transporting translatable text between translation tools. It supports standard XLIFF, SDLXLIFF, and IWS XLIFF variants with dedicated writer modes for each. The filter handles `<seg-source>` segmentation, `<alt-trans>` translation matches, ITS annotations, and maps XLIFF metadata (`approved`, `resname`, `restype`, `coord`, `target-language`) to Okapi resource properties.

extraction.fallbackToID.help:
Uses the `id` attribute of `<trans-unit>` as the text unit name when `resname` is not present.

Useful for XLIFF documents that use `resname`-like values for `id` but omit the `resname` attribute.

extraction.forceUniqueIds.help: Ensures all text unit IDs are unique across the document. Useful for XLIFF files where `<trans-unit>` `id` values may be duplicated across different `<file>` elements.

extraction.ignoreInputSegmentation.help:
Discards all segmentation information (`<seg-source>` elements) from the input XLIFF. Segmented content is reduced to a single unsegmented content block.

**Warning:** Any `<alt-trans>` data attached to individual segments is also lost when this is enabled.
notes: Any `<alt-trans>` data attached to a given segment is also lost when segmentation is ignored.

extraction.includeExtensions.help: Includes non-standard XLIFF extension elements (from custom namespaces) in the extracted output. Typically needed for vendor-specific XLIFF variants like SDLXLIFF or IWS XLIFF.

extraction.includeIts.help: Includes ITS (Internationalization Tag Set) and ITSXLF annotations in the extracted data. See the [ITS Components page](ITS_Components) for details on supported annotations.

extraction.inlineCdata.help: Treats CDATA sections found within translatable content as inline codes rather than text content.

extraction.preserveSpaceByDefault.help:
Controls the default whitespace handling when `xml:space` is not specified on a `<trans-unit>`.

- When enabled, whitespace is preserved by default (as if `xml:space="preserve"` were set)
- When disabled, content without an explicit `xml:space="preserve"` attribute is unwrapped (normalized)

extraction.skipNoMrkSegSource.help: Skips `<seg-source>` elements that do not contain any `<mrk mtype='seg'>` segment markers. When a `<seg-source>` has no explicit segment markers, it is treated as if the whole content is a single segment.

extraction.subAsTextUnit.help: Processes `<sub>` elements found inside inline codes (`<bpt>`, `<ept>`, `<ph>`, `<it>`) as separate text units rather than including them in the parent inline code's data.
notes: By default, `<sub>` element content is included in the code of the parent inline element. A warning is generated when a `<sub>` element is detected.

inlineCodes.enabled.help: Enables pattern-based detection of inline codes within translatable text using regex rules. The XLIFF filter already handles standard XLIFF inline codes (`<bpt>`, `<ept>`, `<ph>`, etc.) natively — this is for detecting **additional** patterns in the text content itself.

iws.iwsBlockFinished.help: Blocks (skips) trans-units that have a "finished" IWS translation status, treating them as non-translatable.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled

iws.iwsBlockLockStatus.help: Blocks trans-units that have a locked status in IWS, treating them as non-translatable.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled

iws.iwsBlockMultipleExact.help: Blocks trans-units that have multiple exact TM matches, treating them as non-translatable.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled

iws.iwsBlockTmScore.help: Blocks trans-units with a TM score at or above the threshold value, treating them as non-translatable.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled

iws.iwsBlockTmScoreValue.help: The TM score threshold. Trans-units with scores at or above this value are blocked when `iwsBlockTmScore` is enabled.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this threshold to apply

iws.iwsIncludeMultipleExact.help: Includes trans-units that have multiple exact TM matches in the extraction.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled

iws.iwsRemoveTmOrigin.help: Removes TM origin metadata from IWS XLIFF output.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled

iws.iwsTransStatusValue.help: The IWS translation status value to set on output segments.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled

iws.iwsTransTypeValue.help: The IWS translation type value to set on output segments.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled

iws.useIwsXliffWriter.help: Enables the Idiom WorldServer-specific XLIFF writer for IWS XLIFF documents. Required for proper handling of IWS metadata and extensions.

output.addTargetLanguage.help: Adds the `target-language` attribute to `<file>` elements in the output if it is not already present.

output.allowEmptyTargets.help: Prevents copying source text into empty `<target>` elements. When enabled, empty targets remain empty in the output rather than being populated from the source.

output.alwaysAddTargets.help: Always adds `<target>` elements to `<trans-unit>` entries in the output, even if no target existed in the input.

output.alwaysUseSegSource.help: Always includes `<seg-source>` elements in the output, even when the original input did not use segmentation.

output.outputSegmentationType.help:
Controls how segmentation is represented in the output:

- **0** — Segment only if the input was segmented (preserves original)
- **1** — Always segment (adds `<seg-source>` even for unsegmented input)
- **2** — Never segment (removes all `<seg-source>` elements)
- **3** — Segment only if the entry has multiple segments, regardless of input
output.outputSegmentationType.values: `0` = preserve input segmentation, `1` = always segment, `2` = never segment, `3` = segment only multi-segment entries

output.overrideTargetLanguage.help:
Overrides the target language throughout the XLIFF document. Sets the `target-language` attribute and `xml:lang` in all `<target>` elements to the user-specified target language, regardless of original values.

Useful when using an XLIFF document as a **template for multiple target languages**.
notes: Depending on the original XLIFF content, this may result in `<alt-trans>` elements that no longer correspond to the target language of a given `<trans-unit>`.

parser.factoryClass.help: The fully-qualified Java class name of the `XMLInputFactory` implementation to use as the custom XML stream parser.
parser.factoryClass.values: A fully-qualified Java class name, e.g. `com.ctc.wstx.stax.WstxInputFactory`
examples: `com.ctc.wstx.stax.WstxInputFactory` — Woodstox parser (recommended)

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this to be used

parser.useCustomParser.help:
Uses an alternative XML stream parser instead of the default JVM parser. The default custom parser is [Woodstox](http://woodstox.codehaus.org/).

**Recommended for SDLXLIFF files** — the default Java XML parser uses recursive whitespace collapsing that can cause `StackOverflowError` on documents with large chunks of element content.
notes: The default Java parser can cause stack overflow errors on XLIFF documents with large element content (e.g., SDLXLIFF files). Using Woodstox avoids this issue.

sdl.sdlSegConfValue.help: The SDL segment confirmation status value to set on output segments.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled

sdl.sdlSegLockedValue.help: The SDL segment locked status value to set on output segments.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled

sdl.sdlSegOriginValue.help: The SDL segment origin value to set on output segments (e.g., the TM or MT source).

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled

sdl.useSdlXliffWriter.help: Enables the SDL-specific XLIFF writer that handles SDLXLIFF metadata and extensions. Required for proper round-tripping of `.sdlxliff` files.
notes: SDL properties `locked`, `conf`, and `origin` are stored in the TextContainer of the target rather than per-segment. If there are multiple segments, values come from the last segment. From M35 on, segment-level properties are also stored with correct values, but those are read-only.

sdl.useSegsForSdlProps.help: Reads SDL properties (`locked`, `conf`, `origin`) from individual segments rather than the text container level.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled

states.addAltTrans.help:
Allows adding new `<alt-trans>` elements to the output. When used with the Leveraging Step, translation matches found during leveraging are included as `<alt-trans>` entries.

The `<alt-trans>` entries include `match-quality` scores, `origin` attributes, and optionally match type information.

states.addAltTransGMode.help: Uses `<g>`/`<x>` notation for inline codes in newly added `<alt-trans>` elements instead of `<bpt>`/`<ept>`/`<ph>` notation.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this to have any effect

states.editAltTrans.help: Allows modification of existing `<alt-trans>` elements in the output. Existing entries are treated the same as newly added ones.

dependsOn.condition: typically used together with addAltTrans

states.targetStateMode.help:
Controls how the `state` attribute on `<target>` elements is handled in the output.

- **0** — Do not modify the state attribute
- Other values control whether and how state values are set on targets

states.targetStateValue.help: The value to set for the `state` attribute on `<target>` elements when `targetStateMode` is configured to set a state.

dependsOn.condition: must be set to a mode that modifies states

states.useTranslationTargetState.help: Uses translation-specific target state handling. When enabled, the state is managed based on translation workflow status.

subfilters.cdataSubfilter.help: Specifies a sub-filter to apply to CDATA content within the XLIFF. The sub-filter processes the CDATA content as a separate document format.

subfilters.pcdataSubfilter.help: Specifies a sub-filter to apply to PCDATA (parsed character data) content within the XLIFF.

xtmAttributes.description: Process XTM-specific attributes in the XLIFF document.
xtmAttributes.help: Enables processing of XTM-specific attributes in the XLIFF document.

limitations: The content of `<sub>` elements is not supported as text by default. Content inside `<bpt>`, `<ept>`, `<ph>`, and `<it>` (including `<sub>`) is included in the parent inline code. A warning is generated when `<sub>` is detected.

The `<mrk mtype='protected'>` marker is supported by converting content to an inline code. Nested `<mrk mtype='x-its-translate-yes'>` within protected markers is not supported — its content goes into the original data of the inline code.

For SDLXLIFF: SDL properties `locked`, `conf`, and `origin` are stored at the TextContainer level (not per-segment). With multiple segments, values come from the last segment. From M35, segment-level properties are also stored but are read-only.
The default Java XML parser can cause `StackOverflowError` on XLIFF documents with large element content chunks (e.g., SDLXLIFF files). Use the Woodstox custom parser to avoid this.
processingNotes: **Encoding detection**: Uses the document's encoding declaration if present; defaults to UTF-8 otherwise (ignoring any externally specified default encoding).
**BOM handling**: For UTF-8 output, a BOM is written only if the input was UTF-8 and contained a BOM. No BOM is added when converting from other encodings to UTF-8.
**Line breaks**: Output preserves the same line-break style as the input document.
**Whitespace**: `<trans-unit>` elements with `xml:space="preserve"` retain whitespace as-is. Without this attribute (or with a different value), source/target content is unwrapped.
**Segmentation validation**: If `<seg-source>` content (without segment markers) does not match `<source>` content, the segmentation is discarded.
**Alt-trans mapping**: `<alt-trans>` entries are sorted by match type and score. `match-quality` is used as score if it's an integer or percentage. `origin` defaults to `SourceDoc` if not present.

**maxbytes mapping**: The `maxbytes` attribute maps to `its-storageSize`. If `its:storageSizeEncoding` is present, that encoding is used; otherwise UTF-8 is the default. Note: `its:storageSize` (the ITS attribute) is not recognized — the size must use `maxbytes`.

examples.title: SDLXLIFF configuration
examples.description: The `okf_xliff-sdl` configuration enables SDL-specific handling with Woodstox parser to avoid stack overflow on large SDLXLIFF files.

examples.title: Template for multiple target languages
examples.description: Use `overrideTargetLanguage` to reuse a single XLIFF as a template for translating into multiple languages. The filter replaces all `target-language` and `xml:lang` values.

examples.title: IWS XLIFF configuration
examples.description: The `okf_xliff-iws` configuration enables Idiom WorldServer-specific handling, blocking finished translations and using pending status for new translations.

doc.overview: The XLIFF-2 Filter implements the `IFilter` interface for **XLIFF 2.x** (XML Localisation Interchange File Format) documents. XLIFF v2 is an OASIS Standard for transporting translatable text and localization-related information across translation and localization tool chains. The filter provides basic support for XLIFF 2.x core including extended attributes/namespaces, segments and ignorables, inline codes, notes, groups, and the XLIFF 2.x Metadata Module.

extraction.forceUniqueIds.help: Forces inline-tag IDs to be unique within each unit on both read and write. On read, it is passed to the XLIFF 2 Toolkit reader so duplicate code IDs are disambiguated; it is also passed to the inline code finder so codes it injects receive unique IDs; on write, the toolkit writer renumbers IDs to keep them unique. Enable this for documents whose authoring tool emitted repeated tag IDs within a unit, which would otherwise break tag matching.

extraction.ignoreTagTypeMatch.help: Relaxes the reader's validation that an inline tag in the `<target>` has the same type as its counterpart in the `<source>`. Passed to the XLIFF 2 Toolkit reader. When disabled (default), a source/target tag-type mismatch is reported as an error; enable this to tolerate files where targets use a differing tag type for the corresponding code.

extraction.simplifyTags.help: Declared but not consumed by the current filter implementation — the flag is defined on the parameters object and defaults to `false`, but it is not surfaced in the filter's editor and is not read by the reader, writer, or converter. It is retained for configuration compatibility and has no effect at runtime.
notes: No-op in the current Okapi xliff2 filter: no reader/writer code path reads this flag.

maxValidation.description: Perform maximum validation of the XLIFF 2.x format when parsing.
maxValidation.help: Ensures the XLIFF-2 parser performs the **maximum verification** of the format during parsing. Useful for catching malformed or non-compliant XLIFF 2.x files early in the pipeline.

needsSegmentation.description: Resegment units marked with canSegment="yes" that do not already have a target.
needsSegmentation.help: Resegments any unit that is marked with `canSegment="yes"` in the XLIFF source. Units that already have a `<target>` element **cannot** be segmented, even if `canSegment="yes"` is set.
notes: Units with an existing target cannot be segmented, regardless of this setting.

output.writeOriginalData.help: Controls whether the original-data store (the `<originalData>` content that backs inline codes via `<data>` references) is written to the output when present. Passed to the XLIFF 2 Toolkit writer as `setWithOriginalData`. Enabled by default so round-tripped files retain the verbatim original markup behind each code; disable it only when the original data should be dropped from the output.

states.discardInvalidTargets.help: Determines how the reader reacts to a `<target>` that fails validation. Passed to the XLIFF 2 Toolkit reader. When enabled, an invalid target is discarded and parsing continues; when disabled (default), the invalid target causes the file to be rejected. Enable this to salvage usable content from files containing some malformed targets.

limitations: Skeleton not supported.
Comments are lost in the merged document.
Original XML formatting is lost in the merged document.
Attributes can be reordered.
Attributes may be removed or added compared to the original depending on default values and logic in the XLIFF 2 Toolkit.
processingNotes: Supports XLIFF 2.x core features: extended attributes and namespaces, segments and ignorables, inline codes, notes, groups, and the XLIFF 2.x Metadata Module.
Merged documents will not preserve original XML formatting, comments, or attribute ordering.

examples.title: Strict validation on ingest
examples.description: Parse with maximum format verification (the default) and reject files whose targets are invalid, so malformed XLIFF 2 is caught before it enters the pipeline rather than being silently accepted.
examples.config:
```yaml
output:
  maxValidation: true
states:
  discardInvalidTargets: false
```

examples.title: Tolerant ingest of imperfect XLIFF 2
examples.description: Read files produced by tools that emit duplicate tag IDs and mismatched source/target tag types, discarding individual invalid targets instead of failing the whole file. Useful for salvaging content from non-conformant exports.
examples.config:
```yaml
extraction:
  forceUniqueIds: true
  ignoreTagTypeMatch: true
states:
  discardInvalidTargets: true
```

examples.title: Drop original data on output
examples.description: Write merged output without the `<originalData>` store that backs inline codes. Produces leaner files when downstream tools do not need the verbatim original markup behind each code.

entries.description: Extract and reinsert translatable text from generic XML, controlled by element and attribute rules.

subfilters.title: Subfilters
subfilters.description: Array of {pattern, format} mappings for embedded content. Patterns use dot-separated element paths with glob support.

translatableAttributes.title: Translatable attributes
translatableAttributes.description: Attribute names that are translatable across all elements

translatableElements.title: Translatable elements
translatableElements.description: Element names whose text content is translatable. If empty, all text content is translatable.

useCodeFinder.title: Enable inline code detection
useCodeFinder.description: Enable regex-based detection of inline codes within translatable text

doc.overview:
The **XML** format reads generic XML documents, extracts the text of
configured elements and attributes as translatable blocks, and writes the
translations back while preserving the surrounding markup. Inline elements
become inline codes within a block, so formatting tags survive translation.

Extraction is rule-driven. The simple lists — `translatableElements`,
`translatableAttributes`, `inlineElements`, `excludedElements` — cover most
cases. The advanced `elements` and `attributes` maps express richer,
ITS-style rules with attribute conditions, ID and translatable-attribute
mappings, and explicit include/exclude behaviour. When no translatable
elements are listed, all text content is treated as translatable; set
`excludeByDefault` to invert that and include only what rules opt in.

attributes.help:
A map of attribute name (or a single-quoted regex pattern) to a rule
object. A rule may carry `ruleTypes` (such as `ATTRIBUTE_TRANS`,
`ATTRIBUTE_ID`, `ATTRIBUTE_WRITABLE`) and an element scope via
`onlyTheseElements` or `allElementsExcept`.
attributes.values: Map of attribute name → rule object (default empty)
examples: `{ alt: { ruleTypes: [ATTRIBUTE_TRANS], onlyTheseElements: [img] } }`

blockTypeMap.description: Map element names to semantic block-type strings.
blockTypeMap.help:
A map from element name to a block-type string, annotating the resulting
blocks with a semantic type for downstream tools.
blockTypeMap.values: Map of element name → type string (default empty)

codeFinderRules.description: Regex patterns that match inline codes within text.
codeFinderRules.help:
An array of regular expressions; text matching any of them is treated
as an inline code rather than translatable words. Only used when
`useCodeFinder` is enabled.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for these patterns to apply

elements.help:
A map of element name (or a single-quoted regex pattern) to a rule
object. A rule may carry `ruleTypes` (`TEXTUNIT`, `INLINE`, `EXCLUDE`,
`INCLUDE`, `PRESERVE_WHITESPACE`, …), an attribute `conditions` triplet,
`idAttributes`, and a `translatableAttributes` map. Conditions let a rule
fire only when an attribute matches, mirroring ITS-style selectors.
elements.values: Map of element name → rule object (default empty)
examples: `{ p: { ruleTypes: [TEXTUNIT] } }` — extract `p` element text

excludeByDefault.description: Exclude all elements unless a rule includes them.
excludeByDefault.help:
When enabled, all elements are excluded from extraction unless an element
rule explicitly includes them with an `INCLUDE` rule type. This inverts
the default, where text content is translatable unless excluded.

excludedElements.description: Element names whose content is excluded.
excludedElements.help:
A list of element names whose content is not extracted, even when their
text would otherwise be translatable.
examples: `[script, style]` — never extract the content of these elements

groupElements.description: Element names that produce group/layer boundaries.
groupElements.help:
A list of element names that create group (layer) boundaries in the
output, grouping the blocks they contain.

idAttributes.description: Attribute names used to derive block IDs.
idAttributes.help:
A list of attribute names consulted, in order, to obtain a block's ID
from the enclosing element. When none match, the `id` attribute is used
as a fallback.
examples: `[name, key]` — use a `name` or `key` attribute as the block ID

inlineElements.description: Element names treated as inline spans.
inlineElements.help:
A list of element names treated as inline — represented as inline codes
within the surrounding text block rather than as block boundaries.
examples: `[b, i, span]` — treat these as inline formatting within text

preserveWhitespace.description: Preserve whitespace globally instead of collapsing it.
preserveWhitespace.help:
When enabled, whitespace in text content is preserved as-is. By default
runs of whitespace are collapsed to a single space and trimmed.

preserveWhitespaceElements.description: Element names that always preserve whitespace.
preserveWhitespaceElements.help:
A list of element names whose text content keeps its original whitespace
regardless of the global `preserveWhitespace` setting.
examples: `[pre, code]` — preserve whitespace inside these elements

subfilters.description: Per-pattern subfilter mappings for embedded content.
subfilters.help:
An array of `{pattern, format}` objects routing the text content of
matching element paths to another format reader. Patterns use
dot-separated element paths with glob support. Each mapping requires a
non-empty `pattern` and `format`.
examples: `[{pattern: 'root.body', format: html}]` — parse `body` content as HTML

translatableAttributes.description: Attribute names that are translatable across all elements.
translatableAttributes.help:
A list of attribute names whose values are extracted as translatable,
on any element that carries them.
examples: `[title, alt]` — extract `title` and `alt` attribute values

translatableElements.description: Element names whose text content is translatable.
translatableElements.help:
A list of element names whose text content is extracted as translatable.
When empty, all text content is treated as translatable.
translatableElements.values: Array of element names (default empty — all text translatable)
examples: `[title, para]` — extract the text of `title` and `para` elements

useCodeFinder.description: Enable regex-based inline-code detection.
useCodeFinder.help:
Enables regex-based detection of inline codes within translatable text.
The patterns are supplied via `codeFinderRules`.

limitations: This is a generic XML reader; for specific XML dialects (such as RESX) start from a tailored rule set rather than the bare defaults.
Element and attribute names in advanced rules wrapped in single quotes are treated as anchored regular expressions.
processingNotes: Inline elements become inline codes within blocks; block-level elements form the surrounding structure.
Element rules with `INCLUDE`/`EXCLUDE` combine with `excludeByDefault` to give fine control over which content is extracted.

examples.title: Extract specific elements
examples.description: Translate only title and para text, treating b and i as inline.
examples.config:
translatableElements:
  - title
  - para
inlineElements:
  - b
  - i

examples.title: Translatable attributes
examples.description: Extract title and alt attribute values across all elements.

examples.title: Conditional extraction with rules
examples.description: Include only div elements whose translate attribute is yes.
examples.config:
excludeByDefault: true
elements:
  div:
    ruleTypes:
      - INCLUDE
    conditions:
      - translate
      - EQUALS
      - "yes"

simplifierRules.description: Rules for simplifying inline code representation
simplifierRules.title: Simplifier Rules

path.title: XPath Expressions
path.description: XPath expressions defining which elements and attributes to extract for translation.

doc.overview: The XML Filter processes XML documents using a DOM-based parser with full **ITS (Internationalization Tag Set)** support (both 1.0 and 2.0). By default, all element content is translatable and no attribute values are translatable — behavior is customized via ITS rules in a parameters file (`.fprm`). If you need to process very large XML documents and have no need for ITS, consider using the **XML Stream Filter** instead.

codeFinder.help:
Defines regular expressions to capture spans of extracted text that should be treated as inline codes. Useful when element content contains variables (e.g. `#VAR#`), placeholders (e.g. `%1%`), or embedded HTML tags (e.g. `<b>`) that must be protected from modification.

The code finder uses a specific serialization format inside the `<okp:codeFinder>` element:

```xml
<okp:codeFinder useCodeFinder="yes">#v1
count.i=2
rule0=<(/?)\ w[^<]*?>
rule1=(#\w+?\#)|(%\d+?%)
</okp:codeFinder>
```

**Format rules:**
- Set `useCodeFinder="yes"` to activate (defaults to `no` if missing)
- First line of content must be `#v1`
- `count.i=N` must precede rules, where `N` is the total rule count
- Rules are numbered `rule0`, `rule1`, etc.
- Patterns must be **XML-escaped** (e.g. `<` → `<`, `>` → `>`)
- No leading spaces before `count.i` or `ruleN`, no trailing spaces after expressions
codeFinder.values: Serialized `#v1` code finder format with Java regex patterns (XML-escaped)
notes: This option must be placed in the parameters file (`.fprm`), not in embedded or linked ITS rules.
Regex patterns must be escaped for XML — `<(/?)\ w[^>]*?>` becomes `<(/?)\ w[^<]*?>` in the parameters file.
examples:
`<(/?)\ w[^<]*?>` — matches XML-type tags like ``, ``, `
`
`(#\w+?\#)|(%\d+?%)` — matches `#VAR#` or `%1%` style placeholders

escapeGT.help: Controls whether the greater-than character (`>`) is escaped as `>` in the output.
notes: This option must be placed in the parameters file (`.fprm`).

escapeNbsp.help: Controls whether the non-breaking space character is escaped as ` ` in the output.
notes: This option must be placed in the parameters file (`.fprm`).

escapeQuotes.help:
Controls whether double-quote characters (`"`) are escaped in **element content**.

Default escaping behavior:
- **Attribute values**: single-quotes never escaped, double-quotes always escaped (cannot be changed)
- **Element content**: single-quotes not escaped, double-quotes escaped by default

This option **only takes effect** if no ITS rule triggers translation of any attribute in the document. If any attribute is made translatable, this option is ignored and double-quotes are always escaped in element content.
notes: Only affects element content, not attribute values.
Ignored when any translatable attribute rule is triggered in the document.
This option must be placed in the parameters file (`.fprm`).

extractIfOnlyCodes.help:
Controls whether entries containing only white-space and/or inline codes (no actual translatable text) are extracted.

By default all extractable entries are extracted even when they contain only whitespace and/or inline codes. Set to `no` to skip such entries.
notes: This option must be placed in the parameters file (`.fprm`).

extractUntranslatable.help:
Extracts entries marked as untranslatable (`its:translate="no"`) alongside translatable content. Extracted untranslatable entries are marked as `translate="no"` in the output (e.g. XLIFF).

Useful for extracting context around translatable text.

**Tip:** To selectively exclude certain untranslatable elements from extraction, combine with the `localeFilterRule` and set `localeFilterList="!*"` on elements to skip:

```xml
<its:localeFilterRule selector="//yourTag" localeFilterList="!*"/>
```
notes: This option must be placed in the parameters file (`.fprm`).
When enabled, **all** untranslatable content is extracted by default. Use `localeFilterList="!*"` to selectively exclude specific elements.

inlineCdata.help: Controls how CDATA sections are handled. By default, CDATA sections are exposed as regular content and the CDATA markers (`<![CDATA[` and `]]>`) are discarded. When enabled, the CDATA markers are exposed as inline codes instead.
notes: This option must be placed in the parameters file (`.fprm`).

lineBreakAsCode.help:
Treats line-break characters (`
`) in element content as inline codes rather than literal line breaks in the extracted text.

Useful for XML documents generated by Excel where cell formatting uses `
` entity references — these need to be passed as inline codes rather than breaking the text into multiple lines.

Affects **all** extracted content globally.
notes: This option must be placed in the parameters file (`.fprm`), not in embedded or linked ITS rules. Options in embedded/linked ITS rules have no effect.

omitXMLDeclaration.help:
Suppresses the XML declaration (`<?xml ...?>`) from the output document.

By default an XML declaration is always written at the top of the output, regardless of whether the original had one. This is needed when the output encoding is not UTF-8, UTF-16, or UTF-32. Only use this option for rare special cases where the declaration must be omitted.
notes: XML documents without a declaration may be read incorrectly if the encoding is not UTF-8, UTF-16, or UTF-32.
This option must be placed in the parameters file (`.fprm`), not in embedded or linked ITS rules.

limitations: The ITS rule `withinTextRule` with value `nested` may act like `yes` in some cases.
The values of `xml:lang` attributes are **not** updated in output to reflect the target language.
The entire input file is loaded into memory (DOM parser) — very large documents may cause memory issues. Consider the **XML Stream Filter** for large files.
processingNotes: **Input encoding**: Uses the document's encoding declaration if present; otherwise defaults to UTF-8 (ignores any externally specified default encoding).
**Output encoding (UTF-8)**: BOM is written only if the input was UTF-8 *and* had a BOM. If converting from another encoding to UTF-8, no BOM is added.
**XML declaration**: If the original document had an encoding declaration it is updated; if not, one is automatically added (unless `omitXMLDeclaration` is set).
**Line-breaks**: Output preserves the same line-break style as the input.
**ITS rule precedence**: External parameters file rules → document global rules → document local rules (later rules override earlier ones).
**DOM-based parser**: The entire document is loaded into memory, enabling full ITS support but limiting maximum file size.

examples.title: Basic ITS rules file with filter options
examples.description: A parameters file (`.fprm`) that marks `` as non-translatable, treats ``, ``, and `` as inline elements, and enables line-break-as-code processing.
examples.config:
```xml
<its:rules version="1.0"
 xmlns:its="http://www.w3.org/2005/11/its"
 xmlns:okp="okapi-framework:xmlfilter-options">
 <its:translateRule selector="//head" translate="no"/>
 <its:withinTextRule selector="//b|//code|//img" withinText="yes"/>
 <okp:options lineBreakAsCode="yes"/>
</its:rules>
```

examples.title: Combined filter options
examples.description: Multiple filter options must be set in a single `<okp:options>` element.

examples.title: Code finder with regex patterns
examples.description: Defines regex rules to treat embedded HTML tags and placeholder variables as inline codes.
examples.config:
```xml
<its:rules version="1.0"
 xmlns:its="http://www.w3.org/2005/11/its"
 xmlns:okp="okapi-framework:xmlfilter-options">
 <okp:codeFinder useCodeFinder="yes">#v1
count.i=2
rule0=<(/?)\ w[^<]*?>
rule1=(#\w+?\#)|(%\d+?%)
 </okp:codeFinder>
</its:rules>
```

examples.title: Custom ID values with ITS extensions
examples.description: Uses the `itsx:idValue` extension to derive text unit IDs from XPath expressions on element attributes.
examples.config:
```xml
<its:rules version="1.0" xmlns:its="http://www.w3.org/2005/11/its"
 xmlns:itsx="http://www.w3.org/2008/12/its-extensions">
 <its:translateRule selector="//text" translate="yes" itsx:idValue="concat(../@name, '_t')"/>
 <its:translateRule selector="//desc" translate="yes" itsx:idValue="concat(../@name, '_d')"/>
</its:rules>
```
examples.input:
```xml
<doc>
 <msg name="id1">
  <text>Value of text</text>
  <desc>Value of desc</desc>
 </msg>
</doc>
```
examples.output:
```
Text unit "id1_t": "Value of text"
Text unit "id1_d": "Value of desc"
```

examples.description: Extracts untranslatable entries for context, while excluding specific elements using `localeFilterList`.

doc.overview: The XML Stream Filter processes XML documents using a stream parser, allowing it to handle much larger documents than the DOM-based [[XML Filter]]. It uses the same YAML-based configuration syntax as the HTML Filter, with all HTML Filter parameters available except `escapeCharacters`. If you need ITS (Internationalization Tag Set) support, use the XML Filter instead.

attributes.help:
Defines global attribute extraction rules that apply across all elements. Each key is an attribute name (e.g., `xml:lang`, `id`, `xml:space`) mapped to a rule specifying how that attribute should be handled.

The default configuration handles:
- `xml:lang` — writable localizable (language tag)
- `xml:id` and `id` — used as segment identifiers
- `xml:space` — controls whitespace preservation state

editorTitle.description: Title displayed in the parameters editor UI
editorTitle.help: Title displayed in the parameters editor UI. This is a cosmetic setting used by the Okapi GUI tools.

inlineCodes.enabled.help:
Activates pattern-based detection of inline codes (placeholders, markup tags, variables, etc.) within translatable text. The patterns are defined in the **rules** section below.

This uses the same code finder mechanism as the HTML Filter.

inlineCodes.mergeAdjacent.help: When multiple inline codes appear next to each other with no translatable text between them, merge them into a single code placeholder. This simplifies the segments presented to translators.

inlineCodes.moveBoundaryCodes.help: Moves inline codes that appear at the very start or end of a segment out into the non-translatable skeleton. This keeps segment boundaries clean for translation tools.

inlineCodes.rules.help:
Regex patterns that identify inline codes within translatable text segments. Each pattern should match a complete inline code (placeholder, variable reference, embedded markup, etc.).

These are Java regex patterns applied to the extracted text content.

inlineCodes.simplifierRules.help: Rules for simplifying the representation of inline codes in extracted segments. Uses the Okapi simplifier rules format to reduce complex inline code patterns into simpler placeholders.

parser.assumeWellformed.help:
Controls whether the parser assumes the input XML is well-formed. When enabled, the parser uses a more lenient parsing mode that can handle documents that may not be strictly well-formed XML.

The default configurations all set this to `true`.

parser.preserveWhitespace.help:
Controls the global default for whitespace preservation. Individual elements can override this via `PRESERVE_WHITESPACE` element rules (e.g., `<pre>`, `<codeblock>`) or the `xml:space` attribute.

When `false` (the default), insignificant whitespace is normalized.

path.description: Path to an external YAML configuration file
path.help: Path to the YAML configuration file containing the filter parameters. When set, the filter loads its configuration from this external file rather than using inline parameters.

taggedConfig.description: Internal parsed configuration object for element and attribute rules
taggedConfig.help: Internal tagged configuration object used by the filter's YAML-based configuration system. This stores the parsed element and attribute rules from the YAML configuration file.

limitations: No transparent support for namespace prefixes — element names must be declared with their full prefix in the configuration (e.g., `ns:element` not just `element`).
The filter is **not case-sensitive** for element names — `<elem>` and `<Elem>` are treated as identical, which violates the XML specification.
Does not support ITS (Internationalization Tag Set) — use the XML Filter instead for ITS support.
The `escapeCharacters` parameter from the HTML Filter is **not available** in this filter.
processingNotes: **Input encoding**: Uses the document's encoding declaration if present; otherwise defaults to UTF-8 (ignoring any externally specified default encoding).
**Output encoding (UTF-8)**: A BOM is written only if the input was UTF-8 and had a BOM. If the input was non-UTF-8, no BOM is added.
**XML declaration**: If the original document had an XML encoding declaration, it is updated on output. If it did not, one is automatically added.
**Line breaks**: Output preserves the same line-break style (CRLF/LF) as the input document.

**Quote mode**: Escaping of quotes and apostrophes can be configured via `quoteModeDefined: true` and `quoteMode` (0=unescaped, 1=all named entities, 2=double named + single numeric, 3=double quotes only).
**CDATA subfiltering**: The `global_cdata_subfilter` parameter applies a secondary filter (e.g., `okf_html`) to all CDATA section content.

**PCDATA subfiltering**: The `global_pcdata_subfilter` parameter applies a secondary filter to escaped markup in PCDATA. Only content matched by `TEXTUNIT` element rules is passed to the subfilter — `INCLUDE` rules are not subfiltered.
Uses the same YAML-based configuration syntax as the HTML Filter — all HTML Filter parameters apply except `escapeCharacters`.

examples.title: CDATA subfiltering with HTML
examples.description: Process CDATA sections containing HTML content using the HTML filter as a subfilter. This is useful for XML documents that embed HTML within CDATA blocks.

examples.title: PCDATA subfiltering for escaped HTML
examples.description: Process double-escaped HTML content in PCDATA. The element containing the escaped HTML must have a `TEXTUNIT` rule — `INCLUDE` rules will not trigger subfiltering.
examples.input:
```xml
<test>
  <p>This is embedded HTML content within a p tag.</p>
</test>
```

examples.title: Quote mode configuration
examples.description: Configure how quote and apostrophe characters are escaped in the output. Mode 3 escapes only double quotes.

examples.title: Java Properties XML with embedded HTML
examples.description: Built-in configuration (`okf_xmlstream-JavaPropertiesHTML`) for Java Properties XML files where `<entry>` elements contain HTML in CDATA sections.
examples.config:
```yaml
assumeWellformed: true
global_cdata_subfilter: okf_html
preserve_whitespace: false

elements:
  entry:
    ruleTypes: [TEXTUNIT]
    idAttributes: [key]
```

entries.description: Extract and reinsert translatable scalar values from YAML files, with key-path filtering and subfilters.

extractNonStrings.description: Extract non-string scalar values (booleans, numbers, nulls) as translatable blocks

keyPathPatterns.title: Key Path Patterns

useCodeFinder.title: Enable Inline Code Detection

doc.overview:
The **YAML** format reads YAML documents, extracts scalar string values as
translatable blocks, and writes the translations back while preserving the
surrounding structure, comments, and ordering. It suits Rails locale files,
application configuration, and other key/value catalogues expressed in YAML.

By default only string scalars are extracted. You can also extract
non-string scalars, restrict extraction to specific key paths with
glob-style patterns, detect inline codes, and route values containing
embedded markup (such as HTML) to a subfilter.

codeFinderRules.description: Regex patterns that match inline codes within values.
codeFinderRules.help:
An array of regular expressions; text matching any of them is treated
as an inline code rather than translatable words. Only used when
`useCodeFinder` is enabled.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for these patterns to apply

extractNonStrings.help:
When enabled, non-string scalars — booleans, numbers, and nulls — are
also extracted as translatable blocks. By default only string scalars
are extracted.

keyPathPatterns.description: Glob patterns selecting which key paths are extracted.
keyPathPatterns.help:
An array of glob-style key-path patterns. When non-empty, only keys
matching one of the patterns are extracted. `*` matches a single key
segment; `**` matches any number of segments at any depth.
keyPathPatterns.values: Array of glob patterns (default empty — all keys extracted)
examples: `en.**` — extract only keys nested under the top-level `en` key
`*.title` — extract the `title` key directly under any top-level key

subfilter.description: Subfilter format applied to scalar values.
subfilter.help:
The name of a format used as a subfilter (for example `html`) to parse
embedded markup inside extracted scalar values, so the markup is exposed
as inline codes rather than translated as literal text.
examples: `html` — parse HTML inside YAML scalar values

useCodeFinder.description: Enable regex-based inline-code detection.
useCodeFinder.help:
Enables regex-based detection of inline codes within string values. The
patterns are supplied via `codeFinderRules`.

limitations: Only scalar values are translatable; mapping keys and sequence structure form the non-translatable skeleton.
processingNotes: Document structure, key order, and comments are preserved across the read/write roundtrip.

examples.description: Translate only the keys under the en branch of a Rails-style locale file.

examples.description: Parse HTML markup within YAML scalar values.

useFullPath.title: Use full path as name
useFullPath.description: Use full nested path as the name instead of immediate key.

subFilterProcessLiteralAsBlock.title: Sub-filter Literal Blocks
subFilterProcessLiteralAsBlock.description: Process YAML literal block scalars through the configured sub-filter.

ui:groups.label: Control what content is extracted for translation
Configure how YAML keys map to text unit identification

doc.overview: The YAML Filter processes [YAML files](http://www.yaml.org/) and supports [Ruby on Rails](http://rubyonrails.org/) message variables. It extracts translatable string values from YAML key-value pairs, lists, and maps, assigning each entry a name composed of its full parent key path (e.g., `fr/activerecord/errors/messages/exclusion`). The filter handles Unicode BOM detection for encoding and preserves original line-break types in output.

extraction.excludeKeys.description: Regular expression for keys that behave opposite to the default extraction mode.
extraction.excludeKeys.help:
A regular expression matched against YAML keys. Matching keys behave **opposite** to the default extraction mode:

- If `extractAll` is **enabled**: matching keys are **excluded** from extraction.
- If `extractAll` is **disabled**: matching keys are **included** for extraction.

When `useFullPath` is enabled, the regex is matched against the full key path (e.g., `menu/value/popup/menuitem/value`).
extraction.excludeKeys.values: Java regular expression pattern matched against YAML key names (or full key paths if `useFullPath` is enabled).
examples: `^(date|time|number)$` — exclude date, time, number keys
`^en/.*` — match all keys under the `en` locale when using full paths

dependsOn.condition: inverts meaning: when extractAll is true, matching keys are excluded; when false, matching keys are included
when enabled, regex matches against full key path instead of immediate key

extraction.extractAll.help:
Controls the default extraction behavior for key/string pairs.

- **When enabled**: all key/string pairs are extracted, *except* those matching the `excludeKeys` pattern.
- **When disabled**: no key/string pairs are extracted, *except* those matching the `excludeKeys` pattern (inverted logic).

This works as a toggle that flips the meaning of the `excludeKeys` regex — matching keys become exceptions to whichever default you choose.

extraction.includeIsolatedStrings.description: Extract string values that are not directly associated with a key.
extraction.includeIsolatedStrings.help:
Extracts strings that are **not associated with a key**, such as bare string values in YAML arrays or lists.

For example, in:
```yaml
list: [one, two, three]
```
the values `one`, `two`, `three` are isolated strings — they have no direct key association.

inlineCodes.enabled.help:
Activates pattern-based detection of inline codes within extracted text. Matched patterns are converted to inline code placeholders (like `<ph>` in XLIFF), protecting them from translation.

The default patterns detect:
- **printf-style format specifiers**: `%d`, `%s`, `%2$f`, etc.
- **Escape sequences**: `\n`, `\r`, `\t`, `\a`, `\b`, `\f`, `\v`, `\r\n`
- **Numbered placeholders**: `{0}`, `{1}`, etc.
notes: **Note:** This option cannot be used together with the sub-filtering option. Use one or the other.

inlineCodes.mergeAdjacent.help: When multiple inline codes appear next to each other with no translatable text between them, merges them into a single placeholder. This simplifies the segment for translators.

inlineCodes.moveBoundaryCodes.help: Moves inline codes that appear at the very beginning or end of a segment out of the translatable content and into the skeleton. This produces cleaner segments for translation.

inlineCodes.rules.help:
Regular expression patterns that identify inline codes within extracted text. Each match is converted to a placeholder code.

The default expression covers:
```
((%(([-0+#]?)[-0+#]?)(([0-9]\$)?)(([-0-9*]*)(\.[-0-9*]*)?)[dioxXucsfeEgGpn])
|((\\r\\n)|\\a|\\b|\\f|\\n|\\r|\\t|\\v)
|(\{[0-9].*?\}))
```
examples: `%\{[^}]+\}` — Ruby i18n interpolation: matches `%{name}`, `%{count}`

`<[^>]+>` — HTML tags: matches ``, ``, `
`
`\{\d.*?\}` — Numbered placeholders: matches `{0}`, `{1 items}`

inlineCodes.simplifierRules.help: Rules for simplifying the representation of inline codes, reducing complexity for translators by combining or collapsing code patterns.

keys.useAsName.help: Uses the YAML key as the `resname` (resource name) of each extracted text unit. This maps to the `name` attribute in XLIFF output, making entries identifiable by their YAML key rather than an auto-generated ID.

keys.useFullPath.help:
Uses the complete nested key path as the resource name instead of just the immediate key.

For example, for a value nested under `menu > value > popup > menuitem > value`, the name becomes `menu/value/popup/menuitem/value` instead of just `value`.

Also affects how `excludeKeys` regex matching works — when enabled, the regex is matched against the full path.
examples: Full path: `fr/activerecord/errors/messages/exclusion`
Immediate key only: `exclusion`

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for useFullPath to take effect

output.escapeNonAscii.help: Escapes non-ASCII characters in the output using Unicode escape sequences. Useful when the target system requires ASCII-safe YAML files.

output.subFilterProcessLiteralAsBlock.description: Process YAML literal block scalars through the configured sub-filter.
output.subFilterProcessLiteralAsBlock.help:
When sub-filtering is active, controls whether YAML literal block scalars (using `|` syntax) are processed through the sub-filter.

This is useful when literal blocks contain structured content (e.g., HTML) that should be parsed by the sub-filter.

output.wrap.help: Controls whether long lines are wrapped in the output YAML file.
notes: **Warning:** Wrapped lines in the original source document will be **unwrapped** in the target document regardless of this setting — all text will appear on a single line in the output.

limitations: Wrapped lines in the original source document will be **unwrapped** in the target document — all text will appear on a single line.

If an illegal character is introduced during translation, the user must manually add double or single quotes to make the target YAML file valid. A WARNING is issued by the filter when this scenario is detected.
Inline code detection (`inlineCodes.enabled`) cannot be used together with sub-filtering — they are mutually exclusive.
processingNotes: **Encoding detection**: If a Unicode BOM is present, the corresponding encoding (UTF-8, UTF-16, etc.) is used. Otherwise, the default encoding specified when opening the document is applied.
**Line breaks**: The output preserves the same line-break type (CRLF, LF) as the original input.
**Entry naming**: Each extracted entry is named using the full path of its parent keys joined by `/` (e.g., `fr/activerecord/errors/messages/exclusion`).

examples.title: Ruby on Rails i18n YAML
examples.description: A typical Rails locale file. All highlighted string values are extracted with full key paths as names.
examples.input:
```yaml
fr:
  activerecord:
    errors:
      template:
        header:
          list: [one, two, three]
          map: {key: value, key2: value2}
          one: "Impossible d'enregistrer {{model}}: 1 erreur"
          other: "Impossible d'enregistrer {{model}}: {{count}} erreurs."
        body: "Veuillez vérifier les champs suivants :"
      messages:
        inclusion: "n'est pas inclus(e) dans la liste"
        exclusion: "n'est pas disponible"
```

examples.title: Inline codes for Ruby interpolation
examples.description: Configure inline code detection to protect Ruby i18n `%{variable}` placeholders from translation.
examples.config:
```yaml
inlineCodes:
  enabled: true
  rules:
    rules:
      - pattern: "%\\{[^}]+\\}"
    sample: "Hello %{name}, you have %{count} messages"
```
